title: Password Incorrect / Reset Password / Forgot Password
summary: If you see the error message "Password is incorrect, try again" while logging into applications like ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS or any similar application, it typically means that the password entered is incorrect. Solution → Double-check the ...
content:
If you see the error message "Password is incorrect, try again" while logging into applications like ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS or any similar application, it typically means that the password entered is incorrect.

      

Solution →
Double-check the password you entered. If it was entered incorrectly, simply re-enter the correct password and try logging in again.

If you continue to see the error message "Password is incorrect, try again," you will need to reset your password.

To Reset Your Password, Please follow these steps:

Step 1: Click on the "Forgot Password?" option.

      

Step 2: Enter your Registered mobile number. You will receive an OTP on this number for verification.

      

Step 3: Enter the OTP.

      
      
Step 4: Create a new password, confirm it and click on Update Password.

      

You can now log in with your updated password.

title: Sign-Up and Sign-In Process – SPECTRUM CLOUD
tags: sign-up and sign-in process – spectrum cloud
summary: This article provides a step-by-step guide to help you create an account and log in to the SPECTRUM CLOUD platform by KDK Software. SPECTRUM CLOUD enables tax professionals to manage GST, Income Tax (ITR), and TDS compliance for all their clients ...
content:
This article provides a step-by-step guide to help you create an account and log in to the SPECTRUM CLOUD platform by KDK Software.

SPECTRUM CLOUD enables tax professionals to manage GST, Income Tax (ITR), and TDS compliance for all their clients from a single, secure cloud-based platform.

Note: If you are signing up as a business entity (e.g., SME, Corporate, Enterprise), please refer to the Business Users section below for the appropriate platforms.

🎯 Intended Users : The SPECTRUM CLOUD portal is designed for:

Chartered Accountants (CAs)
Accountants
Tax Practitioners
GST Practitioners (GSTPs)

📝 Step 1: Account Registration (Sign-Up)

To access the platform, you must first register an account.

Navigate to the Sign-Up page: https://app.kdksoftware.com/signup

Complete the registration form with the following:
Full Name
Email Address
Mobile Number
Other required details

Click "SIGN UP" to submit the form.

You will receive a One-Time Password (OTP) on your registered mobile number.

Enter the OTP in the designated field to verify your account.

🔐 Step 2: User Login (Sign-In)

Once your account is verified, follow the steps below to log in.

Go to the Login page: https://app.kdksoftware.com/login

Enter your registered mobile number and password.
Click "Login" to access your dashboard.

🏢 For Business Users (SMEs, Corporates, Enterprises)

If you are representing a Small or Medium Enterprise (SME), Corporate, or Enterprise, please use the appropriate platform designed specifically for business entities. These platforms provide tailored solutions to meet business compliance and filing requirements:

🔗 Recommended Platforms for Business Users:

ExpressGST – For GST filing and compliance 👉 https://www.expressgst.com/

ExpressITR – For Income Tax Return (ITR) filing 👉 https://www.expressitr.com/

ExpressTDS – For TDS return filing and related compliance 👉 https://www.expresstds.com/

Note: These platforms are optimized for organizations and business entities rather than individual professionals such as CAs or tax consultants.

⚠️ Important Guidelines

Password Confidentiality: Use a secure password and do not share your login credentials.
Consistent Credentials Across Products: If you use multiple KDK Software products with the same mobile number, please ensure you use the same password across platforms to maintain account consistency.

📞 Need Assistance?

If you experience any issues during registration or login, please contact our customer support team.

title: ExpressGST || Invoice Management System (IMS) Dashboard
summary: The Invoice Management System (IMS) is an integrated feature within the GST framework that enables recipients to manage invoices filed by suppliers through GSTR-1, GSTR-1A, or IFF. Recipients can take necessary actions—Accept, Reject, or Mark as ...
content:
The Invoice Management System (IMS) is an integrated feature within the GST framework that enables recipients to manage invoices filed by suppliers through GSTR-1, GSTR-1A, or IFF. Recipients can take necessary actions—Accept, Reject, or Mark as Pending—to ensure accurate and timely availment of Input Tax Credit (ITC).
Additionally, users can import their book data to reconcile it against GST data, facilitating informed decisions directly within the IMS Dashboard.

      
IMS Dashboard – Process Flow

1. Sync Data

Action: Click the Sync button to update all invoice data up to the current date.

      

2. Import Book Data for Reconciliation

Action: Click the Import Book Data icon.

      
Outcome: The system will display both reconciled and unreconciled invoices, enabling users to verify invoice status.

      
      
Next Step: Once synchronization is complete, select No Action to continue.

      

3. Filter and Sort Invoices by Status

Action: Use the Sort by Status option on the right-hand side of the data table.

Available Statuses:
Matched

Partially Matched

Books Only

IMS Only

            
Purpose: This functionality helps users quickly identify discrepancies and take appropriate action with greater efficiency.

4. Manage Invoices

Action: Select invoices individually, in bulk, or party-wise.
Available Actions:

Accept – for verified invoices.

Reject – for incorrect or invalid invoices.

Mark as Pending – when further verification is required.

            

5. Return to Dashboard

Action: Click the Back to IMS Dashboard button to return to the main dashboard.

      

6. Compute GSTR-2B

Action: Click the Compute 2B icon.

      
Follow-up: Click Compute 2B again to push the reconciled data into your GSTR-2B records for accurate return filing.

      
      

We hope this addresses your concern clearly.

title: ExpressGST || Reconciliation Process: Books vs 2A/2B
summary: Reconciliation Process: Books vs 2A/2B Step 1: Import GSTR-2 Data Start by importing the GSTR-2 data into the software. You can do this by either uploading an Excel file or directly importing data from Tally (if your data is stored there). Step 2: ...
content:
Reconciliation Process: Books vs 2A/2B

Step 1: Import GSTR-2 Data

Start by importing the GSTR-2 data into the software.

You can do this by either uploading an Excel file or directly importing data from Tally (if your data is stored there).

Step 2: Navigate to the "Books vs 2A/2B Annual" Section

After importing the data, go to the Books vs 2A/2B Annual section in the software.

      
This is where the reconciliation process takes place.

Step 3: Select the "Invoice Wise Reco" Option

For detailed reconciliation, click on Invoice Wise Reco.

This option allows reconciliation at the invoice level, ensuring each invoice is compared between your books and the GSTR-2A/2B data.
Step 4: Review Reconciliation Status

The software will categorize invoices based on their reconciliation status:

Matched: Invoices that match between your books and the GSTR-2A/2B data.

Partially Matched in Books Only: Invoices where some data (e.g., taxable value, GSTIN) matches, but discrepancies exist in your books.

Partially Matched in 2A/2B Only: Invoices where some data matches in GSTR-2A/2B but discrepancies exist in your books.

In Books Only: Invoices available in your records but not in GSTR-2A/2B.

In Portal Only: Invoices available on the portal (GSTR-2A/2B) but not in your books.

      

By following these steps, you can ensure accurate reconciliation of GSTR-2 data with your books. This process helps you identify discrepancies and take corrective actions effectively.

title: ExpressGST || Process to Check E-Invoice Details
summary: Process to Check E-Invoice Details in Express GST The following steps outline how to check E-invoice details within Express GST to ensure accurate filing and reconciliation: Step 1: Navigate to the Return Summary Page Log in to Express GST and access ...
content:
Process to Check E-Invoice Details in Express GST

The following steps outline how to check E-invoice details within Express GST to ensure accurate filing and reconciliation:

Step 1: Navigate to the Return Summary Page

Log in to Express GST and access the Return Summary page.

You will see various GSTR filings, such as: GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, GSTR-9 or GSTR-9C

Step 2: Open GSTR-1 Summary Details

Under the GSTR-1 section, click on the Prepare option to open the GSTR-1 Summary Details page.

      
On the summary page, click on the Data as per Portal option to retrieve portal data.

      

Step 3: View Data

Click on the View Data option to access the detailed GSTR-1 data.

      
If E-invoices are available on the portal, they will be displayed in the data grid.

The presence of an E-Sign icon in the Source column indicates that the data originates from E-invoices.

      

Step 4: Search for E-Invoice Details

Use the Source Search option to filter and locate specific E-invoice details within the data.

      
This feature allows you to efficiently search and review E-invoice records for reconciliation or verification.

By following these steps, you can easily check and verify E-invoice details in Express GST, ensuring accurate and compliant GSTR filings.

title: ExpressGST || GSTR-6 Reconciliation & ITC Distribution
summary: An Input Service Distributor (ISD) is a registered taxpayer that receives invoices for services utilized by its branches. The ISD distributes the tax paid—referred to as Input Tax Credit (ITC)—to the respective branches proportionally by issuing ISD ...
content:
An Input Service Distributor (ISD) is a registered taxpayer that receives invoices for services utilized by its branches. The ISD distributes the tax paid—referred to as Input Tax Credit (ITC)—to the respective branches proportionally by issuing ISD invoices.

🔹 Although branches may have different GSTINs, they must operate under the same PAN as the ISD.

Books vs GSTR-6A Reconciliation
Select PAN - Go to the Client Master and select the applicable PAN.

Access Reconciliation Summary - Navigate to the Reconciliation Summary section and click on the Reco option.

Import Books Data - 
Click Import Books Data.

Choose the relevant month and upload the corresponding file.

Click Import to proceed.

Compare Data - Once imported, the Books Data can be directly compared with the GSTR-6A details.

Supplier-wise Reconciliation View - For a summarised, vendor-level comparison, view the Supplier-wise Reconciliation report.

Configuration Settings

Users can configure how receiver GSTIN information is displayed using the “Show Receiver GSTIN By” setting. Available options include:

GSTIN

Alias Name

Default

State Column

Legal/Trade Name

ITC Distribution Summary

Select Month - Choose the desired month to view the ITC distribution ratio, based on:
Previous Year (PY) turnover

Previous Quarter turnover

Invoice-Based Distribution - To override the standard ratio and base distribution on actual invoices, click "Do Distribution Based on Invoices".

ITC Distribution Process

Select Invoices -

Choose the invoice(s) for distribution.

Select the respective GSTIN or Branch Name.

Distribute ITC

Click Distribute to allocate ITC to the selected branch.

Adjust Input Amounts

Modify input amounts as per the type of service being distributed.

Split Input Amounts (Optional)

Use the Split option to divide the ITC among multiple branches.

Enter the desired values manually.

Multi-Branch Distribution 

A single invoice can be distributed across multiple branches:

Select the invoice

Choose multiple branches

Click Distribute

Additional Features

Unlink Distributed Invoices - Use the Unlink option to remove an existing ITC distribution.

Supplier-wise ITC Distribution Summary - View a summary of ITC distribution grouped by supplier for better tracking.

Excel Export - Export the final distributed invoices in Excel format for:
GST return filing

Compliance reporting

Internal audits and reviews

summary: GSTR-1 → How to Transfer Canceled, Suspended, or Su-Moto Canceled GSTN Bills to B2CS Follow these steps to transfer canceled, suspended, or Su-moto canceled GSTN bills to the B2CS section in ExpressGST: Steps to Transfer GSTN Bills to B2CS: Step 1: ...
content:
GSTR-1 → How to Transfer Canceled, Suspended, or Su-Moto Canceled GSTN Bills to B2CS

Follow these steps to transfer canceled, suspended, or Su-moto canceled GSTN bills to the B2CS section in ExpressGST:

Steps to Transfer GSTN Bills to B2CS:

Step 1: Import GSTR-1 Data

Import the GSTR-1 data into ExpressGST as usual.

Step 2: Save Data on the Portal

After importing, click on the "Save Data on Portal" option to ensure all details are saved.

      

Step 3: Upload to GSTN

Click on the "Upload on GSTN" button to push the data to the GSTN portal.

Step 4: Wait for Status Update

After uploading, the initial status might show as "Errored".

      

Step 5: Fix GSTN-Related Issues

If errors occur, click on "Click Here to Fix GSTN-Related Issues" to resolve discrepancies.

A table will display the GSTN bill's statuses, including canceled, suspended, or Su-moto canceled bills.

      
Step 6: Move Bills to B2CS

In the table, select the bills you want to transfer:

Select all bills, or

Choose individual bills.

Click on the "Move to B2CS" option to transfer the selected bills.

      

Step 7: Confirm the Action

After transferring the selected bills, click "Done" to confirm the action.

Step 8: Re-Upload to GSTN

Click the "Upload on GSTN" button again to upload the corrected data to the GSTN portal.

      

Step 9: Enter Captcha or OTP
During the final upload, enter the required Captcha or OTP to complete the process.

      

Step 10: Final Status Update

Once the data is successfully uploaded, the status will update to "Successful".

      
You can now proceed with filing your GSTR-1 return.

By following these steps, you can efficiently transfer canceled, suspended, or Su-moto canceled GSTN bills to the B2CS section in ExpressGST. This ensures the corrected data is uploaded successfully to GSTN, allowing you to file your return without errors.

Sign-Up and Sign-In Assistance

Follow these steps to create an account and log in successfully:

Step 1: Access the Official Website
Visit our official platform and select the "TRY Now" option to access the sign-up page.

Depending on your requirement, use the respective application links:

GST Application: https://www.expressgst.com/

                        
Income Tax Return Filing Application: https://www.expressitr.com/

                        
TDS Return Filing Application: https://www.expresstds.com/

                        

Step 2: Register Your Account

Fill in the required details such as name, email address, mobile number, and any other requested information.

Click "SIGN UP" to proceed.

You will receive a One-Time Password (OTP) on your registered mobile number or email address.

Enter the OTP in the verification field to complete your registration.

Step 3: Log In to Your Account

Return to the website or application.

Select the "Sign In" option.

Enter your registered mobile number and password.

Click "Log In" to access your account.

Important Notes:

Password Security: Ensure your password is strong and remains confidential to protect your account.

Account Consistency: If you are using multiple products from the KDK Software and the same mobile number is registered, please use the same password across all platforms for a seamless experience.

We hope this guide helps you!

title: Experience Unified Power with SPECTRUM CLOUD Enterprise
tags: experience unified power with spectrum cloud enterprise
summary: To enhance operational efficiency, simplify user management, and deliver a more integrated cloud experience, KDK Software has successfully consolidated its individual cloud applications into a single unified platform — Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise. With ...
content:
To enhance operational efficiency, simplify user management, and deliver a more integrated cloud experience, KDK Software has successfully consolidated its individual cloud applications into a single unified platform — Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise.

With this transformation, applications such as ExpressGST, ExpressTDS, and ExpressITR are now centrally accessible through the Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise ecosystem, enabling organizations to manage all compliance activities from one unified platform.

This upgrade is designed to provide improved accessibility, centralized subscription management, enhanced security, and a seamless user experience across all KDK cloud services.

      

Key Platform Enhancements & Internal Updates

1. Unified Subscription Management

Previously, subscriptions for ExpressGST, ExpressTDS, and ExpressITR were managed independently. As part of the new architecture enhancement, all existing standalone subscriptions have now been automatically consolidated into a single Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise subscription.

Benefits:

Centralized subscription management

Simplified license administration

Unified renewal and access control

Improved operational efficiency for corporate users

This consolidation eliminates the need to manage multiple product subscriptions separately and ensures a streamlined experience across all integrated modules.

2. Centralized Dashboard with Feature Continuity

All existing configurations, operational limits, and user-level permissions from the individual applications have been securely migrated and preserved within the new unified dashboard.

The migration process ensures complete continuity of existing services without impacting current workflows.

Preserved Components Include:

Existing usage limits

Return filing quotas

User access rights

Role-based permissions

Client data and configurations

Operational workflows and settings

Users can continue their activities seamlessly within the new platform environment without requiring any manual reconfiguration.

3. Unified Authentication & Access Management

To simplify user access and improve security management, a centralized authentication framework has been implemented across the Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise platform.

A single Sign-In and Sign-Up interface is now available for all integrated cloud applications, enabling users to access the entire ecosystem through one unified authentication process.

Key Advantages:

Single-point login access

Simplified user onboarding

Improved credential management

Enhanced security and authentication control

Consistent user experience across applications

This unified approach minimizes login complexity and improves overall accessibility for corporate users and administrators.

4. Advanced Customer Profiling & Personalization

As part of the enhanced onboarding process, the platform now captures structured customer profiling information during user registration and sign-up activities.

This information enables the system to deliver a more personalized and optimized platform experience based on the customer’s business profile, operational requirements, and service usage patterns.

Objectives of Customer Profiling:

Personalized dashboard experience

Improved service recommendations

Better customer engagement

Optimized support and communication

Enhanced product customization capabilities

The profiling framework helps ensure that users receive more relevant features, workflows, and support experiences aligned with their business needs.

Conclusion

The transition to Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise marks a significant advancement in KDK Software’s cloud ecosystem strategy. By unifying multiple applications into a centralized enterprise platform, organizations can now benefit from:

Simplified subscription and access management

Centralized operational control

Seamless user experience

Improved scalability and security

Enhanced personalization and service delivery

This transformation ensures a more efficient, secure, and future-ready compliance management ecosystem for all corporate users.

content:
Step-by-Step Guide for Editing Book Data in IMPG/SEZ, B2B, and ISD Sections

Step 1: Import GSTR-2 Data

Begin by importing your GSTR-2 data into the software. You
can choose any of the following methods:
Upload
     an Excel File: Containing your purchase data.

Import
     from Tally: If your books are maintained in Tally.

Ensure the file structure
adheres to the system format for a seamless import process.

 Step 2: Access the “Books vs 2A/2B Annual” Section
After importing the data:
Navigate
     to the “Books vs 2A/2B Annual” section.

      
This
     section displays a comparison between Books Data and 2A/2B Data.

Step 3: Edit Book-Wise Entries

Within this section, users can edit Books data irrespective
of the current status:

Matched
Partially
     Matched
Books
     Only

Editable Fields Include:
Document
     Type
Document Number
Document Date
Taxable
     Value
Tax
     Rate
Reverse
     Charge (RCM)
Eligibility
Tax
     Amounts

      

Step 4: How to Edit Fields

To update or correct any detail:

Click
     on the field you wish to modify (e.g., Document Type, Invoice Number, Tax
     Amount).

The
     selected field will become editable for direct input.

      
After
     making the necessary changes, click Done to save.

 

Step 5: Automatic Linking with 2A/2B Data

Once the edits are completed:
If the
     updated entry matches a record in 2A/2B, the system will automatically
     link it.
Important: Ensure key
fields such as GSTIN, Invoice Number, Invoice Date, and Tax Amount are
accurate for successful auto-matching.

 

Impact on Reconciliation Status

Editing Books data can change the reconciliation status:

Matched
     → Unmatched / Partially Matched if key details are altered.

Unmatched
     → Matched if the modifications align with 2A/2B records.

 

Impact on Manual or Multi-Linking

If a
     record was manually linked, any change in Books data may break the link.
The
     system will prompt for re-validation if re-linking is required.

 

Document Type Editing Restrictions

Document
     Type edits are allowed only within the same section.
For
     example:

Invoice
      can be changed to Debit Note (DN) or Credit Note (CN).

It cannot
      be changed to IMPG or IMPG-SEZ, and vice versa.

Note: The objective is to correct entries, not to
reallocate them across sections.

title: ExpressGST || Reconciliation of ISD Invoices in GSTR-2A/2B
summary: ISD (Input Service Distributor) reconciliation is the process of matching and verifying Input Tax Credit (ITC) distributed by the ISD with the credits received and recorded by respective recipient units (branches/locations). This ensures accurate ...
content:
ISD (Input Service Distributor) reconciliation is the process of matching and
verifying Input Tax Credit (ITC) distributed by the ISD with the credits
received and recorded by respective recipient units (branches/locations). This
ensures accurate reporting and compliance under GST.

Step 1: Import GSTR-2 Data

Import the GSTR-2 data into the software using one of the
following methods:

Upload
     an Excel file containing the purchase data, or
Import
     directly from Tally, if your accounting data is maintained in Tally.
Note: Ensure that the file structure matches the
prescribed format for successful import.

Step 2: Access the “Books vs 2A/2B Annual” Section

After importing the data, navigate to the Books vs 2A/2B
Annual section in the software.

      

Step 3: Select “Invoice Wise Reco”
Choose the Invoice Wise Reco option for detailed
reconciliation.

The
     ISD reconciliation table is positioned below the B2B and IMPG
     reconciliation tables.

      
This
     feature enables invoice-level comparison between your books and ISD data,
     ensuring accurate matching.

Step 4: Review Reconciliation Status
The system will categorize invoices into the following
statuses:
Matched:
     Invoice details match completely between your books and ISD data.
Partially
     Matched – Books Only: Certain details (e.g., taxable value, GSTIN)
     match, but discrepancies exist in your books.
Partially
     Matched – ISD Only: Some details match in ISD data, but discrepancies
     exist in your books.
In
     Books Only: Invoices are present in your books but missing in ISD
     data.
In
     Portal Only: Invoices are available on the ISD portal but not in your
     books.

Benefits of ISD Reconciliation
Ensures
     accuracy in ITC reporting.
Identifies
     mismatches and discrepancies promptly.
Facilitates
     timely corrective actions to maintain compliance.

summary: In some cases, reconciliation of records between books and portal data (as per GSTR 2A and GSTR 2B) is required. This may occur due to mismatches caused by discrepancies in data or unmet reconciliation conditions. In such instances, the software ...
content:
In some cases, reconciliation of records between books and portal data (as per GSTR 2A and GSTR 2B) is required. This may occur due to mismatches caused by discrepancies in data or unmet reconciliation conditions. In such instances, the software allows for manual linking of records to reconcile and mark them as matched for better clarity and recordkeeping.

Below are the steps to manually link unmatched records in the software for the GSTR 2A/2B reconciliation process:

Steps to Manually Link Unmatched Records

Step 1: Access the Reconciliation Module

Log in to the software.

Navigate to the GSTR 2A/2B reconciliation section.

      

Step 2: Identify Unmatched Records

Review the list of records that have not been automatically matched.

Carefully evaluate potential matches from the available data.

      

Step 3: Select the Record to Link

Click on the unmatched record in the "Books Data" section.

Select the Chain Icon to initiate the linking process.

      

Step 4: Link the Record

For example, if a record was not automatically matched due to minor differences in the invoice number or taxable value:

Click the Chain Icon on the screen.

A prompt will display a list of available records for linking.

Identify the most accurate match for the unmatched record.

Click the Link Icon for the selected match.

(Optional) Add a remark in the "Remarks" field for future reference.

Click the Link Now button to complete the process.

      

Step 5: Confirm Linking

After clicking Link Now, a confirmation popup will appear, notifying you about the ITC claimed status in GSTR 3B.

Click Yes to proceed and finalize the linking process.

      

Step 6: Completion Message

Once the process is complete, the software will display a message confirming the successful linking of the record.

The linked record will now appear aligned with the corresponding "Books Record".

      
      
Step 7: Unlink a Record (If Needed)

To unlink a record, click the Scissor Icon for the respective record.

      
This action will revert the invoice to its "Unmatched" status.

      

This manual linking process ensures accuracy in reconciling unmatched records with GSTR 2A/2B data. It is a useful method to address discrepancies and maintain compliance effectively.

title: GST Software > GSTR-9C > Error Occurred, please close the browser, close the Utility and try again.
summary: Solution: All of us know that we need to first download GSTR 9C utility from GST portal’s Download section and Import our JSON file in this through " Open GSTR-9C JSON File Downloaded from GST Portal" option, still after this our job is not over. ...
content:
Solution: 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
 
   All of us know that we need to first download GSTR 9C utility from GST portal’s Download section and Import our JSON file in this through " 
  Open GSTR-9C JSON File Downloaded from GST Portal" option, still after this our job is not over. 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
   
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
 
   Real trouble will start when we try to create Json file and thereafter from "To generate a JSON(.json) file to upload GSTR-9C details added in offline tool on GST Portal" option. 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
   
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
 
   Following are the important steps before generating Json file and for filing GSTR 9C. 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
   
   
     Make sure that all the 15 sheets are validated individually on the GSTR 9C Excel Utility. 
    
 
   
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
   
    
    
      Make sure that “wsweb” file is in the same folder in PC in which GSTR 9C utility is saved. 
     
 
    
 
  
 
   
   
 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
 
   If you get any kind of error then you have to compulsorily follow these steps: 
  
 
 
 
  
   
   
 
  
 
   
    
     
     
       Step 1 : 
      
 
     
 
   
 
   
     Please close all the application on your system. 
    
 
   
 
  
 
 
 
  
   
    
    
      Step 2 :  
    
 
  
 
   
   Open your Internet Explorer 
  
 
 
 
  
   
    
     
     
       Step 3 : 
     
 
   
 
   
     Go to Tool–>Internet Option–>Security–>Custom level, 
    
 
   
 
 
 
  
   
    
     
     
       Step 4 : 
     
 
   
 
   
     You will find two options – 
    
 
   
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
 
               i) “Automatic prompting for ActiveX control”. Please click on Enable 
 
 
 
               ii) “Download unsigned ActiveX Control”. Click on Prompt then click OK 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
   
    
    
      Step 5 : 
    
 
  
 
  
    Now download the Notepad++ from google and follow the following process. 
   
 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
 
               i) Install the Notepad++, 
 
 
 
               ii) Go to the utility folder, right click on “wsweb.html”—> click on “Edit with Notepad++” 
 
 
 
               iii) Click after “<html>” tag, give a space after “<html>” tag and then press the backspace. 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
   
    
    
      Step 6 : 
    
 
  
 
  
    Save the file using “Ctrl+S” key, close the file and stop the Emsigner Service, if started. 
   
 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
   
    
    
      Step 7 : 
    
 
  
 
  
    Now start Emsigner again 
   
 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
   
    
    
      Step 8 : 
    
 
  
 
  
    Open the utility and try to generate the JSON file again 
   
 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
 
   Internet explorer pop-up will come, inside that you’ll get one more pop-up asking for “Allow Blocked content”, click on it then click on “Initiate signing”, then “Open Emsigner”. 
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
 
 
  
   
    
    
      Even after all these things it doesn’t works then delete emsigner from your PC and download the latest Emsigner and follow all the above steps again! 
    
 
  
 
   
   
 
  
 
 
 
 
   This will helps.

title: ExpressGST || Reconciliation of ISD Invoices in 2A/2B & PAN Level Reconciliation (DIY)
summary: Reconciliation of ISD Invoices in 2A/2B & PAN Level Reconciliation 1. Overview ISD reconciliation refers to the process of matching and verifying Input Tax Credit (ITC) distributed by the Input Service Distributor (ISD) with the credits received and ...
content:
Reconciliation of ISD Invoices in 2A/2B & PAN Level
Reconciliation

1. Overview

ISD reconciliation refers to the process of matching and
verifying Input Tax Credit (ITC) distributed by the Input Service Distributor
(ISD) with the credits received and accounted for by the respective recipient
units (branches/locations). This ensures accurate ITC utilization and
compliance.
 

2. ISD Reconciliation Process

Step 1: Import GSTR-2 Data

Begin by importing the GSTR-2 data into the software
using one of the following methods:
Upload
     an Excel file containing your purchase data.
Import
     directly from Tally, if your accounting data is maintained in Tally.
Ensure the file structure matches the required format
for successful import.

Step 2: Navigate to the “Books vs 2A/2B Annual” Section
After importing the data, go to the Books vs 2A/2B Annual
section within the software.

      

Step 3: Select the “Invoice Wise Reco” Option
For detailed reconciliation:
Click
     Invoice Wise Reco.
The
     ISD reconciliation table is located below the B2B and IMPG reconciliation
     tables.

      
This
     option enables invoice-level comparison between your books and ISD data.

Step 4: Review Reconciliation Status
The system will categorize invoices as follows:
Matched:
     Invoices that match between your books and ISD data.
Partially
     Matched – Books Only: Some details (e.g., taxable value, GSTIN) match, but
     discrepancies exist in your books.
Partially
     Matched – ISD Only: Some details match in ISD data, but discrepancies
     exist in your books.
In
     Books Only: Invoices present in your books but not in ISD data.
In
     Portal Only: Invoices available on the ISD portal but not in your books.
 

3. PAN View & Client Master Dashboard

Accessing PAN View
Navigate to the PAN View section in the application to
monitor company statuses and registrations based on their PAN.

3.1. PAN Dashboard
This dashboard allows you to:

View
     company statuses at a PAN level.
Compare
     registrations between the software and the GST portal.
3.2. Invoice-Wise Reconciliation in PAN View

This feature enables comparison of invoices recorded in
books against GST returns. The status categories include:

Cross
     Matched: Invoices fully matched across entities.
Cross
     Partial Match: Invoices partially matched across entities.
In
     Books Only: Invoices present in books but missing in GST returns.
In
     ISD Only: Invoices present in ISD data but missing in books.

      

4. Benefits of ISD and PAN Level Reconciliation
Ensures
     accurate ITC reporting and compliance.
Helps
     identify discrepancies at both branch and PAN level.
Facilitates
     timely corrective actions to avoid penalties or ITC loss.

title: ExpressGST || GSTR-1 – Preparation of Table 14 & Table 15 Data (Including GSTR-1A & IFF)
summary: 1. Background As per Notification No. 26/2022 – Central Tax, dated 26th December 2022, two additional tables have been introduced in GSTR-1 to capture supplies made through E-Commerce Operators (ECOs): Table 14: Supplies through ECOs liable to ...
content:
1. Background

As per Notification No. 26/2022 – Central Tax, dated 26th
December 2022, two additional tables have been introduced in GSTR-1
to capture supplies made through E-Commerce Operators (ECOs):

Table
     14: Supplies through ECOs liable to collect Tax Collected at Source
     (TCS) under Section 52.

Table
     15: Supplies through ECOs under Section 9(5).

These changes are effective from the January 2024 tax
period and are now available on the GST common portal.

2. Key Highlights
Applicability:
     From January 2024 onwards
Modes
     of Data Entry:

Manual
      Entry (View/Edit Data)

Excel
      Template Upload

Purpose:
     Accurate reporting of ECO-based transactions to ensure GST compliance.

3. Table-wise Details
3.1 Table 14 – Supplies through ECOs liable to collect
TCS (Section 52)
Captures
     all B2B and B2C supplies made through ECOs where TCS is applicable.

3.2 Table 15 – Supplies under Section 9(5)

Captures
     services notified under Section 9(5) of the CGST Act supplied through
     ECOs.

      

4. Modes of Data Entry
4.1 Excel Template Upload
Use the Blank Excel Template, which includes the
following sections:
Table
     1: Registered to Registered
Table
     2: Registered to Unregistered (R2U)
Table
     3: Unregistered to Registered
Table
     4: Unregistered to Unregistered (U2U)
Amendment
     Sections (if applicable)

Action: Fill the relevant details and import the
template into the portal.

4.2 Manual Entry (View/Edit Data)
Steps:
Navigate
     to GSTR-1 Filing → View/Edit Data.
Use
     the dropdown menu to select the last two options related to E-Commerce.
Enter
     the details manually for:

Table
      14: Supplies through ECOs under Section 52 (TCS)

Table
      15: Supplies under Section 9(5)

5. Process for Importing Book Data
Step 1: Log in to Express GST Portal and
select the client.

Step 2: Navigate to Return Summary → GSTR-1 → Prepare.

Step 3: Click Import and upload the file.

      

Step 4: Review and edit details if required.

      
      

6. Integration with E-Commerce Operators (Amazon &
Flipkart)
Steps for Importing E-Commerce Data:
Open GSTR-1
     Summary Details.
Click Import
     Book Data.

Select
     Import E-Commerce Template (Amazon/Flipkart).

Drag
     and drop the file or browse to upload.

Submit
     the data.

      

Post-Import Actions:

Data
     for B2B / B2C transactions will auto-populate in Table 14
     and Table 15.
Review,
     save, and proceed to file GSTR-1 as usual.
 

7. Compliance Notes

Dual
     Reporting Obligation:

File
      regular GSTR-1.

Report
      e-commerce transactions separately in Table 14.

This ensures accurate tracking of ECO-based supplies
and adherence to GST provisions.

title: ExpressGST || How to change or update the email address for sending reports via email
summary: To update the email address for receiving notifications for Reports, follow these steps: 1. Check Email Settings in the Client Master Information By default, emails are sent to the email ID listed under the Authorized Signatory section in Client ...
content:
To update the email address for receiving notifications for Reports, follow these steps:

1. Check Email Settings in the Client Master Information

By default, emails are sent to the email ID listed under the Authorized Signatory section in Client Master Information.
If there is no email in the Contact Person section, emails will only go to this default address.

      

2. Update the Contact Person Section
If the person listed in the Authorized Signatory section does not frequently check emails, you can add another contact in the Contact Person section.
This ensures emails are sent to the relevant person who can review them promptly.

      

3. Add Multiple Emails in the Contact Person Section
You can add more than one email address in this section.
All listed contacts will receive emails, ensuring key notifications reach multiple relevant people.

      

Note: Adding details in the Contact Person section is recommended. 

It ensures that important emails aren't missed if the Authorized Signatory is unavailable or occupied with other tasks.

title: Spectrum-CLOUD Mobile Application
summary: The Spectrum CLOUD Mobile Application enables users to securely access Spectrum Cloud data directly from their mobile devices. The application provides seamless access to GST, TDS, and ITR-related information, allowing users to monitor critical ...
content:
The Spectrum CLOUD Mobile Application enables users to securely access Spectrum Cloud data directly from their mobile devices. The application provides seamless access to GST, TDS, and ITR-related information, allowing users to monitor critical compliance activities anytime and anywhere through a secure, efficient, and user-friendly interface.

Key Features & Enhancements

1. Mobile – ITR Notices

Users can now access Income Tax Return (ITR) notices directly through the mobile application.

Features

View notice summaries

Access detailed notice information

Financial Year-wise notice mapping for easy reference
2. Mobile – TDS Notices

The application now supports mobile access to TDS-related notices, enabling users to manage and monitor notices more efficiently.

Features

View detailed notice information

Track notice status in real time

Identify the applicable section under which the notice has been issued

Integrated workflow support with existing TDS processes
3. Mobile Utilities

GSTIN Search → A quick and efficient utility for verifying GSTIN details.
Available Information

Legal Name

Registration Status

State Jurisdiction
TDS Rate Finder → Simplifies the process of identifying applicable TDS rates.
Benefits

Section-wise TDS rate identification

Helps minimize manual errors during deduction processing
Find HSN Code → Enables users to search and identify the appropriate HSN code through keyword-based lookup functionality.

IT New Session → Allows users to initiate a new Income Tax session directly from the mobile application with improved performance and seamless session initialization.

The application is designed to provide fast, secure, and convenient access to essential compliance data through a simplified mobile interface.

Accessing the Application

Downloading the Application →  To download the application:

Open the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.

Search for KDK Software.

Download and install the application.
Sign-In Process → Existing Spectrum Cloud Web Application users can log in using their registered mobile number.
Steps to Sign In

Enter the registered mobile number.

An OTP (One-Time Password) will be sent to the registered mobile number.

Enter the OTP to complete authentication and securely access the application.
Client Master → The Client Master section displays all companies configured within the Spectrum Cloud Web Application.
Companies are automatically categorized under the following modules:

GST

TDS

ITR
Accessing Company Information → To access company-related information:

Select the required company from the list.

Choose the appropriate module to view the relevant details.
GST Module → The GST Module provides comprehensive access to GST-related information for the selected company.
Available Sections

Notices

Ledgers

Returns
Notices → The Notices section enables users to view GST notices and orders received for the selected company.
Viewing Notice Details

Click the View (Eye) icon corresponding to the notice.

The application will display detailed information related to the selected notice or order.

Available Notice Information

ARN Number

Application / Case Creation Date

Notice Sub-Type

Status

Order Description

Order Number

Issued Date

Due Date

Demand Amount

Reply Details

This functionality helps users efficiently monitor notices and track compliance activities.

Ledgers → The Ledgers section provides access to various GST ledgers associated with the selected company.
Users can:

View ledger details directly within the application

Download ledger reports in Excel format for detailed analysis and record-keeping

Available Ledgers

Cash Ledger → Displays tax head-wise balances along with the total available balance in the Cash Ledger.
RCM Liability / ITC Statement → Provides details of Reverse Charge Mechanism (RCM) liabilities and Input Tax Credit (ITC) balances.
Negative Liability Statement → Displays details related to negative liability adjustments along with tax head-wise information.
Electronic Credit Reversal → Provides information regarding credit reversals recorded in the electronic credit ledger.
Credit Ledger → Displays Input Tax Credit balances with tax head-wise breakup.
Non-Return Liability Ledger → Shows liabilities arising due to non-filing of returns, including detailed tax head-wise balances.

Ledger Options → Each ledger provides the following options:

View (Eye) Icon – Access detailed ledger transactions

Date Range Filter – Review transactions for a selected period
Returns

The Returns section allows users to monitor the filing status of GST returns for the selected company.

Return Categories

Pending Returns

Overdue Returns

Filed Returns
Financial Year Selection → Users can select the applicable Financial Year (FY) to view return details for the respective period.

Return Information Views

The application provides two convenient viewing modes for return information.

Month View → The Month View enables users to:

Select a specific month

View return details for the selected month

Download or share return information
Return View → The Return View enables users to:

Select a specific return type

View filing status month-wise

Check return filing dates

Available GST Returns → The following GST returns can be viewed through the mobile application:

GSTR-1

GSTR-1A

GSTR-3B

GSTR-9

GSTR-9C

The KDK Software Mobile Application is available for download on both the Google Play Store and the Apple App Store, enabling users to access Spectrum CLOUD services seamlessly across Android and iOS platforms.

title: Express GST || Download GSTR-2A / GSTR-2B Reports
summary: Downloading GSTR-2A / GSTR-2B Reports in Express GST This document provides a structured procedure for downloading GSTR-2A and GSTR-2B reports in the Express GST application. These reports play a critical role in Input Tax Credit (ITC) verification, ...
content:
Downloading GSTR-2A / GSTR-2B Reports in Express GST

This document provides a structured procedure for downloading GSTR-2A and GSTR-2B reports in the Express GST application. These reports play a critical role in Input Tax Credit (ITC) verification, reconciliation, and compliance management.

​

Navigation Path: To access the required reports, navigate through the following path:

GSTR-3B → Eligible ITC → GSTR-2A/2B Report

Procedure to Download Reports

Step 1: Access GSTR-3B Module
 From the main dashboard, navigate to the GSTR-3B module.
Step 2: Open Eligible ITC Section
 Within the GSTR-3B module, select the Eligible ITC option.
Step 3: Select GSTR-2A/2B Report
 Click on GSTR-2A/2B Report from the available options.
      
Step 4: Initiate Report Download

To download GSTR-2A, click on Download 2A Report 

To download GSTR-2B, click on Download 2B Report 

Important Considerations

Ensure that the correct return period is selected prior to initiating the download process. 

GSTR-2A is a dynamic report, which is updated continuously based on supplier filings. 

GSTR-2B is a static report, generated for a specific return period and does not change once published. 

These reports are essential for accurate ITC reconciliation and statutory compliance. 

Troubleshooting Guidelines

In case of issues during report download, please verify the following:

Ensure a stable internet connection. 

Confirm that GST portal credentials and API integrations are properly configured and active. 

Refresh the application session and retry the process. 

If the issue persists, re-login to the application and attempt the download again. 

Downloading GSTR-2A and GSTR-2B reports from Express GST enables users to perform accurate ITC validation and maintain compliance with GST regulations. Adhering to the above steps and checks will ensure a smooth and error-free process.

title: Express Connector

summary: Express Connector – Installation & Troubleshooting Guide This document provides step-by-step instructions to install, verify, and troubleshoot the Express Connector to ensure uninterrupted application functionality. Overview: If you receive an error ...
content:
Express Connector – Installation & Troubleshooting Guide

This document provides step-by-step instructions to install, verify, and troubleshoot the Express Connector to ensure uninterrupted application functionality.

Overview: If you receive an error message related to the Express Connector, it typically indicates one of the following:

The connector is not installed.
The installed version is outdated.
The connector service is not running.
Browser security settings are restricting required permissions.

      
      

Please follow the instructions below to resolve the issue.

Step 1: Download the Express Connector

Log in to the application.
Download the latest available version of the Express Connector.

      
            

Step 2: Install the Express Connector

Open the Downloads folder on your system.
Locate the downloaded installer file.
Double-click the file to initiate the installation process.
Upon successful installation, the following confirmation message will appear: 
“The installation has completed successfully! Express Connector is ready to use.”

Click OK to close the installer.
Step 3: Verify Connector Status via Task Manager

After installation, please confirm that the connector service is running:
Open Task Manager (Ctrl + Shift + Esc).
Go to the Details tab.
Verify that ExpressConnector2.exe is listed and running.

If the process is not visible:
Restart your system.

Reopen Task Manager and verify again.
If the process still does not appear, reinstall the connector.

Step 4: Enable Required Browser Permissions (Google Chrome / Microsoft Edge)

When accessing the application via Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge, certain browser permissions must be enabled.

Recommended Method: Using Site Settings
Open the application in your browser.
Click the “View Site Information” icon located next to the address bar.
Locate and enable the following permissions:

Local Network Access

Apps on Device

Refresh the page or restart the browser to apply the changes.

Important: If these permissions are disabled, the application may not communicate properly with the Express Connector.

Conclusion
To ensure proper functionality of the application:

Install the latest version of the Express Connector.
Confirm that the connector service is actively running.
Enable the required browser permissions.

If the issue persists after completing all steps outlined above, please contact the Support Team and provide relevant screenshots or error messages for further assistance.

summary: NIC GSP Login Creation Process (Note: This is distinct from the NIC Portal Login credentials) To facilitate E-Way Bill Reconciliation, users are required to create a new GST Suvidha Provider (GSP) account. Follow the steps below to complete the ...
content:
NIC GSP Login Creation Process 

(Note: This is distinct from the NIC Portal Login credentials)

To facilitate E-Way Bill Reconciliation, users are required to create a new GST Suvidha Provider (GSP) account. Follow the steps below to complete the process efficiently:

Step-by-Step Guide to Create GSP Login

Step 1: Access the E-Way Bill System Portal

Navigate to the E-Way Bill system portal - https://ewaybill.nic.in/
Log in to the portal using your existing credentials.

      

Step 2: Initiate GSP Registration

Click on the “Registration” tab located in the left-hand menu.

From the dropdown menu, select “For GSP”.
      

Step 3: OTP Verification

The “Register your GST Suvidha Provider / ERP” window will appear.
Click “Send OTP”, input the OTP received on your registered mobile number, and then click “Verify OTP”.
      
      
      
Upon successful OTP verification, you will be redirected to the next step.

Step 4: Complete the GSP Registration

In the subsequent window, perform the following actions:

Select “Add/New” to begin the GSP Registration.
      
From the “GSP Name” dropdown, choose - "IRIS BUSINESS SERVICES LIMITED".
Enter a unique username for your GSP by appending any three alphabetic characters of your choice as a suffix (confirm by entering the same value twice).
      
Create a password (maximum of 15 characters) and confirm it by re-entering. This username and password will be used as NIC GSP credentials for future access.

Click “Add” to finalize the registration.

Step 5: Review GSP Details

After successfully creating your GSP, use the “View” option to verify and review the following details:

      
GSP Name

GSTIN

Username

Status

Type
Registered By

Registration Date

By following these steps, you will successfully generate NIC GSP login credentials, including the NIC GSP Username and Password, for seamless access to E-Way Bill services.

Watch the full tutorial here : NIC GSP Login Creation Process

      ​

title: Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for Enhanced Security
tags: enable two-factor authentication for enhanced security
enhance your account security with two-factor authentication
summary: To ensure the highest level of security for your account, we strongly recommend enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) across all our applications, including ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS, and ExpressReco. With 2FA enabled, a single One-Time ...
content:
To ensure the highest level of security for your account, we strongly recommend enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) across all our applications, including ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS, and ExpressReco.

With 2FA enabled, a single One-Time Password (OTP) generated through the Google Authenticator app will be used for authentication across all these applications, ensuring seamless yet secure access.
​

Why Enable 2FA?

2FA provides an additional layer of protection by requiring a One-Time Password (OTP) generated via the Google Authenticator app. This extra security measure helps prevent unauthorized access, even if your password is compromised.

​

Steps to Enable 2FA:

Access Security Settings: Navigate to your Profile and select the Security option.

Initiate Setup: Click on "Set Up Now" to begin the activation process.

Install Google Authenticator : Download the Google Authenticator app on your mobile device.

Scan the QR Code: Use the authenticator app to scan the QR code displayed on your screen.

Enter the OTP: Retrieve the generated OTP from the app and enter it to complete the setup.

​

How 2FA Works During Login:

Once enabled, every login attempt will require an OTP from Google Authenticator. Simply open the app, retrieve the latest code, and enter it to access your account. 

      

Without the correct OTP, login will be denied.

      
​

​

The Importance of 2FA:

By activating 2FA, you are significantly enhancing your account’s security, making it nearly impossible for unauthorized users to gain access — even if they have your password. This is a crucial step in protecting your personal data and preventing security breaches.

🔒 Secure your account today by enabling 2FA.

title: ExpressTDS || Renewal Process
tags: steps to renew your expresstds subscription
summary: Renewing your ExpressTDS subscription is a seamless process designed to ensure uninterrupted access to the platform’s comprehensive features and services. Follow these detailed steps to complete your subscription renewal effortlessly. Steps to Renew ...
content:
Renewing your ExpressTDS subscription is a seamless process designed to ensure uninterrupted access to the platform’s comprehensive features and services. Follow these detailed steps to complete your subscription renewal effortlessly.

      

Steps to Renew Your ExpressTDS Subscription

Step 1: Access Profile Settings

To begin, log in to your ExpressTDS account and navigate to the top-right corner of the screen. Click on the Profile Settings option to access your account settings.
Step 2: Open the Subscription Plan Section

Within your profile, locate and click on the Subscription Plan tab. This section provides an overview of your current plan and renewal options.

      
Step 3: Initiate the Renewal Process

Click on the Renew Now button to proceed. This will guide you to the plan selection page.

      

Step 4: Select a Subscription Plan

Browse the list of available plans and choose the one that best aligns with your requirements. Once you’ve made your selection, click the Select button to confirm.

      

Step 5: Enter Billing Information

Provide accurate billing details in the designated fields. Once all required information has been entered, click on the Make Payment button to process the transaction securely.

      

Step 6: Confirm Payment

Upon successful payment, your subscription will be renewed instantly. A confirmation email or notification will be sent to your registered account.

Step 7: Re-login to Finalize Changes

For the renewal to take full effect, log out and log back into your ExpressTDS account. This step ensures all updates are synchronized with your account.

By following these steps, you can easily renew your ExpressTDS subscription and maintain uninterrupted access to its robust features and services.

title: ExpressTDS || Outstanding Demand
summary: Process to Check Outstanding Demand in ExpressTDS In Express TDS, an Outstanding Demand refers to a notice issued by the Income Tax Department when there is a discrepancy between the TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) reported by the deductor and the TDS ...
content:
Process to Check Outstanding Demand in ExpressTDS

In Express TDS, an Outstanding Demand refers to a notice issued by the Income Tax Department when there is a discrepancy between the TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) reported by the deductor and the TDS actually deposited. This occurs when the department identifies that the deductor owes an additional TDS amount due to underpayment, short payment, or incorrect filing. Such discrepancies are reflected in the Outstanding Demand section of Express TDS.

Steps to Check Outstanding Demand in ExpressTDS:

Step 1: Log in to ExpressTDS

Log in to the ExpressTDS portal with your username and
password, the Deductor Master page will open, listing all the deductors.
Step 2: Select the Deductor

From the Deductor
Master, click on a particular deductor to access their page.

      

Step 3: Choose Financial Year

In the deductor’s profile, use the dropdown menu to select the relevant financial year.

      
Ensure that the financial year corresponds to the period of the outstanding demand.

Step 4: View Outstanding Demand

Scroll down to locate the Outstanding Demand section.

      
Review the details, including:

Amount due

Nature of the demand (e.g., short payment, late payment)

Relevant assessment year.

Important Note: Ensure that the data sync is completed in ExpressTDS to get the latest updates from the Income Tax Portal.

By following this process, you can easily check and verify the Outstanding Demand for any deductor in ExpressTDS.

Syncing TDS Returns, Acknowledgments, Challans, PAN Status, Notices and Orders and Raised Request Status data in the ExpressTDS software ensures your records remain accurate and up-to-date.

Step 2: Initiate Sync

Locate the Sync Option: Find the "Sync" button at the top-right corner of the page.

Click Sync: Start the synchronization process by clicking the "Sync" button.

      
Enter Captcha: Complete the CAPTCHA verification to confirm you are not a robot.

Proceed: After entering the CAPTCHA, click the "Proceed" button to continue.

      

💡 Important Notes

✔ Stable Internet Connection: Ensure a reliable connection to prevent sync interruptions.

✔ Updated Credentials: Verify that your TRACES, Income Tax, or Insight credentials are up to date.
📌 Ensure the latest Express Connector is running before syncing data.
🔗 Download & Install the Latest Connector Here: 👉 Express Connector - Installation Guide

By following these steps, your data will always stay synchronized and up-to-date with the portal.

title: ExpressTDS || Notice & Orders
summary: Viewing and Downloading Notices & Orders in ExpressTDS ExpressTDS provides a comprehensive feature that allows users to view and download all Notices and Orders for all Deductors within a single-page view. This centralized interface simplifies the ...
content:
Viewing and Downloading Notices & Orders in ExpressTDS

ExpressTDS provides a comprehensive feature that allows users to view and download all Notices and Orders for all Deductors within a single-page view. This centralized interface simplifies the monitoring and management of multiple Deductors' notices and orders, streamlining the process of accessing necessary documents without navigating individual Deductor profiles.

Notices in ExpressTDS refer to official communications or alerts issued by the Income Tax Department to the Deductor or taxpayer regarding issues such as discrepancies, delays, or compliance concerns related to TDS filings.

Orders refer to formal directives from the Income Tax Department issued after the assessment or review of TDS returns or compliance matters. An order may result from a notice, audit findings, or non-compliance.

Steps to View and Download Notices & Orders in ExpressTDS:

Step 1: Access the ExpressTDS portal using your username and password. Once logged in, the Deductor Master page will open, displaying a list of all Deductors.
Step 2: Click on the "Notice & Orders" button. This will open the page containing all Notices and Orders for the selected Deductor.

      

Step 3: To download the Notice/Orders, click on the PDF icon corresponding to the document you wish to download.

      

Additionally, you can easily download the Justification Report by clicking on the "Excel" icon, or view the Justification Report by clicking on the "eye" button.

      

      

If any Notices/Orders are received, you can easily raise a Justification Request directly from this page by clicking on the icon shown below.

      

By following these steps, you can easily view and download all Notices and Orders related to your Deductors in ExpressTDS, ensuring efficient document management and compliance tracking.

title: ExpressTDS || Return Register
summary: How to View and Download Return Register Reports in ExpressTDS The Return Register in ExpressTDS is a comprehensive module that records and tracks all TDS returns filed by a Deductor across various financial periods in a single-page view. It serves ...
content:
How to View and Download Return Register Reports in ExpressTDS

The Return Register in ExpressTDS is a comprehensive module that records and tracks all TDS returns filed by a Deductor across various financial periods in a single-page view. It serves as a detailed log, offering clear insights into the Deductor’s filing activities, providing a streamlined and organized summary of all TDS returns for efficient tracking and compliance.

Steps to View and Download Return Register Reports:

Step 1: Access the ExpressTDS portal by entering your username and password. Once logged in, the Deductor Master page will appear, displaying a list of all deductors.

Step 2: In the Deductor Master page, click on the "Return Register" button.

      

Step 3: A new page will open, showing all the returns filed by the Deductor. This page is divided into two sections:

Return Wise: Displays the returns organized by return type.

Deductor Wise: Shows the returns organized by Deductor.
Step 4: To download a specific Return Receipt/Acknowledgement, click the PDF icon. This will download the selected return receipt.

      

Step 5: To view returns by Deductor, click the "Deductor Wise" tab.

      

Note: After using the "Sync Now" button, the data should always reflect the most up-to-date information.

      

By following these steps, you can easily view and download Return Register reports in ExpressTDS, allowing you to keep track of all TDS filing activities.

title: ExpressTDS || Compliance Check Process
summary: Compliance Check for “Specified Person” in ExpressTDS The functionality in ExpressTDS allows tax deductors or collectors to verify if an individual is a “Specified Person” as defined under Section 206AB and 206CCA. The verification can be done using ...
content:
Compliance Check for “Specified Person” in ExpressTDS

The functionality in ExpressTDS allows tax deductors or collectors to verify if an individual is a “Specified Person” as defined under Section 206AB and 206CCA. The verification can be done using two modes: PAN Search (for single PAN verification) or Bulk Search (for multiple PANs).

Prerequisites

Ensure the deductor is registered on https://report.insight.gov.in/.

Update your User ID and password in Deductor Master within ExpressTDS.

Compliance Check Options

Option 1: By PAN Compliance Option

Step 1: Select the relevant Deductor/Company.

Step 2: Click on PAN Compliances.

      

For Single PAN Verification:

Enter the deductee’s PAN in the designated field and click verify.

      

For Bulk PAN Verification:

Select Bulk Upload.

Download the provided Excel template, fill in the PAN details, and save.

      
Import the completed Excel template and click on Start Verification.

      

Once verification is complete, the status will be displayed, and a report will be sent to the registered email address.

Option 2: By Computation and Health Option

Step 1: Select the Deductor/Company.

Step 2: In the Return Summary, select the return for which you want to perform the compliance check.

      

Step 3: Click on Computation and Health.

      

Step 4: In the Compliance Check Status table, click View Detail.

      

Step 5: Enter the Captcha to proceed; the status will update shortly.

      
      

Step 6: To download the updated information, use the Download option under Computation and Health.

Option 3: By Total Deductee Option

Step 1: Select your Deductor/Company.

Step 2: Go to the Total Deductee View option.

      

Step 3: Prepare and import data following these instructions.

Step 4: Click on Compliance Check.

      

Step 5: Wait for the status to update, then download the results using the Export Excel option.

This comprehensive approach allows for efficient compliance verification with Sections 206AB and 206CCA, ensuring accuracy in deductor obligations.

content:
Late Fee Allocation in ExpressTDS

To allocate a late fee in ExpressTDS, follow these structured steps:

Step 1: Navigate to Deductor Master

Access Deductor Master from the main menu.

Select the relevant Company or Deductor.

Go to the Return Summary and choose the return you want to manage.
Step 2: View the Created Return

Find the View option next to the chosen return.

      
Click on View to access detailed information.

Step 3: Access Late Fee Allocation

Within the return details, go to Computation and Health.

      
Locate the Late Fee section in this area.

      

Late Fee Allocation Steps:

If the Challan for Late Fee is Already Paid:

In the TDS Computation table, click on the Late Fee link icon.

Choose the appropriate challan to allocate the late fee and click Save.

      

If the Challan for Late Fee is Not Paid:

In the TDS Computation table, click on the Late Fee option.
Select the Pay with Challan option, and the data will auto-fill for payment.

      
Choose I Will Deposit Now to proceed with the payment.

After completing payment, allocate the late fee challan as needed.

      

Following these steps allows for efficient allocation of late fees in ExpressTDS, ensuring accurate and compliant return processing.

title: ExpressTDS || Deductor master creation
summary: Deductor Master Creation Guide for ExpressTDS This guide provides instructions on how to create a Deductor Master in ExpressTDS using three methods: Using ITD Credentials: Add deductors by entering ITD login details. Bulk Excel Import: Create ...
content:
Deductor Master Creation Guide for ExpressTDS

This guide provides instructions on how to create a Deductor Master in ExpressTDS using three methods:
Using ITD Credentials: Add deductors by entering ITD login details.
Bulk Excel Import: Create multiple deductors simultaneously using an Excel file.
Importing TXT and TDS/Conso Files: Add multiple deductors by uploading TXT or TDS/Conso files.

      
Using ITD Credentials

Step 1: Login to www.expresstds.com and click on "Add Deductor".

      

Step 2: Enter your Income Tax Portal credentials and Traces Credentials with Passwords.

      
      

Step 3: Click "Proceed" to automatically add the company to the Deductor Master.

Bulk Excel Import
Step 1: Select "Add Deductor" on the Deductor Master page.

Step 2: Click the "Browse Excel" option to download the provided Excel template.

      

      

Step 3: Open the template and fill in the following details:

Deductor Name

Deductor Username (TAN)

Income Tax Password

      

Step 4: Once filled, drag and drop the file into the system or click to select it for upload.

      

Step 5: The system will automatically begin creating deductor records based on the uploaded data.

      

Importing TXT and TDS/Conso Files
Step 1: Click "Add Deductor" on the Deductor Master page.

Step 2: Select the "Browse Folder" option to locate the TEXT or TDS/Conso file.

      

Step 3: Navigate to the file location and click "Import" to upload the data.

      

Step 4: Confirm the import by clicking "Okay".

      

Step 5: For each client, enter the corresponding password, then click "Create Client" to finalize.

Step 6: Click "Save" to complete the process.

Note : By using the "Auto Scan" button, the application can automatically search for and locate any available TXT and TDS/Conso files on your system. Once identified, it will import these files into the application, streamlining the data import process.

      

By following these steps, you can efficiently create a Deductor Master using any of the three methods in ExpressTDS.

Import the Conso File for Corrections in ExpressTDS

Follow these steps to import a consolidated (Conso) file and begin the correction process in ExpressTDS:

Step 1: Navigate to the Company/Deductor Section

Open ExpressTDS and go to the Company/Deductor section.

Access the Return Summary for the relevant company or deductor.

Confirm you have the necessary Conso file by choosing "I already have a consolidated file".

Use the drag-and-drop feature to upload your Conso file into the designated area.

If a Conso File Request Was Raised but Not Downloaded Yet

Raise a New Request: Navigate to TRACES and use the "Raise a New Request" option to generate a request for the Conso file.

      
Update the Status: Sync ExpressTDS with TRACES to update the status of any previously raised requests.

      
Enter the CAPTCHA code as prompted and click "Submit" to verify the update.
Download the File: Once the file status changes to "Available", click the "Download" option to retrieve the Conso file using the TRACES utility.

      

Step 4: Start the Correction Process

After successfully importing the Conso file, click on "Start Correction" to begin making adjustments as required.

This step-by-step guide simplifies importing a Conso file and initiating corrections, ensuring an efficient and accurate workflow.

title: ExpressTDS || Raise Request / Submit Request
summary: In ExpressTDS, the Raise Request feature allows users to submit formal queries or requests for assistance related to their TDS filing activities. This can include downloading specific forms, certificates, or resolving issues with TDS filings. A ...
content:
In ExpressTDS, the Raise Request feature allows users to submit formal queries or requests for assistance related to their TDS filing activities. This can include downloading specific forms, certificates, or resolving issues with TDS filings.

A standout feature of ExpressTDS is its ability to streamline the request process by allowing users to request multiple items, such as the Consolidated File, Justification Report, and Bulk Form 16A, for a specific financial year and quarter with a single click. This simplifies the task of gathering essential documents and reports, significantly reducing the time and effort required to manage TDS compliance and reporting.

Steps to Raise a New Request in ExpressTDS:

Step 1: Access the ExpressTDS portal using your username and password. After logging in, the Deductor Master page will display a list of all deductors associated with your account.

      

Step 2: Click on the name of the deductor for whom you wish to raise a request. This will open the Deductor's profile page.

Step 3: Scroll down the Deductor’s page to find the "View all the Raised Requests" section.

      

Step 4: Select "View All Raised Requests" to display the list of submitted requests to TRACES. Then, click "Submit Request" to initiate a new request.

      
Step 5: Select Request Type and Proceed.

Request For: Choose the specific form type you want to request (e.g., Bulk Form 16).

Form Type: Select the appropriate form (e.g., Form 24Q).

Financial Year: Specify the relevant financial year (e.g., 2023-24).

Quarter: Select the quarter of the financial year (e.g., Q4).

      

Step 6: You can submit the request using one of three methods:
Using TRACES Form (if return is filed via ExpressTDS),

Using TRACES Form (Manual Data entry),

Using FVU/TXT Import (if the return was filed via another software)

Using TRACES Form (if return is filed via ExpressTDS):

The system auto-fetches challan details, deductee transactions, and token numbers if the TDS return was filed through ExpressTDS.

      
Click Proceed to continue. 

A captcha window will appear. Enter the captcha code and click Proceed to confirm the request.

      

Using TRACES Form (Manual Data entry):
Manually input the challan details, deductee transactions, and token number. The token number can be found in the TDS return acknowledgment receipt (RRR number). Make sure to correctly enter the Deductor TAN, Financial Year, and Quarter when entering the token number.

Input the BSR Code, Date of Deposit, Challan Number, Challan Amount, PAN of Deductee, and Tax Deducted amount.

Click Proceed after entering the information.

Using FVU Import (if the return was filed via another software):
If you have an TXT file from another software, select Using FVU Import to autofill the challan and deductee details.

      
Click Browse to upload the TXT file and then click Proceed to import the data.

Once all details are entered, and the process is completed, your request is successfully raised for downloading forms from TRACES.

By following these steps, you can raise a new request in ExpressTDS efficiently.

Salary Details Correction Process

The following steps outline the professional process for correcting salary details in the system:

Step 1: Log In and Access Deductor Master

Navigate to https://www.expresstds.com/ and log in with your credentials.

Access the Deductor Master section from the dashboard.

Select the relevant deductor or organization for which the correction return needs to be prepared.

      

Step 2: Locate the Return for Correction

Identify the original return requiring corrections in the list of submitted returns.

Click on the View option to open the details of the selected return.

      

Step 3: Initiate the Correction Process

Click on the Start Correction button.

      
The system will display the original return data.

Navigate to the Salary section to proceed with the corrections.

      

Step 4: Update Salary Details

Review Existing Details: Existing salary details cannot be edited directly. However, you can view or download the computation for reference.
Remove Incorrect Details: Delete the salary records that require correction.

      

Add Revised Salary Details: Enter the corrected salary details, ensuring the updated information is accurate and complete.

      

      

Save Changes: Confirm and save the updated details to finalize the correction process.

      

This structured approach ensures accurate and seamless updates to salary details, maintaining compliance and data integrity within the system.

title: ExpressTDS || Add/Edit Deductee in correction

content:
Deductee Correction Process in ExpressTDS

To perform corrections for deductees in ExpressTDS, follow these steps based on whether you’re making a single deductee correction or a bulk correction.

Access the Correction Section

Navigate to the Company/Deductor: Select the relevant company or deductor.

Go to Return Summary: Find the correction return and click on the View option.

      

For Single Deductee Correction

Import the Conso File: After importing, click on Start Correction.

      

Select Deductee: Choose the specific deductee and their PAN for correction.

Add New Deductee: Click on the Add icon to include a new deductee in the return.

      

      
Edit Existing Deductee: Click on the Action icon next to an existing deductee to make changes.

      

For Bulk Deductee Correction

Import the Conso File: After importing, click on Start Correction.

Import Return from Excel: Choose this option to upload changes via Excel.

      

Export Existing Data: Use the Export Excel option to download current data.

      

Add/Edit Deductees in Excel: Make the required changes in the Excel file, then save it.

Import Correction Excel: Use the Import Return from Excel option to upload the updated file.

      
Check Status: Use the newly added/updated checkbox option to verify all changes.

      

Important Notes :

New Deductee Information: When adding a new deductee, ensure that their PAN and transaction details are accurately entered in the deductee transactions.

Updated PAN or Deductee Information: If an existing deductee’s details are updated (e.g., name change, new PAN, or changes to the transaction type), the system will automatically update the associated deductee transactions.

This structured approach makes single or bulk deductee corrections manageable and ensures accuracy in data adjustments.

summary: The Section 197 Certificate, commonly known as the Lower Deduction Certificate (LDC), is a key provision under the Income Tax Act that allows taxpayers to avail a Nil or reduced rate of Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) on their income. Steps to Validate ...
content:
The Section 197 Certificate, commonly known as the Lower Deduction Certificate (LDC), is a key provision under the Income Tax Act that allows taxpayers to avail a Nil or reduced rate of Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) on their income.

Steps to Validate the Certificate

Step 1: Login and Select the Deductor

Log in to ExpressTDS website.

From the Deductor Master list, select the company/deductor for which the TDS return is being prepared.

Step 2: Start Filing

Navigate to the Return Summary section.

Click Start Filing.

      
Choose the appropriate Form (e.g., 26Q), quarter, financial year, and type (Regular or Correction), then click Submit.

      
      
Step 3: Create the Return

Click the View option to initiate the return creation process.
      

Step 4: Add the Lower Deduction Certificate Details

Ensure the Certificate Number and Rate entered match exactly with the details provided in the certificate issued on the portal.
      

Step 5: Compute and Review

Navigate to the Computation and Health section to validate all details.
      

Step 6: Verify the Lower Deduction Certificate

Click on the Verify option for the Lower Deduction Certificate.

Enter the Captcha and proceed with the verification.
      
Once completed, the status will display as Valid or Invalid. If invalid, correct the details and re-verify.
      
      

We hope this clarifies the process.
