title: ExpressTDS || Salary TDS Calculator
summary: The Salary TDS Calculator in Express TDS is a tool designed to assist users in accurately calculating TDS based on income, payments, or transactions subject to tax deduction. It automates the calculation process, ensuring compliance with the latest ...
content:
The Salary TDS Calculator in Express TDS is a tool designed to assist users in accurately calculating TDS based on income, payments, or transactions subject to tax deduction. It automates the calculation process, ensuring compliance with the latest tax laws and minimizing human error.

Steps to View the Salary TDS Calculator Report:

Step 1: Access the Express TDS portal by entering your username and password. Once logged in, you will be directed to the Deductor Master page, where all deductors are listed.
Step 2: Select the desired deductor from the list to proceed. This will bring you to a page where the Salary TDS Calculator section is visible.
Step 3: Click on "Salary TDS Calc" to open the Salary TDS Calculator.

      

Step 4: An Import Excel File wizard will appear. You can either:
Import a pre-filled Salary TDS Excel file, or

Download an Excel template to fill in and upload later.

      

Step 5: If you don’t have the template, click Download Excel Template, and fill in the relevant salary and TDS data.

      

Step 6: Once the Excel file is ready, click Browse to locate and select the file from your computer. Select the Salary TDS calculator Excel file and click Open.

      

Step 7: After selecting the file, click Submit to import the Salary TDS Calculator Excel file into Express TDS.
Step 8: Upon successful import, the Employee-Wise Salary Details section will open, displaying the salary data for each employee.

      

Step 9: To view the detailed TDS computation report, click View.

      

Step 10: To see the monthly breakdown of TDS for each employee, click Month Wise.

By following these steps, you can successfully view the Salary TDS Calculator report in Express TDS.

summary: Steps to Download TDS Return Data in Excel Format via ExpressTDS You can download TDS return data for both regular and correction returns in Excel format. Ensure transactions have been created in the return before proceeding. Here’s how: Download TDS ...
content:
Steps to Download TDS Return Data in Excel Format via ExpressTDS

You can download TDS return data for both regular and correction returns in Excel format. Ensure transactions have been created in the return before proceeding. Here’s how:

Download TDS Data for Regular Returns

Process 1: Through Return Summary

Navigate to Deductor Master → After logging in, go to the Deductor Master section to access TDS returns and company details.
Select the Company → Choose the relevant company for which you want to download TDS data.
Download in Excel Format → Look for an Excel icon or Download option within the TDS return section. Click the icon to download the TDS return in Excel format.
      

Process 2: Through Deductee Transaction Page

View Created Return → Go to the Return Summary section. Click on View button.
      

Access Deductee Transaction Summary → Within the return details page, locate the Deductee Transaction Summary section to view all deductee transactions.
Download Return Data in Excel → In the Deductee Transaction Summary section, click Export or Download to save the data in Excel format.
      

Export Error Records (if any) → If errors exist, find the Error Record or Error Summary option. Click on it to export any error data in Excel.
      

Download TDS Data for Correction Returns

Navigate to the Correction Return → Go to the Return Summary section and click View next to the Correction Return you want to download.
      

Import Consolidated File (if not yet imported) → If the required consolidated file hasn’t been imported, do so at this step.
Export Data from Quick Links Table → If you need to update or correct the return, go to the Quick Links table. Click Import Return from Excel if you are importing data for corrections.
      

Export the Corrected Return to Excel → After making necessary corrections, click Export Data to download the corrected return in Excel format.
      

By following these steps, you can efficiently download TDS return data for both regular and correction returns in ExpressTDS, ensuring all details are available in an editable Excel format.

How to View and Work on "Returns in Progress" in ExpressTDS

This will display a list of TDS returns that are currently in progress.

For Regular Returns: Continue adding or reviewing income and TDS data.

For Correction Returns: Edit and rectify the required details.

Tips:

Save Regularly: Always save your work to avoid data loss.

Monitor Deadlines: Ensure returns in progress are filed within the due date.

Check for Errors: Use the validation feature to identify and resolve errors before finalizing.

By using the "Returns in Progress" feature, you can efficiently manage and complete TDS returns for your clients.

tags: adding an employee-deductee master in expresstds

summary: This guide provides step-by-step instructions for managing the Employee/Deductee Master in ExpressTDS, including how to add, edit, activate, or inactivate records. Adding an Employee/Deductee Master in ExpressTDS Step 1: Log In and Navigate to ...
content:
This guide provides step-by-step instructions for managing the Employee/Deductee Master in ExpressTDS, including how to add, edit, activate, or inactivate records.

Adding an Employee/Deductee Master in ExpressTDS

Step 1: Log In and Navigate to Deductor Master

Visit ExpressTDS website and log in using your credentials.

Navigate to the Deductor Master page and select the relevant company or deductor from the list.

      

Step 2: Access Deductee Details

On the dashboard, locate the Total Deductee table.

Click on the View All option to access the deductee details.

      

Step 3: Add Deductee Manually

Click on the Add icon next to the Deductee Name / Name as per PAN field.

      
Enter the required Employee/Deductee details in the provided fields.

      
Click Save to store the information.

Step 4: Add Deductee in Bulk

Click on the Import option on the right side of the screen.

      
Select Download Excel Template to download the template.

      

Step 5: Fill the Excel Template

Open the downloaded Excel file and fill in the required Employee/Deductee details (e.g., PAN, Name, etc.).

      

Step 6: Upload the Completed Excel Template

Return to the software and click Import again.

Select and upload the filled Excel template from your device.

      
Click Submit to complete the import process.

Editing an Employee/Deductee Master in ExpressTDS

Locate the desired record in the Employee/Deductee Master list.

Click the Edit icon for the specific Employee/Deductee.

      

Make the necessary changes in the editable fields.

       

Click Save to update the information.

Deactivating an Employee/Deductee Master in ExpressTDS

Locate the Employee/Deductee you wish to inactivate.

Click the Edit icon for the record.

      
Switch to the Deactivate option.

      
Click Save to inactivate the record.

Activating an Employee/Deductee Master in ExpressTDS

Locate the inactivated Employee/Deductee.

Switch to the Activate option.

      

Click Save to reactivate the record.

By following these steps, you can efficiently manage Employee/Deductee Master records in ExpressTDS, whether adding, editing, activating or inactivating records. This ensures your data is accurate and up to date.

title: ExpressTDS || Change Personal, Contact, Organization Information, and Login Password
summary: How to Change Personal, Contact, Organization Information, and Login Password in ExpressTDS To keep your personal, contact, and organization information, as well as your login password up-to-date in ExpressTDS, follow these steps: 1. Access Profile ...
content:
How to Change Personal, Contact, Organization Information, and Login Password in ExpressTDS

To keep your personal, contact, and organization information, as well as your login password up-to-date in ExpressTDS, follow these steps:

1. Access Profile Settings

Log in to ExpressTDS and click on the Profile Icon located in the top right corner.

      
From the dropdown menu, select Profile Settings to access your account settings.

      

2. Update Personal & Contact Information

In Profile Settings, go to the Personal & Contact Information section.

Click the pencil icon to edit details, including the contact person’s name, mobile number, and email address.

      
After entering the new information, click Save to apply the changes.

3. Change Organization Information

Navigate to the Organization Information section to update the organization name as needed.

      
Once updated, click Save to confirm changes.

Note: Organization/Firm type cannot be changed.

4. Change Login Password

To update your login password, select Reset Password in the profile settings.

      
Enter the new password as prompted and save it to complete the change.

Following these steps will help ensure that your profile and account information in ExpressTDS remains accurate and current.

title: ExpressTDS || Login to TRACES/Income Tax Portal
tags: login to the income tax portal
summary: Process to Login at TRACES/Income Tax Portal through Express TDS Here is a step-by-step guide to access the TRACES and Income Tax portals through Express TDS: Step 1: Login to Express TDS Open a web browser and go to ...
content:
Process to Login at TRACES/Income Tax Portal through Express TDS

Here is a step-by-step guide to access the TRACES and Income Tax portals through Express TDS:

Step 1: Login to Express TDS

Open a web browser and go to https://www.expresstds.com/app/login.

Enter your User ID & Password to log in.

Step 2: Select Company/Deductor

After logging in, locate the section to select the Company/Deductor.

      
Choose the correct organization or entity for which you need TDS-related services, especially if you manage multiple entities.

Step 3: Login to the TRACES Portal

After selecting the company/deductor, look for the TRACES icon.

      
Click on this icon, and it will redirect you to the TRACES portal login page.

Step 4: Login to the Income Tax Portal

In the same dashboard, find the ITD icon.

      
Click on the ITD icon to be redirected to the Income Tax Portal (https://eportal.incometax.gov.in/iec/foservices/#/login).

Important Tips:

Update User ID and Password: Ensure that the credentials for both TRACES and Income Tax portals are updated and accurate.

Reset Forgotten Password: If you’ve forgotten your password, use the password reset option on the respective portal’s login page.

By following these steps, you can smoothly access TRACES and the Income Tax portal through Express TDS.

title: ExpressTDS || Raise a Request for a Consolidated File
summary: Process for Raise a Request for a Consolidated File in ExpressTDS Follow these methods to raise a request for a consolidated file through ExpressTDS: Method 1: Raise a Request via Notices and Orders Step 1: Go to Company/Deductor in ExpressTDS. Step ...
content:
Process for Raise a Request for a Consolidated File in ExpressTDS

Follow these methods to raise a request for a consolidated file through ExpressTDS:

Method 1: Raise a Request via Notices and Orders

Step 1: Go to Company/Deductor in ExpressTDS.
Step 2: Scroll to find the "View All the Raised Requests" option to see all submitted requests.

      

Step 3: Click on Submit Request to begin a new request for a consolidated file.

      

Step 4: Specify Request Details

Request For: Choose the form type (e.g., Form 26Q).

Financial Year and Quarter: Select the financial year and quarter (e.g., Q1 of 2024-25).

Form Type: Choose the appropriate form type.

      

Step 5: Choose a Request Method

Using TRACES Form (Auto-fetch): If filed through ExpressTDS, the system fetches data automatically.

TRACES Form (Manual Data Entry): Manually enter details like Deductor TAN, Challan Number, PAN of Deductee, and Tax Deducted Amount.

Using FVU/TXT Import: Import data from a .TXT file by browsing and uploading the file.

Process 1: Using TRACES Form (Auto-fetch)
If the return was filed via ExpressTDS:

The system will automatically fetch the necessary details.

      
Click Proceed to continue.

Process 2: Using TRACES Form (Manual Data Entry)

For manually entering details:

Input Required Information: Manually enter details such as:

Deductor TAN

Financial Year

Quarter

BSR Code

Challan Number

Challan Amount

PAN of Deductee

Tax Deducted Amount

Token Number (RRR number from TDS acknowledgment)

      
Proceed: After entering all the details, click Proceed to move forward.

Process 3: Using FVU/TXT Import

If using a .TXT file generated by another software:

Upload the .TXT file by selecting the Browse button.

      

      
Click Proceed to import the data.

Step 6: Enter the captcha code to proceed and confirm your request.

      

Step 7: Once all details are entered and the captcha is verified, your request will be successfully submitted. You can download the forms from TRACES once the request is processed.

By following these steps, you can efficiently submit a request for a consolidated file, choosing the method that best suits your data entry needs.

Method 2: Raise a Request Through the Correction Return Process

Step 1: Go to the Return Summary section. Select the Create Correction Return option and choose I don’t have a consolidated file.

      

Step 2: Choose a Submission Method and Select one of the following:
TRACES Form (Auto-fetch): Auto-fetch details if filed via ExpressTDS.

TRACES Form (Manual Data Entry): Manually enter challan and deductee information.

Using FVU/TXT Import: Upload a .TXT file with details.

      

Step 3: Complete the CAPTCHA verification to confirm your submission.
Step 4: Once available from TRACES, import the consolidated file into ExpressTDS to start the correction.

Enabling Automatic Consolidate Request in ExpressTDS

Step 1: Access Profile Settings

Click on your profile icon.

Select Conso Request Configuration from the menu.
      
Step 2: Enable Auto Consolidation

Choose either All Returns or Returns with Default.
      
Enabling this option ensures that the consolidation request is automatically raised if return details are already available in the Return Summary.

Important Note:

📌 Ensure that the latest Express Connector is running before proceeding.
🔗 Download and install the latest connector here: Express Connector Download & Installation Guide

By following these steps, you can efficiently raise a request for a consolidated file in ExpressTDS, ensuring you have the necessary data for correction processes.

title: ExpressTDS || FVU Validate Error → Missing Deductor Information

fvu generate error → missing deductor info
summary: The File Validation Utility (FVU) is a critical tool for validating TDS returns before submission to the Income Tax Department. During the validation process, certain critical errors, known as "showstoppers," may arise. These must be resolved to ...
content:
The File Validation Utility (FVU) is a critical tool for validating TDS returns before submission to the Income Tax Department. During the validation process, certain critical errors, known as "showstoppers," may arise. These must be resolved to ensure the successful generation and submission of returns.

Common Show Stoppers Issues

Missing Details: Errors may occur due to incomplete or missing deductor information, which is essential for processing.

      

Steps to Validate and Upload FVU Returns in ExpressTDS

Step 1: Log in and Access the Deductor Master

Visit ExpressTDS Website and log in using your credentials.

Navigate to the Deductor Master section.

Select the relevant company or deductor from the list.

Step 2: Review the Raised Return

Locate the return you have raised and click View to open the details.

Carefully review all fields to ensure accuracy and completeness.

Step 3: Validate and Upload the Return

Click on the Validate & Upload option to begin the validation process.

Step 4: Resolve Showstopper Issues

Identify any flagged issues highlighted during validation.

      
Click on the flagged sections to update and correct missing or invalid details.
      
Save the changes and proceed to generate the FVU.

By following these steps, you can resolve showstopper errors efficiently and ensure the seamless submission of TDS returns through ExpressTDS.

title: ExpressTDS || Interest Allocation Process
summary: Interest Allocation Process Step 1: Navigate to Deductor Master Access the Deductor Master from the main menu. Select the relevant company or deductor. Go to Return Summary and choose the created return. Step 2: View the Created Return Locate the ...
content:
Interest Allocation Process

Step 1: Navigate to Deductor Master

Access the Deductor Master from the main menu. 

Select the relevant company or deductor. Go to Return Summary and choose the created return.

Step 2: View the Created Return

Locate the View option next to the selected return. Click View to access detailed information about the return.

Step 3: Access Interest Allocation

Find the Interest Allocation option: Within the return view, locate the Interest Allocation feature, which may be found under tools or a specific financial actions menu.

Click on Interest Allocation: Selecting this option will open the interest allocation interface, allowing you to allocate interest either manually or automatically.

      

Step 4: Allocate Interest

Manual Allocation:

Edit interest cells individually: For each transaction, click into the interest cell to manually assign or adjust the interest as required. This method provides full control over the amount allocated to each entry.

Save your manual entries: Ensure each adjustment is saved, either automatically or by clicking a Save button, to confirm the entries.

      
Auto Allocation:

Click on Auto Allocate: To automate interest allocation, select the Auto Allocate option. This will calculate interest based on predefined parameters within the software, such as dates or amounts.

Confirm and proceed: After reviewing the automatically allocated amounts, click Proceed.

Save the allocation: Once confirmed, click Save to finalize the allocation. This step ensures the interest amounts are recorded in the system accurately.

      

      

      
      
Step 5: Review Allocation

Access the review option: If you want to verify or make changes to the allocation, click I Want To Relook. This will open the allocated interest entries for your review.

Make adjustments as needed: Use this opportunity to confirm that each allocation is accurate. If needed, return to the manual or auto allocation steps to make any adjustments.

Finalize the allocation: Once satisfied, close the review to complete the interest allocation process. The software will now use the allocated interest for any relevant calculations or reports.

This structured approach helps ensure accuracy, flexibility, and efficiency in the interest allocation process.

title: ExpressTDS || TDS Certificate Request Raise, Generation and Mail Process (DIY)
summary: TDS Certificate Request Raise, Generation, and Mail Process in ExpressTDS Follow these steps to raise a TDS certificate request, generate it, and send it via email: Step 1: Select Company/Deductor Choose the relevant company or deductor from the ...
content:
TDS Certificate Request Raise, Generation, and Mail Process in ExpressTDS

Follow these steps to raise a TDS certificate request, generate it, and send it via email:

Step 1: Select Company/Deductor

Choose the relevant company or deductor from the available list in ExpressTDS.

Step 2: Access TDS Certificate Option

Navigate to the TDS Certificate option in the software.

      

Step 3: Specify Form Year and Quarter

Select the appropriate Form, Year and Quarter for the certificate.

Click Submit Request to proceed.
      
Step 4: Provide Request Details

Complete the necessary fields to submit the request.

      
Alternatively, you can import the TXT file for the relevant quarter to autofill the details and then click Proceed.

Step 5: Await ZIP File from TRACES
Once the request is successfully submitted, wait for the ZIP file to be generated by TRACES.

To check the request status, use the "View all the Raised Requests" option.
If the request is not available:

Raise a New Request: Navigate to TRACES and select "Raise a New Request".

                  
Update the Status: Sync ExpressTDS with TRACES to refresh the status of previously submitted requests.
                  
Enter the CAPTCHA and click Submit to verify the update.

Step 6: Generate PDF Certificate or Email the Generated PDF

Once the ZIP file is available, you can choose to Generate Unsigned PDF or Generate DSC Signed PDF.

      
EMAIL PDF Process will require a token for DSC (Digital Signature Certificate) signed PDF's.

Step 7: Download or Email the Generated PDFs

After generating the PDFs, you can Download them.

      
Clicking the Download option will provide a ZIP file containing all the generated TDS certificates in PDF format, which you can then save to your system.

Step 8: For Emailing PDFs, Use DSC Generated Signed PDFs

To send the certificate via email, select the DSC Generated Signed PDF option.

      

      

      

      

      

Step 9: Enable Email Option

After selecting the DSC Signed PDF, the Email PDF option will be enabled.

      
You can then add deductee details and their email addresses using an Excel template or manually entering the details.
Step 10: Email the PDFs

Once the deductee details and email addresses are provided, click on the Send E-Mail option.

      

      

      

Step 11: Deductee-wise Email Management

For bulk imports, click on the Export icon.

      
Add deductee emails in bulk (by dragging and dropping the Excel file) and import them into the system.

      

Step 12: Send the Email

After selecting the deductees, click Send.

      

Step 13: TDS Certificates Sent

The selected deductees will receive their TDS certificates via the email addresses entered.

      

Important Tip: Always double-check that the correct email addresses are entered for all deductees to ensure smooth delivery of TDS certificates.

By following these steps, you can efficiently raise a TDS certificate request, generate the certificates, and send them via email using ExpressTDS.

title: ExpressTDS || Procedure for Calculating Interest & Late Fees
tags: procedure for calculating interest and late fees
summary: 1. Late Filing Fee Calculation Statutory Due Dates TDS must be deposited by the 7th day of the following month. For the month of March, the due date for TDS deposit is 30th April. Applicable Penalty A late filing fee of ₹200 per day is levied for ...
content:
1. Late Filing Fee Calculation

Statutory Due Dates

TDS must be deposited by the 7th day of the following month.
For the month of March, the due date for TDS deposit is 30th April.

Applicable Penalty

A late filing fee of ₹200 per day is levied for delays in submitting the TDS return.
The total fee shall not exceed the amount of TDS deductible.

Steps to View Late Filing Fee Calculation

Access the Deductor Master:

Select the relevant company/deductor from the list.

Navigate to the Return Summary section.

Choose the applicable return.

View the Selected Return:

Click on the ‘View’ button for the chosen return.

Open the Computation & Health Section:

Select the ‘Computation & Health’ tab.

                  
View Late Fee Details:

Click on the ‘Late Fee’ option to review the calculation.

                  

2. Interest on Late Deduction

Interest Applicability

If TDS is not deducted on time, interest is charged at 1% per month or part thereof.

This applies for the period from the date TDS was deductible to the actual date of deduction.

Steps to View Late Deduction Interest

Navigate to the ‘Computation & Health’ section.
Click on ‘Late Deduction Interest’ to access the details.

      
Select ‘View’ to see the detailed interest computation.

      

3. Interest on Late Deposit

Interest Applicability

If TDS is deducted but not deposited on time, interest is charged at 1.5% per month or part thereof.

This interest is calculated from the date of deduction to the actual date of payment.

Steps to View Late Deposit Interest

Go to the ‘Computation & Health’ section.

Click on ‘Late Deposit Interest’ to proceed.

      
Select ‘View’ to examine the detailed calculation.

      

By following the steps outlined above, users can efficiently and accurately compute applicable interest and late filing fees using the ExpressTDS application.

title: ExpressTDS || Process to Prepare and File 26Q TDS Return

summary: Process to Prepare and File 26Q TDS Return in Express TDS Step 1: Login and Select Deductor Login to https://www.expresstds.com/ From the Deductor Master list, select the company/deductor for whom the TDS return is being prepared. Step 2: Start ...
content:
Process to Prepare and File 26Q TDS Return in Express TDS

Step 1: Login and Select Deductor

Login to https://www.expresstds.com/
From the Deductor Master list, select the company/deductor for whom the TDS return is being prepared.

      

Step 2: Start Filing

Navigate to the Return Summary section.

Click on the Start Filing option.

      
Select the appropriate Form (26Q), quarter, year and type (Regular or Correction) and click Submit.

      

Step 3: Create Return Process

Click on the View option to begin creating the return.

      

Step 4: Enter Deductee Transactions (Manual)

Click on the deductee/collectee name to manually add transactions for the selected quarter.

Step 5: Import Transactions

Use the Import Return option on the left side menu to download the template and prepare the return data offline.

Upload the filled template via the same Import Return section.

      
For additional guidance, refer to the deductee transaction process guide.

Step 6: Map Challans

Map the challans by one of the following methods:
Manual Mapping: Add challan details directly.

Excel Upload: Import challan data using an Excel file.

Bulk Challan Mapping: Map multiple challans in bulk.

Refer to the challan mapping guide for step-by-step instructions.

Step 7: Verify Return Data

Navigate to the Computation and Health Table sections to verify the accuracy and completeness of the return data.

      
Ensure all discrepancies or errors are resolved before proceeding.

Step 8: Validate and Upload

After thorough review, click on Validate and Upload.

      
Choose one of the following options:

Generate FVU (IT Portal): For returns uploaded directly to the Income Tax Portal.

Generate FVU (OLTAS): For returns uploaded through the OLTAS system.

                  

Step 9: E-File the Return

Click on the E-file option.
You will be redirected to the Income Tax Portal to complete the e-filing process.

For detailed steps on generating and filing FVU, refer to the FVU generation guide.

By following these steps, you can successfully prepare and file the 26Q TDS return.

title: ExpressTDS || How to Delete Deductor or Active/Inactive Deductor
summary: Deductor Deletion Process The Deductor Deletion feature in ExpressTDS allows users to permanently remove a deductor from the system. Once deleted, the deductor is removed from the Dashboard, Deductor Master, and all other sections of the application. ...
content:
Deductor Deletion Process

The Deductor Deletion feature in ExpressTDS allows users to permanently remove a deductor from the system. Once deleted, the deductor is removed from the Dashboard, Deductor Master, and all other sections of the application. This action is irreversible and should be performed with caution.

Process to Delete a Deductor

Step 1: Access the Deductor Profile

Log in to ExpressTDS and navigate to the Deductor List.
Click the Edit option under the Actions column for the deductor you want to delete.

      

Step 2: Perform Deductor Deletion

In the deductor’s profile, locate and click the Delete Deductor button.

      
Click Proceed on the confirmation prompt to authorize the deletion.

      
Upon successful deletion, the system will display the message: “Data Deleted Successfully.”

      

Important Note

Deleting a deductor is a permanent action. The deductor’s data cannot be retrieved or restored once removed. Please verify all details before proceeding with the deletion.

Process for Marking a Deductor as Inactive in ExpressTDS

When a Deductor is marked as Inactive, they are prohibited from engaging in any TDS-related transactions, including tax deductions and filing TDS returns. This serves as a useful alternative to permanently deleting a Deductor's profile.

To change a Deductor's status from Active to Inactive, please follow these steps:

Step 1: Access the ExpressTDS portal by entering your username and password. Upon logging in, the Deductor Master page will display, listing all registered Deductors.

      

Step 2: Identify the Deductor you wish to modify and click the Edit button next to their name.

      

Step 3: On the Deductor profile page, locate the current status indicated as Active. Click the Active toggle button to change this status.

      

Step 4: After toggling the status to Inactive, click the Save button to apply your changes.

Once a Deductor is marked as Inactive, they will be categorized under the All tab in the Deductor Master. This process allows for effective management of Deductor profiles while retaining historical data for reference.

Process for Changing a Deductor's Status from Inactive to Active in ExpressTDS

When a Deductor is marked as Active, they regain the ability to conduct all TDS transactions, including tax deductions, filing TDS returns, issuing TDS certificates, and managing TDS data.

To change a Deductor's status from Inactive to Active, follow these steps:

Step 1: Access the ExpressTDS portal by entering your username and password. Once logged in, you will see the Deductor Master page, listing all registered Deductors.

      

Step 2: Click on the All tab within the Deductor Master to display a list of all Deductors, including those marked as Inactive.

Step 3: Use the search bar to enter either the Deductor's name or their TAN (Tax Deduction and Collection Account Number). Press Enter to locate the specific Inactive Deductor.

      

Step 4: Click the Edit button next to the selected Deductor. This will open the Deductor details page, allowing you to modify their status.

Step 5:  On the Deductor details page, locate the Inactive toggle button. Click this to change the status to Active.

      

Step 6: After toggling the status, click the Save button to apply your changes.

Once the status has been changed, the Deductor will be listed under the Recent tab in the Deductor Master, allowing them to perform all TDS-related activities.

By following these steps, you can successfully update a Deductor's company profile status between Active and Inactive according to your business requirements. This functionality provides flexible management of Deductor profiles without the need for permanent deletion, ensuring that you can retain historical data while adapting to your current operational needs.

title: ExpressTDS || Online activity → Tag Challan on Traces Portal
tags: process for tagging mismatched challans on the traces portal
summary: Process for Tagging Mismatched Challans on the TRACES Portal (Applicable during Correction Return Filing in case of Challan Mismatch Errors) This guide outlines the step-by-step process to correctly tag unmatched challans on the TRACES portal via ...
content:
Process for Tagging Mismatched Challans on the TRACES Portal (Applicable during Correction Return Filing in case of Challan Mismatch Errors)

      

This guide outlines the step-by-step process to correctly tag unmatched challans on the TRACES portal via ExpressTDS.

Step 1: Access the TRACES Portal through ExpressTDS

Log in to the ExpressTDS portal (https://www.expresstds.com/) using your credentials.
Navigate to the Deductor Master page and select the relevant deductor.
Click on the TRACES icon to be redirected to the TRACES login page.

      

Step 2: Initiate a Correction Request

After logging into the TRACES portal, go to the Defaults menu.
Select Request for Correction.
Enter the required Basic Statement Details and submit the request.

Step 3: Track the Correction Request

Navigate to Track Correction Request.
Locate the relevant statement and click on the Available option to proceed.

     

Step 4: Begin the Correction Process

Re-enter the TRACES portal.
Provide the mandatory (*) details and click Proceed.

      
Follow the on-screen instructions and click Proceed with Transaction.

      

Step 5: Access Challan Details

Based on your selected statement, click View Details.

      
If the Unmatched Challans option is available, select it.
Click on Edit Unmatched Challan Details to review the entries.

      

Step 6: Tag the Challan

Review the details and click Tag Challan.

      
Select the applicable challan(s) and click Tag Selected Challan.

      
Verify the challan information carefully before proceeding.

      
Note: Successfully tagged challans will be highlighted in orange.

      

Step 7: Submit the Correction Statement

Click Submit Correction Statement.

      
Confirm the submission by clicking the Confirm button.

      
Select View Statement to review the updated correction statement.

      
The correction statement will now appear under the Correction Ready for Submission section.

      

Step 8: Final Submission

Reopen the correction statement by clicking View Statement.

      
Click Submit for Processing to finalize the submission.

      

Important: This step completes the challan tagging and submission process.

      

Step 9: Track Submission Status

Go to Defaults → Track Correction Request to monitor the status of your submitted correction.

      

By following these steps, users can accurately tag unmatched challans and ensure proper submission of correction returns via the TRACES portal.

content:
TDS Certificate Request Raise and Generation Process

Step 1: Select Company/Deductor 

Begin by selecting the appropriate company or deductor from the available list.

Step 2: Access TDS Certificate Option 

Navigate to and select the TDS Certificate option.

      

Step 3: Specify Form Year and Quarter

Step 4: Provide Request Details

Fill in the required details to submit the request.
Alternatively, you can import a TXT file for the selected quarter to autofill the details.

Click Proceed to continue.

      

Step 5: Await ZIP File from TRACES

Once the request is successfully submitted, wait for the ZIP file to be generated by TRACES.
To check the request status, use the Status Check option.

If the request is not available:
Raise a New Request: Navigate to TRACES and select "Raise a New Request".
                  
Update the Status: Sync ExpressTDS with TRACES to refresh the status of previously submitted requests.
                  
Enter the CAPTCHA and click Submit to verify the update.

Step 6: Generate PDF Certificate

Once the ZIP file is available, choose one of the following options:

Generate Unsigned PDF

Generate DSC-Signed PDF (requires a valid digital signature token)

      

Step 7: Download the Generated PDFs

Once the PDFs are successfully generated, use the Download option.

A single ZIP file containing all PDFs will be available for download and can be saved for record-keeping.

      

      

            

By following these steps, you can efficiently raise, track and download TDS Certificates using ExpressTDS, ensuring compliance and seamless management of TDS records.

This guide outlines the steps for marking deductee transactions as NIL in ExpressTDS, whether handling single or bulk corrections.

Steps for Performing Corrections

Access the Correction Section

Company/Deductor Selection: Navigate to the relevant company or deductor.

For Single Deductee Transaction

Import the file using the Import Return from Excel option.

Always ensure accurate data handling to maintain compliance and avoid errors.

summary: The File Validation Utility (FVU) is a critical tool for validating TDS returns before generating and submitting them to the Income Tax Department. During the validation and upload process, certain errors, known as "showstoppers", may arise. These ...
content:
The File Validation Utility (FVU) is a critical tool for validating TDS returns before generating and submitting them to the Income Tax Department. During the validation and upload process, certain errors, known as "showstoppers", may arise. These errors are critical and must be resolved to ensure successful return generation and submission.

Common Show Stoppers Issues

Mismatch Details: Errors may occur due to discrepancies in deductee transaction details, including mismatched or invalid tax amounts.

      

Steps to Validate and Upload FVU Returns in ExpressTDS

Step 1: Log in and Access the Deductor Master
Visit ExpressTDS Website and log in using your credentials.
Navigate to the Deductor Master section.
Select the relevant company or deductor from the list.

Step 2: Review the Raised Return
Locate the return you have raised and click View to open the details.
Carefully review all fields to ensure accuracy and completeness.

Step 3: Validate and Upload the Return
Click on the Validate & Upload option to begin the validation process.
      

Step 4: Address Showstopper Issues

Review any flagged errors related to mismatched or invalid details.

Click on the highlighted sections to open and address the discrepancies.
      
Use the Action icon to edit the transaction details and correct the mismatches.
      
Save the changes and proceed to generate the FVU.

By following these steps, you can efficiently resolve showstopper errors, such as invalid tax deducted amounts, ensuring smooth submission of TDS returns through ExpressTDS.

content:
27Q FVU Generation Errors – Resolution Guide

To resolve errors encountered during 27Q FVU (File Validation Utility) generation and ensure compliance with Section 206AB of the Income Tax Act, follow the steps below based on your specific scenario.

PAN Not Available (PANNOTAVBL) & Tax Deducted at a Lower Rate

      

If the PAN is unavailable and you are deducting tax at a lower rate, follow these steps before generating the FVU:

Select the Correct PAN Unavailability Reason: In the FVU file, select "Higher Rate - PAN Not Available", even if applying a lower tax rate.
      
Provide Complete Deductee Information:

PAN Reference Number: Enter a 10-digit PAN reference number, even if the PAN is unavailable.

Valid Contact Details: Include a valid email ID and contact number.

Complete Address: Provide the full address of the deductee.

TIN (Taxpayer Identification Number): Ensure the TIN is correctly mentioned.
      

Once the necessary details are entered, proceed with FVU validation.

Step 7: Proceed to Interest Allocation

Click on the Proceed to Interest option to allocate interest and late fee details.

      

Step 8: Submit and File the Correction Statement

After completing all corrections, click on Submit & File Correction Statement to finalize the process.

      

Key Notes:

Ensure that all corrections are accurate before submitting the statement.

Only statements processed by TRACES are eligible for this online correction feature.

By following this structured process, you can efficiently allocate interest and late fees within the TRACES portal.

title: ExpressTDS || Process to Register Deductor on Reporting Portal for 206AB/CCA Verification
tags: income tax department reporting entity identification number
steps for registration on the reporting portal
register deductor on the reporting portal
summary: Process to Register Deductor on the Reporting Portal for 206AB/206CCA Verification The Finance Act of 2021 introduced Sections 206AB and 206CCA into the Income-tax Act, 1961, effective from July 1, 2021. These sections impose higher Tax Deducted at ...
content:
Process to Register Deductor on the Reporting Portal for 206AB/206CCA Verification

The Finance Act of 2021 introduced Sections 206AB and 206CCA into the Income-tax Act, 1961, effective from July 1, 2021. These sections impose higher Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) or Tax Collected at Source (TCS) rates on "Specified Persons" as defined within these provisions.

To assist deductors/collectors in verifying a person’s status under these sections, the Income-tax Department has launched the "Compliance Check for Sections 206AB & 206CCA" functionality. This tool is accessible via the Reporting Portal of the Income-tax Department at https://report.insight.gov.in.

This guide outlines the steps for registering a deductor on the Reporting Portal to use this functionality.

Steps for Registration on the Reporting Portal

Step 1: Log in to ExpressTDS

Visit https://www.expresstds.com/.

Log in using your credentials and navigate to the desired deductor.
Step 2: Select Action for the Deductor

Click on the Action option for the selected deductor.

      

Step 3: Register on the Reporting Portal

In the Edit Deductor section, click on the Register Now option under the Insight section.

      

Step 4: Enter Entity Details

On the Entity Details page, input the relevant information about the entity.

Click on the Add Principal Officer button to register the Principal Officer associated with the TAN.

      

Step 5: Submit the Registration Request

Review the details entered and click on the Submit button.

      
Upon submission, an Acknowledgment Receipt of the registration request will be generated.

This acknowledgment will also be sent to the Principal Officer via email.

Step 6: Approval and ITDREIN Allocation

Once the Income-tax Department approves the registration request:

An email notification will be sent to the Principal Officer containing the ITDREIN (Income Tax Department Reporting Entity Identification Number) and login credentials for the Reporting Portal.

      

Activation of the Principal Officer’s Account

To activate the Principal Officer’s account, follow these steps:

The Principal Officer will receive an email with temporary credentials.

Log in to the Reporting Portal using the temporary credentials.

The system will prompt the Principal Officer to change the temporary password.

The account will become active upon successfully updating the password.

What is ITDREIN?

ITDREIN (Income Tax Department Reporting Entity Identification Number) is a 16-character unique identification number generated by the Income-tax Department upon successful registration of an entity on the Reporting Portal.

By following the steps outlined above, deductors/collectors can successfully register on the Reporting Portal for 206AB/206CCA Compliance Check.

To download the TDS filed return acknowledgment on the Express TDS portal, you can choose one of two options:

Option 1: Download Acknowledgment via Return Register

Step 1: Login to Express TDS: Sign in to your Express TDS account.

Step 2: Go to Return Register: At the top right, select Return Register.

      

Step 3: Select the Return: Choose the return for which you want to download the acknowledgment.

      

Step 4: Download Acknowledgment: Click the PDF icon next to the selected return to download the acknowledgment receipt.

      

Note: If you don't see the data, use the Sync option to refresh.

      

Option 2: Download Acknowledgment via Return Summary

Step 1: Login to Express TDS: Sign in to your Express TDS account.

Step 2: Select Deductor/Company: Choose the deductor or company for which the return was filed.

      

Step 3: Go to Return Summary:

For returns filed in Express TDS: In the Created Return Table, find the return and click the PDF icon to download the acknowledgment.

      
For returns filed outside Express TDS: Go to Other Returns Filed Outside Express TDS and locate the return. Click the PDF icon to download.

      

Note: Use the Sync option if the data isn't visible to update it.

By following these steps, you should be able to quickly download your Income Tax acknowledgment receipt after filing.

title: ExpressTDS || Challan Creation Process
summary: Process for Adding a Challan in Deductor Master Step 1: Log in to the Website Open your web browser and go to www.expresstds.com. Enter your username and password, then click Login to access your account. Step 2: Access the Deductor Master After ...
content:
Process for Adding a Challan in Deductor Master

Step 1: Log in to the Website

Open your web browser and go to www.expresstds.com.

Enter your username and password, then click Login to access your account.

Step 2: Access the Deductor Master

After logging in, navigate to the dashboard and click on the Deductor Master tab.

Select the relevant company or deductor from the list provided.

      
Step 3: View Created Returns

In the Returns Created section, find the list of returns that have already been generated.

Click the View button next to the return for which you want to add a challan.

      
Step 4: Add a New Challan

Within the selected return, go to the All Challans section.

Click the Add icon or button to start the process of adding a new challan.

      

Note: After syncing in the "All Challans" section, check the already generated challans in the software or those paid manually on the portal.

Step 5: Enter Challan Details

A form will be displayed. Enter the following required details: Challan Number, Date of Payment, Amount Paid, Any other (mandatory fields as required).

Once the details are filled in, select the option I will deposit now to proceed with payment.

      
Step 6: Proceed to Payment

After verifying the entered details, click on the Pay Now button.

You will be redirected to the official Income Tax e-Payment portal.

Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the payment.
Once the payment is completed, the challan will appear in the Default or All Challans section.

By following these steps, you can successfully generate challans in the software.

title: ExpressTDS || Raise Request, Download and Generate Justification Reports
summary: Steps to Raise Request, Download, and Generate Justification Reports in Express TDS In Express TDS, you can manage Justification Reports by raising requests, downloading, and generating them using the following methods: Raising a Request for ...
content:
Steps to Raise Request, Download, and Generate Justification Reports in Express TDS

In Express TDS, you can manage Justification Reports by raising requests, downloading, and generating them using the following methods:

Raising a Request for Justification Report

You can raise a request for Justification in two ways:

Process 1: Request from Deductor Dashboard

Open the Deductor Dashboard.

To raise a Justification request, refer to this guide for detailed steps - Click Here.
Process 2: Request from Notices & Orders

Go to Notices & Orders from the main screen.

When you synchronize the software with TRACES, any notices available will show in this section with a Raise Request option.

For detailed guidance, refer to this article - Click Here.

Downloading the Justification Report

Once the request is raised, follow these steps to download it:

Wait for Processing: Once the request is raised, the status will show as "Submitted", Wait for 2 to 3 hours for the request to process.

      
Update Status from TRACES: After the waiting period, go back to the Request Raised to TRACES screen and click Update Status from TRACES. The status should change to "Available".

      
Download the File: Once available, click on the download option to retrieve the ZIP file. This file can then be accessed from the Notices & Orders section.

Generating the Justification Report

There are two ways to generate the Justification Report:

Process 1: Generate via Excel
Go to Notices & Orders and locate the updated request in the Request Raised to TRACES section.

Click on the Excel option to download the file.

            
Open the downloaded Excel file to view the department’s summary, organized sheet-wise.

      
Process 2: Generate via Software (View)
In Notices & Orders, locate the updated request and click on the View option next to Excel.

      
The Justification Report will open directly within the software for easy viewing.

      

By following these steps, you can efficiently raise, download, and generate Justification Reports in Express TDS.

content:
Issue Description:

In certain scenarios, users may experience difficulties where specific options, such as Validate and Upload, are not displayed. This issue is typically caused by improper screen resolution settings.

Resolution Steps:

1. Adjust System Screen Resolution:

Right-click on the desktop and select Display Settings.
      
Navigate to the Display Resolution section.

Set the resolution to 100% or the recommended setting.
      
After making the adjustment, restore the resolution to its default 100% setting.

Verify that all options, are now visible.

2. Modify Browser Display Settings (If Necessary):

If the issue persists, adjust the screen resolution settings within the specific browser where the cloud software is being accessed.

Navigate to the Settings menu of the browser and modify the resolution accordingly.
      
By following these steps, users should be able to resolve visibility issues and restore full functionality.

To ensure seamless TDS certificate generation, the department’s utility requires a compatible version of Java to be installed on the system.

Error - Something went Wrong! Please try again after some time.

Minimum Java Requirement:

Java 8 or above → To check the installed version, navigate to Control Panel → Programs and Features.

Troubleshooting Java Errors

Step 1. Verify Java and Express Connector Installation

Open Control Panel → Programs and Features

Confirm that Java and the Express Connector are installed.

Step 2. Automatic Java Installation

Run the Express Connector as Administrator to allow automatic Java installation.

Restart the system after installation.

Step 3. Manual Java Installation (If the Issue Persists)

If required, download and install Java 8 Update 251 (32-bit version)

Restart the system and proceed with TDS certificate generation

By following these steps, Java-related issues should be resolved, ensuring smooth processing of TDS certificate generation.

title: ExpressTDS || Clause 34-Form 3CD Annex (DIY)
summary: Process to Generate Clause 34-Form 3CD Annex Report in ExpressTDS In Express TDS, Clause 34 of Form 3CD relates to the compliance reporting of Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) for audit purposes under the Income Tax Act of India. This clause requires ...
content:
Process to Generate Clause 34-Form 3CD Annex Report in ExpressTDS

In Express TDS, Clause 34 of Form 3CD relates to the compliance reporting of Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) for audit purposes under the Income Tax Act of India. This clause requires detailed information on TDS compliance as part of the tax audit report (Form 3CD) filed by taxpayers whose accounts are audited under Section 44AB.

 

Clause 34(a) asks whether the taxpayer is required to deduct or collect tax under the provisions of Chapter XVII-B (TDS) or Chapter XVII-BB (TCS) of the Income Tax Act. If applicable, it requires reporting details such as the Tax Deduction and Collection Account Number (TAN), the total amount deducted, the amount deposited, and whether the TDS/TCS was deposited on time.

Clause 34(b) focuses on cases where the taxpayer is liable to deduct or collect tax but has failed to do so. It requires reporting any shortfall in TDS/TCS along with applicable interest and penalties.

Clause 34(c) asks whether any payment made to a non-resident is liable for withholding tax under Section 195. If so, it requires reporting details of such payments, the tax deducted, and remittance details.

Step 1: Log In to ExpressTDS

Visit the ExpressTDS portal and log in using your username and password.

Navigate to the Deductor Master page, where all the deductors are listed.

Step 2: Access Clause 34

Click on the Clause 34 Form 3CD Annex option to open the Clause 34 section.

      
In the TAN Wise PAN Info tab, use the search bar to enter the PAN of the deductor.

      
Press Enter to fetch the deductor details.

Step 3: Sync Deductor Data

Ensure that details such as TAN, deductor name, ITD password, TRACES username and password are correctly reflected.

      
Click on the Sync button to fetch data from TRACES:
A captcha window will appear. Enter the captcha code and click Proceed.

      

            
The sync process will automatically fetch TDS-related data, including:
TDS deducted, deposited, and whether it was done on time or late.

This data will populate Clause 34(a), Clause 34(b), and Clause 34(c).

Step 4: Verify Data in Clause 34

To confirm the fetched data:

Click on any Clause, e.g., Clause 34(b).

Verify the displayed details for accuracy.

      

Step 5: Handle Missing Data

If the Sync process does not fetch deductor data:

Navigate to the TAN Form – Quarter Summary tab.

Select the Raise Manually option to request data for a specific form type and quarter from TRACES.

      
Once the request is processed, the Clause 34 data will be automatically fetched and reflected.

Step 6: Download Clause 34 Data

After verifying or updating data in Clause 34, click the Download icon to save the report.

Ensure the data is accurate and matches the compliance requirements for the audit.

To raise a request for a particular form type and quarter, click Raise Manually.

Note: When you Raise Manually a request to fetch deductor data from Traces, on submitting the request it will automatically fetch the Clause 34 data from Traces and will reflect the deductor information in Clause 34(a), Clause 34 (b), Clause 34 (c).

To know how to raise request manually , see Raise Request in Express TDS.

After raising the request at Traces, to check if the deductor data is shown in Clause 34, click on any of the Clause from Clause 34 (a), Clause 34 (b), Clause 34(c). For Example – Clause 34(b).

This opens up the following window.

      

Click on Download icon to get data  icon provided.
      

​

By following this detailed process, you can efficiently generate, sync, and verify Clause 34 (Form 3CD) data using ExpressTDS for audit purposes.

Procedure to Identify and Address Rate-wise or Date-wise Discrepancies in Deductee Transactions

Ensuring accurate information for return filing is critical to avoid complications such as audits, penalties, or future defaults. Adopting a structured approach to identifying and resolving discrepancies can significantly reduce errors and ensure compliance. Follow the steps below:

Step 1: Access the Deductor Master

Navigate to the Deductor Master section.

Select the relevant company or deductor.

Open the Return Summary and choose the corresponding created return.

      

Step 2: View the Created Return

Click the "View" option associated with the selected return to access detailed information.

      

Step 3: Access the Conflict Icon

Locate the Conflict Icon at the bottom-right corner of the screen and click on it.

      

Step 4: Review Discrepancy Details

Examine the discrepancies displayed:Rate-wise Discrepancies: Review and resolve mismatches related to deduction rates.

      
Date-wise Discrepancies: Review and resolve mismatches related to transaction dates.

      

By systematically reviewing and addressing these discrepancies, you can ensure the accuracy and reliability of the data used in return filing, minimizing the likelihood of errors and future complications.

title: ExpressTDS || Process to Prepare and File 27Q TDS Return
tags: process to prepare and file 27q tds return
summary: Form 27Q is the statement of the return for the Non-Salary Payments Done to an NRI. The Indian buyer is required to submit the form 27Q every quarter before the due date. Non-resident Indians who do not hold a PAN card are required to pay TDS at a ...
content:
Form 27Q is the statement of the return for the Non-Salary Payments Done to an NRI. The Indian buyer is required to submit the form 27Q every quarter before the due date. 

Non-resident Indians who do not hold a PAN card are required to pay TDS at a higher rate of 20% or,

For formal TDS rate applicability required to mention deductee's address, E Mail, Contact number and TIN number.

Step 1: Login and Select Deductor
Login to https://www.expresstds.com/
From the Deductor Master list, select the company/deductor for whom the TDS return is being prepared.
      

Step 2: Start Filing
Navigate to the Return Summary section.
Click on the Start Filing option.
      
Select the appropriate Form (27Q), quarter, year and type (Regular or Correction) and click Submit.
            

Step 3: Create Return Process
Click on the View option to begin creating the return.
            

Step 4: Enter Deductee Transactions (Manual)
Click on the deductee/collectee name to manually add transactions for the selected quarter.
Step 5: Import Transactions
Use the Import Return option on the left side menu to download the template and prepare the return data offline.
Upload the filled template via the same Import Return section.
      
For additional guidance, refer to the deductee transaction process guide.
Step 6: Map Challans
Map the challans by one of the following methods:
Manual Mapping: Add challan details directly.
Excel Upload: Import challan data using an Excel file.
Bulk Challan Mapping: Map multiple challans in bulk.
Refer to the challan mapping guide for step-by-step instructions.

Step 7: Verify Return Data
Navigate to the Computation and Health Table sections to verify the accuracy and completeness of the return data.
      

Ensure all discrepancies or errors are resolved before proceeding.
Step 8: Validate and Upload
After thorough review, click on Validate and Upload.
      

Choose one of the following options:
Generate FVU (IT Portal): For returns uploaded directly to the Income Tax Portal.
Generate FVU (OLTAS): For returns uploaded through the OLTAS system.
            

Step 9: E-File the Return

Click on the E-file option.
You will be redirected to the Income Tax Portal to complete the e-filing process.
      

For detailed steps on generating and filing FVU, refer to the FVU generation guide.

By following these steps, you can successfully prepare and file the 27Q TDS return.

title: ExpressTDS || Process to Request, Download and Email Form 16 with Form 12BA (DIY)
tags: process to request download and email form 16 with form 12ba
summary: Process to Request, Download, and Email Form 16 with Form 12BA Form 12BA is a critical document for employees receiving perquisites or benefits from their employers. It complements Form 16 by providing a detailed breakdown of income, tax deductions, ...
content:
Process to Request, Download, and Email Form 16 with Form 12BA

Form 12BA is a critical document for employees receiving perquisites or benefits from their employers. It complements Form 16 by providing a detailed breakdown of income, tax deductions, and the valuation of perquisites or benefits, ensuring transparency and accuracy in tax compliance.

The ExpressTDS Application supports Form 12BA generation starting from the financial year 2024–25 onwards. To generate and distribute this document, follow the steps outlined below:

Step-by-Step Process

Step 1: Select Company/Deductor

Log in to the ExpressTDS Application using your credentials.

From the available list, select the company or deductor for whom the Form 12BA is to be generated.

Step 2: Access the TDS Certificate Option

Navigate to the TDS Certificate section within the software.
      

Step 3: Specify Form Year and Quarter

Choose the appropriate form type, financial year and quarter for the certificate.
      
Click on Submit Request to initiate the process.
Step 4: Await ZIP File from TRACES

Upon submission, wait for the ZIP file to be generated by TRACES.

Use the Status Check option to track the progress of your request.

Step 5: Generate the PDF Certificate
Once the ZIP file is available, proceed to generate the required certificates.

Choose one of the following options:

Generate Unsigned PDF

Generate DSC (Digital Signature Certificate) Signed PDF

Step 6: Download the Generated PDFs

Use the Download option to retrieve a ZIP file containing all TDS certificates in PDF format.
Save the ZIP file securely to your system for future reference.

Step 7: Email the Generated PDFs

To email the certificates along with Form 12BA, select the DSC Signed PDF option.
      
This ensures that the documents are digitally signed and ready for secure electronic distribution.

Note: ✅ From ExpressTDS, you can email Bulk Form 16 with Form 12BA in a single click.

Form 12BA is downloaded in PDF format, and it includes a detailed breakdown of all perquisites received by the employee. The form provides transparency in taxable benefits and ensures compliance with income tax regulations.

      

By following these steps, you can efficiently generate, download, and share Form 16 along with Form 12BA, ensuring compliance with tax regulations while maintaining data integrity and security.

title: Password Incorrect / Reset Password / Forgot Password
summary: If you see the error message "Password is incorrect, try again" while logging into applications like ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS or any similar application, it typically means that the password entered is incorrect. Solution → Double-check the ...
content:
If you see the error message "Password is incorrect, try again" while logging into applications like ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS or any similar application, it typically means that the password entered is incorrect.

      

Solution →
Double-check the password you entered. If it was entered incorrectly, simply re-enter the correct password and try logging in again.

If you continue to see the error message "Password is incorrect, try again," you will need to reset your password.

To Reset Your Password, Please follow these steps:

Step 1: Click on the "Forgot Password?" option.

      

Step 2: Enter your Registered mobile number. You will receive an OTP on this number for verification.

      

Step 3: Enter the OTP.

      
      
Step 4: Create a new password, confirm it and click on Update Password.

      

You can now log in with your updated password.

title: Sign-Up and Sign-In Process – SPECTRUM CLOUD
tags: sign-up and sign-in process – spectrum cloud
summary: This article provides a step-by-step guide to help you create an account and log in to the SPECTRUM CLOUD platform by KDK Software. SPECTRUM CLOUD enables tax professionals to manage GST, Income Tax (ITR), and TDS compliance for all their clients ...
content:
This article provides a step-by-step guide to help you create an account and log in to the SPECTRUM CLOUD platform by KDK Software.

SPECTRUM CLOUD enables tax professionals to manage GST, Income Tax (ITR), and TDS compliance for all their clients from a single, secure cloud-based platform.

Note: If you are signing up as a business entity (e.g., SME, Corporate, Enterprise), please refer to the Business Users section below for the appropriate platforms.

🎯 Intended Users : The SPECTRUM CLOUD portal is designed for:

Chartered Accountants (CAs)
Accountants
Tax Practitioners
GST Practitioners (GSTPs)

📝 Step 1: Account Registration (Sign-Up)

To access the platform, you must first register an account.

Navigate to the Sign-Up page: https://app.kdksoftware.com/signup

Complete the registration form with the following:
Full Name
Email Address
Mobile Number
Other required details

Click "SIGN UP" to submit the form.

You will receive a One-Time Password (OTP) on your registered mobile number.

Enter the OTP in the designated field to verify your account.

🔐 Step 2: User Login (Sign-In)

Once your account is verified, follow the steps below to log in.

Go to the Login page: https://app.kdksoftware.com/login

Enter your registered mobile number and password.
Click "Login" to access your dashboard.

🏢 For Business Users (SMEs, Corporates, Enterprises)

If you are representing a Small or Medium Enterprise (SME), Corporate, or Enterprise, please use the appropriate platform designed specifically for business entities. These platforms provide tailored solutions to meet business compliance and filing requirements:

🔗 Recommended Platforms for Business Users:

ExpressGST – For GST filing and compliance 👉 https://www.expressgst.com/

ExpressITR – For Income Tax Return (ITR) filing 👉 https://www.expressitr.com/

ExpressTDS – For TDS return filing and related compliance 👉 https://www.expresstds.com/

Note: These platforms are optimized for organizations and business entities rather than individual professionals such as CAs or tax consultants.

⚠️ Important Guidelines

Password Confidentiality: Use a secure password and do not share your login credentials.
Consistent Credentials Across Products: If you use multiple KDK Software products with the same mobile number, please ensure you use the same password across platforms to maintain account consistency.

📞 Need Assistance?

If you experience any issues during registration or login, please contact our customer support team.

Important Notes:

Password Security: Ensure your password is strong and remains confidential to protect your account.

Account Consistency: If you are using multiple products from the KDK Software and the same mobile number is registered, please use the same password across all platforms for a seamless experience.

We hope this guide helps you!

title: Experience Unified Power with SPECTRUM CLOUD Enterprise
tags: experience unified power with spectrum cloud enterprise
summary: To enhance operational efficiency, simplify user management, and deliver a more integrated cloud experience, KDK Software has successfully consolidated its individual cloud applications into a single unified platform — Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise. With ...
content:
To enhance operational efficiency, simplify user management, and deliver a more integrated cloud experience, KDK Software has successfully consolidated its individual cloud applications into a single unified platform — Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise.

With this transformation, applications such as ExpressGST, ExpressTDS, and ExpressITR are now centrally accessible through the Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise ecosystem, enabling organizations to manage all compliance activities from one unified platform.

This upgrade is designed to provide improved accessibility, centralized subscription management, enhanced security, and a seamless user experience across all KDK cloud services.

      

Key Platform Enhancements & Internal Updates

1. Unified Subscription Management

Previously, subscriptions for ExpressGST, ExpressTDS, and ExpressITR were managed independently. As part of the new architecture enhancement, all existing standalone subscriptions have now been automatically consolidated into a single Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise subscription.

Benefits:

Centralized subscription management

Simplified license administration

Unified renewal and access control

Improved operational efficiency for corporate users

This consolidation eliminates the need to manage multiple product subscriptions separately and ensures a streamlined experience across all integrated modules.

2. Centralized Dashboard with Feature Continuity

All existing configurations, operational limits, and user-level permissions from the individual applications have been securely migrated and preserved within the new unified dashboard.

The migration process ensures complete continuity of existing services without impacting current workflows.

Preserved Components Include:

Existing usage limits

Return filing quotas

User access rights

Role-based permissions

Client data and configurations

Operational workflows and settings

Users can continue their activities seamlessly within the new platform environment without requiring any manual reconfiguration.

3. Unified Authentication & Access Management

To simplify user access and improve security management, a centralized authentication framework has been implemented across the Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise platform.

A single Sign-In and Sign-Up interface is now available for all integrated cloud applications, enabling users to access the entire ecosystem through one unified authentication process.

Key Advantages:

Single-point login access

Simplified user onboarding

Improved credential management

Enhanced security and authentication control

Consistent user experience across applications

This unified approach minimizes login complexity and improves overall accessibility for corporate users and administrators.

4. Advanced Customer Profiling & Personalization

As part of the enhanced onboarding process, the platform now captures structured customer profiling information during user registration and sign-up activities.

This information enables the system to deliver a more personalized and optimized platform experience based on the customer’s business profile, operational requirements, and service usage patterns.

Objectives of Customer Profiling:

Personalized dashboard experience

Improved service recommendations

Better customer engagement

Optimized support and communication

Enhanced product customization capabilities

The profiling framework helps ensure that users receive more relevant features, workflows, and support experiences aligned with their business needs.

Conclusion

The transition to Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise marks a significant advancement in KDK Software’s cloud ecosystem strategy. By unifying multiple applications into a centralized enterprise platform, organizations can now benefit from:

Simplified subscription and access management

Centralized operational control

Seamless user experience

Improved scalability and security

Enhanced personalization and service delivery

This transformation ensures a more efficient, secure, and future-ready compliance management ecosystem for all corporate users.

title: ExpressTDS || Process to Fill Salary Details (Annexure-II)
tags: process to fill salary details - annexure-ii in diy plan
process to fill salary details - annexure-ii
process to fill salary details - annexure-ii in diy
summary: Salary details are mandatory for e-filing the Fourth Quarter returns for all employees. You can enter salary details under Salary Details (Annexure-II). Steps to Add Salary Details: Step 1: Navigate to the Deductor Master and select the relevant ...
content:
Salary details are mandatory for e-filing the Fourth Quarter returns for all employees.

You can enter salary details under Salary Details (Annexure-II).

Steps to Add Salary Details:

Step 1: Navigate to the Deductor Master and select the relevant company or Deductor. Then go to the Return Summary and select the created return.
Step 2: Click the "View" option for the created return.

      

Step 3: Click on the Salary Details (Annex-II) option.

      

Step 4: Add Salary Details → You can add salary details using one of two methods: Manual Addition or Bulk Import.

Manual Addition:

Click on the "Add" icon.

      
A salary creation table will appear.

Enter the transaction details, including the employee's name and PAN.

Enter the complete details.

      

      
Press "ENTER" to create the transaction.

Bulk Import:

Click on the Import Salary Details option and download the Excel template.

      

      

Enter the details as mentioned in the shared Note.

Save the Excel file and upload it by clicking on Import Salary Details.

      
If any errors occur during the import process, download the error log for corrections.

Notes:

This process helps ensure salary details are accurately entered in Annexure-II.

You can generate salary computations for each deductee as a PDF or Excel file.

To download in PDF format: Click on the View option for the created salary details, then click on Download.

      

      
      
To download in Excel format: Navigate to Employee Trans. (Annex-I) and click on the Download Return option to get Annexure II in the Excel template.

      

This process ensures accurate entry of salary details in Annexure II and allows for computation of deductee-wise salary details in PDF or Excel format.

title: ExpressTDS || Valid Count of Challan/Transfer voucher Records Related Error
tags: valid count of challan transfer voucher records must be provided
valid count of challan transfer voucher records
summary: Valid Count of Challan/Transfer Voucher Records Related Error Root Cause of the Error → This issue arises when challans are not mapped with the deductee transaction entries. Resolving the Error → To address the "Valid Count of Challan/Transfer ...
content:
Valid Count of Challan/Transfer Voucher Records Related Error

      

Root Cause of the Error → This issue arises when challans are not mapped with the deductee transaction entries.

Resolving the Error → To address the "Valid Count of Challan/Transfer Voucher Records" error and map challans with deductee transactions in the File Validation Utility (FVU), follow the steps below:

Steps for Bulk Mapping

Step 1: Navigate to the Mapping Section

Open the FVU tool and go to the Mapping section.

Step 2: Select All Records

Select all the relevant records that need to be mapped.

Step 3: Drag and Drop

Drag and drop the selected records onto the corresponding challan records.

This will automatically map the challans with the deductee transactions.

      

Steps for Manual Mapping

Step 1: Open Deductee Transactions

Navigate to the section listing deductee transactions.

Step 2: Manually Map Each Deductee

For each deductee transaction, manually select and map the relevant challan.

Ensure accuracy when linking deductees to their respective challan records.

      

For further instructions, refer to the Challan Mapping Guide.

Key Points to Remember

Accurate Mapping: Ensure every challan is correctly linked to the relevant deductee records to prevent errors.

Error Prevention: Unmapped records will result in FVU errors related to the "Valid Count of Challan/Transfer Voucher Records."

Bulk Operations: Utilize the drag-and-drop functionality for bulk mapping to streamline the process.

By following these steps, you can efficiently resolve the error and successfully generate the FVU file.

title: ExpressTDS || Correction Return Filing → Invalid previous RRR number
summary: Correction Return Filing: Steps to Avoid 'Invalid Previous RRR Number' Step 1: Prepare to Upload Correction Return Follow these steps to upload the FVU file without encountering errors related to the Previous RRR Number. Step 2: Locate the Return to ...
content:
Correction Return Filing: Steps to Avoid 'Invalid Previous RRR Number'

Step 1: Prepare to Upload Correction Return

Follow these steps to upload the FVU file without encountering errors related to the Previous RRR Number.

      

Step 2: Locate the Return to Be Corrected

Identify the original return requiring corrections.

Click on the View option to open the return details.

      

Step 3: Start the Correction Process

Click the Start Correction button.

      
The software will display the original return data.

Make the necessary corrections, such as updating:

PAN details

Challan details

Deductee details

Save the changes after completing the corrections.

Step 4: Validate the Correction Return

Select the Validate & Upload option to validate the corrected return data.

Step 5: Generate FVU for the Correction Return

After validation, choose one of the following based on your filing method:

Generate FVU (IT Portal): For filing through the Income Tax portal.

Generate FVU (OLTAS): For filing through OLTAS.

      
The software will generate the File Validation Utility (FVU) file for the corrected return.

Step 6: E-File the Correction Return

Click the E-file button.

      
You will be redirected to either:

TRACES portal

Income Tax portal

Log in to the portal and upload the FVU file.

Note: The Original RRR Number and Previous RRR Number fields are automatically filled during this step to ensure no errors occur during submission.

      

Additional Tips for Handling the FVU File

Locate the FVU File Folder

Open the folder where the FVU file was saved.

This folder typically contains the TXT file of the return.

Verify RRR Numbers in the TXT File

Open the TXT file using a text editor (e.g., Notepad or Notepad++).

      
Locate the lines containing:

Original RRR Number

Previous RRR Number

      
Confirm the correctness of these numbers:

The RRR (Receipt Reference Number) is unique for each tax filing.

This structured process ensures the correction return filing is seamless and errors related to RRR numbers are avoided.
