title: TRACES Websocket Esigner error
summary: Error in establishing connection with TRACES Websocket Esigner. Please ensure that WebSigner Setup is installed and services is running on your machine and there are no proxies enabled on the browser while doing DSC activities. Root Cause of the ...
content:
Error in establishing connection with TRACES Websocket Esigner. Please ensure that WebSigner Setup is installed and services is running on your machine and there are no proxies enabled on the browser while doing DSC activities.

            

Root Cause of the error →

The ESigner Setup may not be installed properly or the service may not be running on your system.

There might be proxies enabled on your browser during DSC activities, which can interrupt the connection.

The FVU file (File Validation Utility), em-Signer Utility or the PDF converter utilities are basically Java-based utilities provided by the Traces (TDS) department. The utility of the department would respond only if there is required JAVA installed on the system.

The minimum required version of JAVA should be JAVA 8 or above (32-Bit Only). 

(Check the version from Control Panel → Program and Feature → to get help in knowing the installed JAVA on the system)

            

If there are multiple JAVA's installed on the system, then the command line "Java -Version" will help to know the java running on the system.

      

Steps to Resolve:

Step 1: Verify the Java Version

Ensure you have Java 8 or above (32-Bit Only) installed on your system as required by the TRACES department.

Step 2: Close the Browser and TRACES em-Signer

Before proceeding with any changes, close the browser and the em-Signer application.

Step 3: Download and Install Latest em-Signer

Log in to the TRACES website.

Go to the Downloads section and download the latest version of em-Signer.

Install the downloaded em-Signer.

Step 4: Add Sites to Java Security Exception List

To ensure the Java application can connect to the necessary sites, add the following URLs to the Java security exception list:
https://downloads.tdscpc.gov.in/applet/docwebsigner4.6.jar

https://tdscpc.gov.in/app/ded/kyc3formdsc.xhtml

https://www.tdscpc.gov.in/

To add these sites:

Open Control Panel > Java (32-Bit).

                  
In the Java Control Panel, go to the Security tab and click on Edit Site List.

                  
Click the Add button and add the above URLs one by one.

                  

                  
Click OK and then OK again.

Step 5: Run em-Signer as Administrator

Run the em-Signer application as Administrator and proceed with the DSC activities again.

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the connection error and continue with your DSC activities.

title: Spectrum-CLOUD Mobile Application
summary: The Spectrum CLOUD Mobile Application enables users to securely access Spectrum Cloud data directly from their mobile devices. The application provides seamless access to GST, TDS, and ITR-related information, allowing users to monitor critical ...
content:
The Spectrum CLOUD Mobile Application enables users to securely access Spectrum Cloud data directly from their mobile devices. The application provides seamless access to GST, TDS, and ITR-related information, allowing users to monitor critical compliance activities anytime and anywhere through a secure, efficient, and user-friendly interface.

Key Features & Enhancements

1. Mobile – ITR Notices

Users can now access Income Tax Return (ITR) notices directly through the mobile application.

Features

View notice summaries

Access detailed notice information

Financial Year-wise notice mapping for easy reference
2. Mobile – TDS Notices

The application now supports mobile access to TDS-related notices, enabling users to manage and monitor notices more efficiently.

Features

View detailed notice information

Track notice status in real time

Identify the applicable section under which the notice has been issued

Integrated workflow support with existing TDS processes
3. Mobile Utilities

GSTIN Search → A quick and efficient utility for verifying GSTIN details.
Available Information

Legal Name

Registration Status

State Jurisdiction
TDS Rate Finder → Simplifies the process of identifying applicable TDS rates.
Benefits

Section-wise TDS rate identification

Helps minimize manual errors during deduction processing
Find HSN Code → Enables users to search and identify the appropriate HSN code through keyword-based lookup functionality.

IT New Session → Allows users to initiate a new Income Tax session directly from the mobile application with improved performance and seamless session initialization.

The application is designed to provide fast, secure, and convenient access to essential compliance data through a simplified mobile interface.

Accessing the Application

Downloading the Application →  To download the application:

Open the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.

Search for KDK Software.

Download and install the application.
Sign-In Process → Existing Spectrum Cloud Web Application users can log in using their registered mobile number.
Steps to Sign In

Enter the registered mobile number.

An OTP (One-Time Password) will be sent to the registered mobile number.

Enter the OTP to complete authentication and securely access the application.
Client Master → The Client Master section displays all companies configured within the Spectrum Cloud Web Application.
Companies are automatically categorized under the following modules:

GST

TDS

ITR
Accessing Company Information → To access company-related information:

Select the required company from the list.

Choose the appropriate module to view the relevant details.
GST Module → The GST Module provides comprehensive access to GST-related information for the selected company.
Available Sections

Notices

Ledgers

Returns
Notices → The Notices section enables users to view GST notices and orders received for the selected company.
Viewing Notice Details

Click the View (Eye) icon corresponding to the notice.

The application will display detailed information related to the selected notice or order.

Available Notice Information

ARN Number

Application / Case Creation Date

Notice Sub-Type

Status

Order Description

Order Number

Issued Date

Due Date

Demand Amount

Reply Details

This functionality helps users efficiently monitor notices and track compliance activities.

Ledgers → The Ledgers section provides access to various GST ledgers associated with the selected company.
Users can:

View ledger details directly within the application

Download ledger reports in Excel format for detailed analysis and record-keeping

Available Ledgers

Cash Ledger → Displays tax head-wise balances along with the total available balance in the Cash Ledger.
RCM Liability / ITC Statement → Provides details of Reverse Charge Mechanism (RCM) liabilities and Input Tax Credit (ITC) balances.
Negative Liability Statement → Displays details related to negative liability adjustments along with tax head-wise information.
Electronic Credit Reversal → Provides information regarding credit reversals recorded in the electronic credit ledger.
Credit Ledger → Displays Input Tax Credit balances with tax head-wise breakup.
Non-Return Liability Ledger → Shows liabilities arising due to non-filing of returns, including detailed tax head-wise balances.

Ledger Options → Each ledger provides the following options:

View (Eye) Icon – Access detailed ledger transactions

Date Range Filter – Review transactions for a selected period
Returns

The Returns section allows users to monitor the filing status of GST returns for the selected company.

Return Categories

Pending Returns

Overdue Returns

Filed Returns
Financial Year Selection → Users can select the applicable Financial Year (FY) to view return details for the respective period.

Return Information Views

The application provides two convenient viewing modes for return information.

Month View → The Month View enables users to:

Select a specific month

View return details for the selected month

Download or share return information
Return View → The Return View enables users to:

Select a specific return type

View filing status month-wise

Check return filing dates

Available GST Returns → The following GST returns can be viewed through the mobile application:

GSTR-1

GSTR-1A

GSTR-3B

GSTR-9

GSTR-9C

The KDK Software Mobile Application is available for download on both the Google Play Store and the Apple App Store, enabling users to access Spectrum CLOUD services seamlessly across Android and iOS platforms.

title: Express Connector
tags: enable local area access in google chrome
express connector – installation and troubleshooting guide
summary: Express Connector – Installation & Troubleshooting Guide This document provides step-by-step instructions to install, verify, and troubleshoot the Express Connector to ensure uninterrupted application functionality. Overview: If you receive an error ...
content:
Express Connector – Installation & Troubleshooting Guide

This document provides step-by-step instructions to install, verify, and troubleshoot the Express Connector to ensure uninterrupted application functionality.

Overview: If you receive an error message related to the Express Connector, it typically indicates one of the following:

The connector is not installed.
The installed version is outdated.
The connector service is not running.
Browser security settings are restricting required permissions.

      
      

Please follow the instructions below to resolve the issue.

Step 1: Download the Express Connector

Log in to the application.
Download the latest available version of the Express Connector.

      
            

Step 2: Install the Express Connector

Open the Downloads folder on your system.
Locate the downloaded installer file.
Double-click the file to initiate the installation process.
Upon successful installation, the following confirmation message will appear: 
“The installation has completed successfully! Express Connector is ready to use.”

Click OK to close the installer.
Step 3: Verify Connector Status via Task Manager

After installation, please confirm that the connector service is running:
Open Task Manager (Ctrl + Shift + Esc).
Go to the Details tab.
Verify that ExpressConnector2.exe is listed and running.

If the process is not visible:
Restart your system.

Reopen Task Manager and verify again.
If the process still does not appear, reinstall the connector.

Step 4: Enable Required Browser Permissions (Google Chrome / Microsoft Edge)

When accessing the application via Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge, certain browser permissions must be enabled.

Recommended Method: Using Site Settings
Open the application in your browser.
Click the “View Site Information” icon located next to the address bar.
Locate and enable the following permissions:

Local Network Access

Apps on Device

Refresh the page or restart the browser to apply the changes.

Important: If these permissions are disabled, the application may not communicate properly with the Express Connector.

Conclusion
To ensure proper functionality of the application:

Install the latest version of the Express Connector.
Confirm that the connector service is actively running.
Enable the required browser permissions.

If the issue persists after completing all steps outlined above, please contact the Support Team and provide relevant screenshots or error messages for further assistance.

title: Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for Enhanced Security
tags: enable two-factor authentication for enhanced security
enhance your account security with two-factor authentication
summary: To ensure the highest level of security for your account, we strongly recommend enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) across all our applications, including ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS, and ExpressReco. With 2FA enabled, a single One-Time ...
content:
To ensure the highest level of security for your account, we strongly recommend enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) across all our applications, including ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS, and ExpressReco.

With 2FA enabled, a single One-Time Password (OTP) generated through the Google Authenticator app will be used for authentication across all these applications, ensuring seamless yet secure access.
​

Why Enable 2FA?

2FA provides an additional layer of protection by requiring a One-Time Password (OTP) generated via the Google Authenticator app. This extra security measure helps prevent unauthorized access, even if your password is compromised.

​

Steps to Enable 2FA:

Access Security Settings: Navigate to your Profile and select the Security option.

Initiate Setup: Click on "Set Up Now" to begin the activation process.

Install Google Authenticator : Download the Google Authenticator app on your mobile device.

Scan the QR Code: Use the authenticator app to scan the QR code displayed on your screen.

Enter the OTP: Retrieve the generated OTP from the app and enter it to complete the setup.

​

How 2FA Works During Login:

Once enabled, every login attempt will require an OTP from Google Authenticator. Simply open the app, retrieve the latest code, and enter it to access your account. 

      

Without the correct OTP, login will be denied.

      
​

​

The Importance of 2FA:

By activating 2FA, you are significantly enhancing your account’s security, making it nearly impossible for unauthorized users to gain access — even if they have your password. This is a crucial step in protecting your personal data and preventing security breaches.

🔒 Secure your account today by enabling 2FA.

title: Online Correction - Add Challan to Statement
tags: online correction - add challan to statement
summary: This feature allows the deductor to add a challan to an already filed statement. The Add Challan functionality is not available through offline correction. If the statement is not processed by TRACES, please file C1 (Personal Information) through the ...
content:
This feature allows the deductor to add a challan to an already filed statement. The Add Challan functionality is not available through offline correction. If the statement is not processed by TRACES, please file C1 (Personal Information) through the Conso file. Once processed, you can proceed with online correction on TRACES.

Traces e-Tutorials Links - Click Here !

Steps for Online Correction:

Step 1: Login to the TRACES Website

Log in to the ExpressTDS portal using your username and password. The Deductor Master page will open, displaying a list of all deductors.

From the Deductor Master, click on the desired deductor to access their details page.

In the selected deductor's page, review the relevant information.

Locate the TRACES icon in the deductor's page.

      
Click on the TRACES icon to be redirected to the TRACES portal login page.

      

Step 2: Navigate to Request for Correction under the Defaults menu.

      

      
Step 3: Enter the relevant details: Financial Year, Quarter, Form Type, and select Correction Category as "Online". Click on the Submit Request button.

      

      
The request will be available under Track Correction Request.

Once the request status changes to Available, click on Available/In Progress to proceed with the correction.

      

Step 4: Validate KYC
Note: The Digital Signature-supported KYC validation screen will appear only if the Digital Signature is registered.

      

      

Normal KYC

      

      

      

      

Step 5: After successful KYC validation, select the Type of Correction Category from the dropdown as Add Challan to Statement.

      

      

Click on Add Challan (Minor Head Code-200/400)

      

      

      

      

      

      

User can remove added challan through ‘Remove Challan’ option.

      

Note: Only newly added challans can be removed from the statement. Challans that were already present in the statement cannot be removed.

      

      

Add Challan (Minor Head Code-100)

      

      

      

      

      

Step 6: Make the required corrections in the selected file and click on Submit for Processing to submit the correction. (Only available to the Admin User)

      

      

      

      

      

Step 7: A 15-digit token number will be generated and sent to the registered email ID.

      

      

      

      

By following this process, you can successfully add a challan to the statement via online correction.

title: ExpressITR || Data Sync Process
summary: The Data Synchronization feature in ExpressITR enables users to fetch and update essential information from the Income Tax Portal, including ITR Returns, Acknowledgments, Form 26AS, AIS Data, Challans, PAN Status, Notices & Orders, and Return Filing ...
content:
The Data Synchronization feature in ExpressITR enables users to fetch and update essential information from the Income Tax Portal, including ITR Returns, Acknowledgments, Form 26AS, AIS Data, Challans, PAN Status, Notices & Orders, and Return Filing Status. This process ensures data accuracy and keeps records current within the software.

Steps to Perform Data Synchronization

Step 1: Select the Client

Navigate to the Client Master section in Express ITR.

Select the relevant client for whom data synchronization is required.

Step 2: Initiate Synchronization

Locate the Sync button at the top-right corner of the screen.

      
Click Sync to begin the synchronization process.

Important Guidelines

Before initiating the sync process, ensure the following:

Stable Internet Connection — Required to avoid interruptions during data synchronization.

Active Login Credentials — Verify that Income Tax e-Filing / AIS (Insight) / PAN Verification credentials are valid and up to date.

Latest Express Connector Installed — Confirm that the most recent version of the Express Connector is installed and running.

Avoid Manual ITD Portal Login — Do not log in to the Income Tax Portal manually during the sync process, as it may interrupt or terminate the synchronization.

📌 Download / Update Express Connector
 Refer to the Express Connector – Installation Guide for instructions on installation and setup.

By following the above steps and prerequisites, users can ensure that all client tax records remain synchronized, accurate, and up to date with the Income Tax Portal.

title: ExpressITR || Client Master Migration from ClearTax to ExpressITR
tags: client master migration from cleartax to expressitr
summary: Client Master Migration from ClearTax to ExpressITR This article explains the complete process to migrate Client Master data from ClearTax software to the ExpressITR application. The migration utility helps users quickly transfer existing client ...
content:
Client Master Migration from ClearTax to ExpressITR
This article explains the complete process to migrate Client Master data from ClearTax software to the ExpressITR application.

The migration utility helps users quickly transfer existing client records from ClearTax into ExpressITR, eliminating the need for manual data entry and ensuring a smooth transition to the new system.

Initiating Client Master Migration

Step 1: Open the Client Master Screen

Launch the ExpressITR application.

Navigate to the Client Master section and click on the Add (+) icon to create or import client records.

This will open the Client Master creation screen where different client addition options are available.

Step 2: Select the ClearTax Migration Option

Click on Migrate from ClearTax.

Enter your ClearTax Login Credentials (Username and Password).

Click the Proceed button.

The system will begin fetching the client details available in your ClearTax account.

Note: During the migration process, only the Client Master details (PAN Number) will be imported from the ClearTax application. The password must be entered manually by the user, as password information is not available from the ClearTax application.

Verifying Migrated Client Data

Once the migration process is completed:

Return to the Client Master screen in ExpressITR.

The migrated client records will now appear in the client list.

Users are required to enter the password manually, after which they can review and manage the migrated client records within the ExpressITR application.
This confirms that the client data has been successfully transferred from ClearTax.

2FA Authentication Error
If an error related to Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) occurs during the migration process, follow the steps below.

      

Step 1: Log in to the ClearTax Portal

Visit the ClearTax ITR website (https://taxcloudindia.com/entity#/) and log in using your credentials.

After successfully logging in, open the following link in the same browser session to access the account security settings: https://accounts.cleartax.in/user-settings/
Step 2: Access Security Settings

Navigate to the Security tab.

Locate the Two-factor authentication (2FA) Status option.

Step 3: Disable Two-Factor Authentication

Turn OFF the 2FA toggle.

Enter your ClearTax password when prompted for confirmation.

Click Save to apply the changes.
After disabling 2FA, retry the migration process in the ExpressITR application.

This should allow the system to successfully connect to ClearTax and complete the client data migration.

content:
This article explains the step-by-step procedure to import a JSON file into ExpressITR, applicable for both filed and unfiled returns.

ExpressITR allows users to seamlessly import JSON files generated from the Income Tax Department portal. The imported data automatically populates the relevant sections of the return, improving accuracy and reducing manual entry. This guide covers:
Importing JSON when the return is already filed
Importing JSON when the return is not yet filed

Importing JSON for a Filed Return

Step 1: Access the Import JSON Feature

Open the relevant Client Profile in ExpressITR.

Select the appropriate Assessment Year.
Navigate to the ITR Summary section.
Under Import JSON, click the Import button.

Click on the JSON Import button to proceed.

Step 2: Confirm the Import
Click Continue to confirm and initiate the JSON import process.

      
      

Importing JSON for an Unfiled Return (Unfilled JSON)

Step 1: Access the Import JSON Feature

Open the relevant Client Profile in ExpressITR.

Select the appropriate Assessment Year.
Navigate to the ITR Summary section.

Click the Import button under Import JSON.

Step 2: Select the JSON File

Click on the Import JSON button.
Browse and select the JSON file you wish to import.

The system will successfully import and populate the JSON data into the client’s ITR workspace.

By following the steps outlined above, you can easily import JSON data into ExpressITR, ensuring smooth and error-free preparation of both filed and unfiled returns.

title: ExpressITR || Significant Accounting Policies
summary: This article explains the step-by-step process to access, view, and generate the Significant Accounting Policies in ExpressITR. Step 1: Access the ITR Summary In the application, open the relevant Client Profile. Navigate to the ITR Summary section. ...
content:
This article explains the step-by-step process to access, view, and generate the Significant Accounting Policies in ExpressITR.

Step 1: Access the ITR Summary

In the application, open the relevant Client Profile.

Navigate to the ITR Summary section.

Click on Return Flow to proceed.

      

Step 2: Open Income Details

Within the ITR Summary, select the Business Profession Income head.

This section displays income details specific to the client’s business or profession.

      

Step 3: Open Financial Statements

In the Business Profession Income section, click on New Financial Statements.

This will display the list of available financial statement options.

      

Step 4: Access Significant Accounting Policies

From the list of available options, click on Accounting Policies to open the Significant Accounting Policies section.

      

Step 5: Select the Policy Format

Choose the required format for generating the Accounting Policy. Two formats are available:

Ind AS (Indian Accounting Standards)

AS (Accounting Standards)

The data is auto-generated based on the comparison between the current and previous financial years.

Once generated, the content can be reviewed and edited as needed.

Two response types — Response 1 and Response 2 — are provided. Select the appropriate option based on your requirement.

Step 6: Generate the Policy Using AI

After selecting the desired response type, click on AI Generate.

The system will process and display a preview of the AI-generated Significant Accounting Policy.
Review the generated policy and make any necessary edits before finalising.
Conclusion

By following the above steps, users can easily access and generate Significant Accounting Policies in ExpressITR.

This AI-assisted feature simplifies policy preparation and ensures accuracy, consistency, and compliance within a few clicks.

title: ExpressITR || Virtual Digital Assets Details (Schedule VDA)
summary: Entering Virtual Digital Asset (VDA) Details in ExpressITR This article provides a detailed, step-by-step guide for entering Virtual Digital Asset (VDA) information in Schedule VDA within ExpressITR. Accurate entry of these details ensures proper tax ...
content:
Entering Virtual Digital Asset (VDA) Details in ExpressITR

This article provides a detailed, step-by-step guide for entering Virtual Digital Asset (VDA) information in Schedule VDA within ExpressITR. Accurate entry of these details ensures proper tax reporting and compliance with Income Tax requirements.

Step 1: Open the Return Flow

Access the relevant Client Profile in ExpressITR.

Navigate to the ITR Summary section.

Click on Return Flow to initiate the ITR filing process.

      

Step 2: Access Schedule VDA

Go to the Capital Gains section.

Select Schedule VDA to begin entering details related to Virtual Digital Assets.

      

Step 3: Enter VDA Details

In Schedule VDA, provide the following information:

Date of Acquisition: Enter the date on which the Virtual Digital Asset was originally purchased.
Date of Transfer: Specify the date on which the asset was sold, transferred, or otherwise disposed of.
Income Head: Choose the applicable income head under which the VDA transaction should be taxed.
Cost of Acquisition: Enter the original purchase cost of the VDA.
Consideration Received: Provide the total amount received from the sale or transfer of the VDA.

      

By accurately completing the fields in Schedule VDA, users can ensure correct reporting of Virtual Digital Asset transactions in ExpressITR. This process supports compliance, enhances reporting accuracy, and simplifies the overall ITR filing workflow.

title: ExpressITR || Form 10IEA Filing Process
tags: filed details will be automatically fetched
summary: Applicability of Form 10IEA Form 10IEA is applicable to individuals, HUFs, AOPs (not being cooperative societies), BOIs, or Artificial Juridical Persons with income from business or profession. It allows taxpayers to opt out of the new tax regime and ...
content:
Applicability of Form 10IEA

Form 10IEA is applicable to individuals, HUFs, AOPs (not being cooperative societies), BOIs, or Artificial Juridical Persons with income from business or profession. It allows taxpayers to opt out of the new tax regime and choose the old tax regime for their income tax filing.

Applicable for :

ITR-3, ITR-4: Individuals and HUFs with business or professional income.

ITR-5: Cooperative societies only.

Steps to File Form 10IEA

Step 1: Access the Client Profile

Open the client’s profile in the Express ITR application and navigate to the ITR Summary section.
Click on the button under Return Flow to open the relevant ITR options.

      

Step 2: Prepare the ITR

Enter all income-related information in the relevant sections of the return.

Once the income details are completed, navigate to the E-Filing section.

      

Click on Review and E-Filing to confirm the entered details.

      

Step 3: File Form 10IEA

Click on Proceed for E-Filing to begin the filing process.

      
The software will automatically:

File Form 10IEA first to declare opting out of the new tax regime.

Proceed with filing the ITR.

      

Important Notes →

Ensure all business/professional income details are accurately entered before initiating the e-filing process.

Filing Form 10IEA is mandatory for taxpayers who opt for the old tax regime in the applicable scenarios.

Step 4: Sync Filed Data

Once Form 10IEA is successfully filed on the Income Tax Portal:

Return to the application dashboard.

Click the Sync button.

      
The filed details will be automatically fetched into the software.

Step 5: Verify Filing Status

The submission status of Form 10IEA will be visible under the E-Filing Details section within the ExpressITR application.

      

By following the above streamlined process, users can efficiently file Form 10IEA and complete the associated ITR filing through the ExpressITR platform, ensuring compliance and accuracy in tax regime selection.

To update your personal, contact, and organization information, as well as your login password in ExpressITR, follow these steps:

Log in to ExpressITR and click on the Profile Icon located in the top right corner of the screen.

      
Select Profile Settings from the dropdown menu to access your account settings.

In Profile Settings, go to Personal & Contact Information.

After entering the new information, click Save to apply the changes.

To update organization details, navigate to the Organization Information section.

Enter the new password as prompted and save it to complete the update.

title: ExpressITR || Advance Tax Calculator and Payment Guide
tags: advance tax calculator and payment guide
summary: This guide outlines the steps to calculate and pay Advance Tax using the Express ITR software. Step 1: Log In Log in to your Express ITR account at "https://www.expressitr.com". Step 2: Select Client Open the client profile for whom you wish to ...
content:
This guide outlines the steps to calculate and pay Advance Tax using the Express ITR software.

Step 1: Log In

Log in to your Express ITR account at "https://www.expressitr.com".
Step 2: Select Client

Open the client profile for whom you wish to calculate Advance Tax.
Step 3: Access the Calculator

Click on the Advance Tax Calculator button located at the top-right corner of the screen.

      
Step 4: Auto-Fill Income (Optional)

To calculate Advance Tax based on the previous year’s income, click on Auto-fill from P.Y. Income.

      

Step 5: Enter Estimated Income

You may also manually enter estimated income in the Estimated Income column.
To add details for specific income types such as Dividends, Lottery Winnings, Crossword Puzzle Winnings, Capital Gains, or Deductions, click the ‘+’ icon on the left side and input the relevant information.

      

Step 6: Calculate Advance Tax

Once all income details have been entered, click on Advance Tax: Calculate & Pay.

      
The system will display a quarterly income breakdown along with the details of Advance Tax payable and any amounts already paid.

Step 7: Include Interest Under Section 234C (If Applicable)

If the Advance Tax installment is overdue and interest under Section 234C is applicable, enable the With 234C Interest option. The software will automatically compute the interest liability.

      

Step 8: Make Payment

Click on the Pay Now button.

      
Select your preferred payment method, and click Pay Now again to proceed. You will be redirected to the official Income Tax Portal to complete the payment.

      
      

We trust you found this guide helpful.

content:
Process to Feed 80IAC Deduction Details for Startup Companies

Eligibility Criteria for 80IAC Deduction

Incorporation Date: The startup must be incorporated or registered between April 1, 2016 and March 31, 2025.

Age Limit: The deduction can be claimed within the first 10 years from the date of incorporation.

Entity Type: The startup should be incorporated as a Private Limited Company, a Partnership Firm or a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP).

Turnover Limit: The turnover should be less than INR 100 Crores in any of the previous financial years.

Recognition: The entity must be recognized as a startup by the DPIIT (Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade).

Steps to Feed 80IAC Deduction in Express ITR

Step 1: Access the ITR Summary

Log in to Express ITR using your credentials. and open the profile of the startup company.
Navigate to the ITR Summary section and click on Return Flow to begin.

Step 2: Provide Business Information

Open the Business Profession head and navigate to Business Information.

      
Go to the Organization Info section and fill in the following:
Select "Yes" for the question, "Whether you are recognized as a startup by DPIIT?"

                  
Enter the DPIIT Registration Number.

Select "Yes" for the question, "Whether Declaration in Form-2 in accordance with para 5 of DPIIT notification dated 19/02/2019 has been filed before filing of the return?"

Enter the date of filing the declaration.

                  

Step 3: Enter 80IAC Deduction Details

Open the Deductions Head in the software.

      
Locate the 80IAC Section for eligible startups.

      
Enter the relevant deduction details based on the financial records and eligibility.

Note: Ensure all data is accurate and supported by appropriate documentation, as per the guidelines laid out by DPIIT and the Income Tax Department.

This process ensures proper claim of deductions under 80IAC, promoting compliance and transparency for eligible startups.

summary: Process to Report Futures and Options (F&O) Income in Tax Returns This guide explains how to classify and report Futures and Options (F&O) income under different income heads: Ordinary Business Income Capital Gain Income Speculation Income The ...
content:
Process to Report Futures and Options (F&O) Income in Tax Returns

This guide explains how to classify and report Futures and Options (F&O) income under different income heads:

Ordinary Business Income

Capital Gain Income

Speculation Income

The classification of income is based on the user's preference, and it is the user's responsibility to determine the appropriate income category.
Each classification requires specific steps in the tax return filing process, outlined below.

1. Reporting F&O Income as Ordinary Business Income

If F&O income is treated as Ordinary Business Income, follow these steps:

Step 1: Access Return Flow

Log in to the client’s profile in ExpressITR.

Navigate to the ITR Summary section and select Return Flow to begin.

      

Step 2: Navigate to the Business and Profession Head

Go to the Business and Profession Head section.

      
Step 3: Add Business Details

Click on Add Business and fill in the following information:

Type of Business

Business Name

Nature of Business

Trade Name

Description of the Business

      

Step 4: Enter Financial Statements

Go to Financial Statements > Trading Account.

Provide the details:

For Sales Amount, select Other Operating Revenues (specify nature and amount) under Revenue from Operations.

For Purchase Amount, use Purchases (net of refunds and duties/taxes, if any) under the Trading Account section.

      

Step 5: Record Business Expenses (if applicable)

Navigate to the Profit and Loss Account.

Record expenses under Other Expenses (specify nature and amount) (Point 46) to reduce taxable profit.

      

2. Reporting F&O Income as Capital Gain

If F&O income is classified as Capital Gain Income, follow these steps:

Step 1: Navigate to the Capital Gain Income Head

Access the Capital Gain Income Head section.

      

Step 2: Feed Capital Gain Details

Provide the relevant details of the gain under the applicable category.

      

3. Reporting F&O Income as Speculation Income

If F&O income is classified as Speculative Income, follow these steps: [Click here for the detailed process].

Step 1: Navigate to the Business and Profession Head

Open the Business Profession Head section.

Step 2: Enter Speculative Income Details

Open the Consolidated Schedule BP.

Scroll to locate Income from Speculative Activity.

Click the Calculator Button and input the following:

Turnover details

Gross profit

Expenditure details

Step 3: Save the Information

Click Done or press Esc to save the entered information.

Accurate reporting of Futures and Options income ensures compliance with tax laws and reduces the likelihood of errors or penalties.

title: ExpressITR || Process to Change Tax Slab in AOP/BOI - ITR-5
tags: process to change tax slab in aop-boi
summary: Process to Change Tax Slab in AOP/BOI ITR-5 This guide provides a step-by-step process to change the tax slab for an Association of Persons (AOP) or Body of Individuals (BOI) while filing ITR-5. The applicable tax slab depends on the type of AOP/BOI ...
content:
Process to Change Tax Slab in AOP/BOI ITR-5

This guide provides a step-by-step process to change the tax slab for an Association of Persons (AOP) or Body of Individuals (BOI) while filing ITR-5. The applicable tax slab depends on the type of AOP/BOI and the nature of income. Follow the steps below to ensure correct tax slab selection and calculation.

Understand the Tax Slab Categories for AOP/BOI

Category 1: Normal AOP/BOI

Taxed at individual rates based on the income range.

Applicable tax slabs:

      

Additional Charges:

Surcharge:

10% of income tax if income > ₹50 lakhs but ≤ ₹1 crore.

15% of income tax if income > ₹1 crore but ≤ ₹2 crores.

25% of income tax if income > ₹2 crores but ≤ ₹5 crores.

37% of income tax if income > ₹5 crores.

Health and Education Cess: 4% on income tax and surcharge.

Category 2: AOP/BOI with Determinate Shares

Taxed at the Maximum Marginal Rate (MMR) of 30% if the income exceeds the basic exemption limit.

Surcharge and cess apply as mentioned above.

Category 3: AOP/BOI with No Member Exceeding the Basic Exemption Limit

Taxed at individual rates as if each member were an individual.

Category 4: AOP/BOI with Foreign Company Members

Taxed at 40% (plus surcharge and cess).

Steps to Change Tax Slab in ITR-5

Step 1: Access the ITR Profile

Open the client profile in your ExpressITR software.

Navigate to the ITR Summary section.

Click on Return Flow to proceed.

      

Step 2: Open the Business and Profession Head

Locate the Business Profession Head section in the ITR form.

      

Step 3: Enter Member Details

Go to the Business Information tab.

      
Select Member Details and fill in the relevant fields based on the category:
For Determinate Shares:

Enter the percentage of shares held by each member.

Set "Income exceeds max amount not charged to tax" to "No".

                        

                        
For Indeterminate Shares:

Leave the "Percentage of Share Held" field blank.

                        

                        
                        
Step 4: Verify Tax Computation

Open the tax computation section.

Ensure that the tax is calculated as per the applicable rates:

Individual slab rates for normal AOP/BOI.

30% for determinate shares.

40% for foreign company members.

Important Considerations

Losses → Losses of an AOP/BOI cannot be carried forward or set off against member's income.
Alternate Minimum Tax (AMT) → AOP/BOI is subject to AMT at 18.5% of adjusted total income, if applicable.
Cross-Check Filing Details →

Ensure all details entered in the ITR form match the financial data and supporting documents.

Validate the return before submission to avoid errors.

We hope this guide helps you accurately change the tax slab for AOP/BOI in ITR-5.

title: ExpressITR || ITR-U Filing Process
tags: process to file itr-u using kdk express itr
summary: Process to File ITR-U Using KDK Express ITR This guide provides a step-by-step process for filing an Updated Income Tax Return (ITR-U) using the KDK Express ITR software. The ITR-U form is filed under Section 139(8A) of the Income Tax Act to update ...
content:
Process to File ITR-U Using KDK Express ITR

This guide provides a step-by-step process for filing an Updated Income Tax Return (ITR-U) using the KDK Express ITR software. The ITR-U form is filed under Section 139(8A) of the Income Tax Act to update income previously reported in an original, belated, or revised return.

Step-by-Step Filing Process

Step 1: Log in to KDK Express ITR

Navigate to https://app.kdksoftware.com/login and sign in using your registered user credentials.

Step 2: Select the Client

From the client list, choose the taxpayer for whom the ITR-U is to be filed.

Step 3: Initiate ITR-U Filing

Go to the ITR Summary section of the selected client.
Click on the ITR-U option under the Start Filing menu.

      
      

Step 4: Choose the Reason for Updating the Return

Select the appropriate reason for filing the updated return from the predefined options as per the Income Tax Act. Reasons may include omission or wrong reporting of income, reduction of carried forward loss, or incorrect rate of tax, etc.

      

Step 5: Auto-Fetch Previous Return Data

The system will automatically populate the income details from the most recently filed return (original/revised/belated) into the ITR-U form.

      

Step 6: Modify Income Details

Click the Edit button to update income figures as necessary under each head (Salary, House Property, Capital Gains, etc.).

      

Step 7: Review Updated Income

Verify the Updated Total Income and ensure it reflects the intended corrections or additions.

      

Step 8: Confirm Amended Computation

Go to the ITR-U > Amended Income section to review the recalculated income and tax computation after changes are applied.

      

Step 9: Enter Tax Paid Details (Section 140B)

Under the Tax Paid (u/s 140B) section, enter details of any additional tax paid.
Click Edit to add or modify Challan details such as BSR Code, Date of Payment, Serial Number, and Amount.

      

Step 10: Finalize and Submit

Navigate to e-Filing > Review and e-Filing.

      
Perform a final review of all data entered.
Click Proceed for e-Filing to submit the ITR-U electronically through the portal.

      

Post-Filing Tips

Retain a copy of the acknowledgement for your records.
You may recheck the status of the filed ITR-U under the client dashboard or the e-Filing history.

summary: The Return Register in Express ITR is a centralized module that provides a detailed overview of all Income Tax Returns (ITRs) filed for clients. It displays essential information such as Filing Status, Filing Date, ITR Form Type, and also offers ...
content:
The Return Register in Express ITR is a centralized module that provides a detailed overview of all Income Tax Returns (ITRs) filed for clients. It displays essential information such as Filing Status, Filing Date, ITR Form Type, and also offers options to download the Form PDF and ITR Receipt PDF.

Steps to Access and Use the Return Register

Step 1: Log in to Express ITR

After logging into the ExpressITR module, the Client Master page will open, displaying all active clients associated with your account.
Step 2: Open the Return Register
Navigate to the left-hand menu and click on Return Register to access all return-related information for your clients.

      
Step 3: Select the Assessment Year
Choose the appropriate Assessment Year (AY) from the dropdown menu.
 The system will then display the filing status of all clients for the selected year in a consolidated view.
      

Step 4: View Filing Status and Download PDFs
Within the Return Register, you can perform the following actions:

View Filing Status: Check the latest filing status for all clients at a glance.

Download Form PDF: Download the ITR Form in PDF format.

      
Download ITR Receipt PDF: Download the ITR-V/acknowledgement receipt.

      

Note: Click the Return Status button to sync and fetch the most recent status from the Income Tax Department, ensuring accurate and updated information.

      

Step 5: Verify & Discard Options for Unverified Returns

To manage returns where the filing status is Filed – Unverified, follow the steps below:

Quick Access from Client Master

The system displays the total number of Unverified Returns directly on the Client Master screen.
Click ACT NOW to proceed with verification or discard actions instantly.

      
      

Standard Procedure via Return Register

Filter Unverified Returns

Open the Return Register and apply the filter: Filing Status = Filed – Unverified.

      
      

To Verify - Unverified Returns

Click the E-Verify button.

A confirmation dialog will appear. Review the message carefully and click Continue.

You will be redirected to Income Tax Department (ITD) Portal.

Select the preferred verification mode (Aadhaar OTP, DSC, or other available methods) and complete the verification.

      
      

To Discard - Unverified Returns
Click the Discard button.

Confirm the action by clicking Continue.

You will be redirected to the Income Tax Department (ITD) Portal.
A confirmation prompt will appear. Read the instructions carefully and click Accept and Continue to complete the discard process.

Using these options, users can efficiently e-verify or discard - Unverified returns with minimal effort.

The Return Register in Express ITR offers a streamlined and efficient way to monitor, verify, and manage all client return filings from a single interface. By following the above steps, users can quickly track filing statuses, download essential documents, and handle actions such as discarding e-verified returns with ease. This ensures better workflow management and improved accuracy in return-related processes.

summary: The Taxpayer Information Summary (TIS) provides a consolidated view of the taxpayer’s financial data as available on the Income Tax Department portal. In Express ITR, users can seamlessly sync, download, and review the TIS data for accurate return ...
content:
The Taxpayer
Information Summary (TIS) provides a consolidated view of the taxpayer’s
financial data as available on the Income Tax Department portal. In Express
ITR, users can seamlessly sync, download, and review the TIS data for accurate
return preparation.

Follow the
step-by-step process below:

Step 1: Perform
AIS/TIS Data Synchronization

Before downloading the
TIS, it is essential to fetch the latest data from the Income Tax Department.

Navigate to the ITR Dashboard within
     Express ITR.
Locate and click on the Sync  option.

Initiate the synchronization process to
     retrieve updated AIS (Annual Information Statement) and TIS data.
Note: Ensure that
valid login credentials for the Income Tax portal are configured to
successfully complete the data sync.

Step 2: Download TIS
Summary in PDF Format

Once the
synchronization is successfully completed:

Go to the relevant section where AIS/TIS
     details are displayed.

Select the option to Download TIS PDF Icon.

The system will generate and download the TIS
     Summary in PDF format for your reference.

Step 3: Review TIS
Data in Computation

In addition to the
standalone TIS PDF:

Generate or open the Computation within Express ITR.

The TIS Summary details will be
     incorporated into the computation report.

Carefully review the data to ensure
     accuracy and consistency with the imported information.

Important Points to
Consider
Always perform a fresh AIS/TIS sync before
     downloading the TIS PDF to ensure updated data.
Review discrepancies, if any, between TIS
     and actual records before finalizing the return.
Use the TIS summary as a reference
     document for accurate income reporting and compliance.

title: ExpressITR || Excel Import and Export Functionality
tags: directors and shareholder details excel import
summary: The Excel Import and Export functionality in Express ITR is introduced to streamline data entry and management for schedules and sections involving large or complex datasets. This feature allows users to download standardized Excel templates, perform ...
content:
The Excel Import and Export functionality in Express ITR is introduced to streamline data entry and management for schedules and sections involving large or complex datasets. This feature allows users to download standardized Excel templates, perform bulk data entry or updates offline, and upload the completed files back into the system. Users can also export existing data for verification, correction, and re-import.

This enhancement is available across multiple schedules and assessee statuses, ensuring faster processing, improved accuracy, and greater operational efficiency.

Objective of the Feature

The primary objective of the Excel Import/Export feature is to enable users to efficiently manage bulky schedule data through Excel files, thereby reducing manual effort and simplifying compliance for schedules that require extensive disclosures.

Key Features

Download Excel Templates - Schedule-wise standardized templates can be downloaded directly from Express ITR.
Bulk Data Import - Upload large volumes of data in a single action, eliminating repetitive manual entry.
Data Export for Review - Existing schedule data can be exported into Excel for offline review and validation.
Re-import Updated Data - After making corrections or enhancements, the updated Excel file can be re-imported to refresh the return data.

Customer Benefits

Time Efficiency - Enables import of hundreds of records within seconds instead of manual, row-wise entry.
Reduced Data Entry Errors - Working in a familiar Excel environment helps minimize typographical and validation errors.
Simplified Corrections - Allows users to export data, rectify issues offline, and re-import without re-entering the entire schedule.
Enhanced Productivity - Particularly beneficial for tax professionals handling multiple clients or complex returns with voluminous disclosures.

Schedules Supported for Excel Import/Export

A. Applicable to All Assessee Statuses

Trial Balance

Schedule VDA (Virtual Digital Assets)
Schedule QD

Schedule FA

Depreciation Chart ( As per provisions of the Income-tax Act )

B. Individual

Schedule AL

Directors and Shareholder Details

C. Partnership Firm / LLP

Details of Other Partnership Firms

Information where the assessee is a partner

Includes partner remuneration details

D. Company Status Schedules

(Applicable to Private and Public Companies – Domestic and Foreign)

Key Persons

Ownership Information

Beneficial Owners Details

Applicable to Unlisted Companies

Individuals holding 10% or more voting power

Immediate Parent Company Details (Foreign Companies)

Ultimate Parent Company Details (Foreign Companies)

Business Reorganization

Holding / Subsidiary Company Details

Schedule AL1

Schedule AL2

Schedule SH

E. Co-operative Society / AOP

Members Details

Usage Guidelines

Always download and use the latest Excel template available in Express ITR.

Do not alter the template structure, sheet names, or column headers.

Ensure all values follow the prescribed data formats (dates, numeric fields, percentages, PAN, etc.).

Validate the data in Excel before uploading to avoid import or validation errors.

Conclusion

The Excel Import and Export functionality in Express ITR offers a robust and efficient solution for managing schedules with extensive data requirements. By enabling bulk uploads, offline review, and easy corrections, this feature ensures higher accuracy, better compliance, and significant time savings for users.

title: ExpressITR || House Rent Allowance (HRA) Calculation
tags: process to feed house rent allowance
actual hra received from the employer
summary: What is House Rent Allowance (HRA)? House Rent Allowance (HRA) is a component of your salary provided by your employer to help you cover the cost of rented accommodation. You can claim HRA exemption only if you are residing in a rented house. HRA ...
content:
What is House Rent Allowance (HRA)?
House Rent Allowance (HRA) is a component of your salary provided by your employer to help you cover the cost of rented accommodation. 
You can claim HRA exemption only if you are residing in a rented house.

HRA Exemption Calculation Process
The HRA exemption is calculated as the least of the following three amounts:
Actual HRA received from the employer.

Actual Rent Paid (-) 10% of Basic Salary + DA (Dearness Allowance).

50% of Basic Salary for those living in metro cities or 40% of Basic Salary for those living in non-metro cities.

Metro cities for the purpose of HRA calculation include: Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Chennai.

Example Calculation

Actual HRA received from the employer: Rs. 120,000

Actual Rent Paid: Rs. 144,000

Basic Salary: Rs. 720,000

DA (Dearness Allowance): Rs. 162,000

Calculation:

Actual HRA received from the employer: Rs. 120,000

Actual Rent Paid (-) 10% of Basic Salary + DA:
144,000−(10%×(720,000+162,000))=144,000−88,200=55,800
50% of Basic Salary (for metro cities): Rs. 360,000
HRA Exemption:
The least of the above three amounts is Rs. 55,800.

Steps in Express ITR:

Enter Salary Details: Input HRA received, DA, and other salary components in the relevant section.

      
Input Rent Paid Details: Under the "Overall Exemptions & Deductions" section, navigate to the House Rent Allowance subsection and input the rent paid.

Metro City Option: Select the metro city option if applicable.

      

The result will show an HRA exemption of Rs. 55,800.

      

Hope this is helpful!

title: ExpressITR || Form 16 Import Process
summary: The Form 16 Import functionality in ExpressITR allows users to directly import salary and TDS details from the Form 16 file into the Income Tax Return (ITR) preparation module. This feature helps reduce manual data entry, improves accuracy, and ...
content:
The Form 16 Import functionality in ExpressITR allows users to directly import salary and TDS details from the Form 16 file into the Income Tax Return (ITR) preparation module. This feature helps reduce manual data entry, improves accuracy, and simplifies the return preparation process.
This article explains the complete process for importing Form 16 data into the software.

Steps to Import Form 16 in ExpressITR

Step 1: Select the Client

Open the application and navigate to the Client Master section.

Select the relevant client for whom the Form 16 data is to be imported.

After selecting the client, the system will redirect to the client dashboard.

Step 2: Open the Return Flow

Click on the Return Flow option available for the selected client.

Select the applicable Assessment Year and return workflow.

This section contains all heads and schedules required for return preparation.

Step 3: Navigate to the Salary Head

Under the Return Flow, open the Salary head.

Click on the Import Form 16 option.

      
The Form 16 Import window will open on the screen.

Step 4: Select the Form 16 File

Click on the Browse or Select File option.

Navigate to the folder or drive where the Form 16 file is stored.

Select the required Form 16 file for import.

Ensure that the selected file is the correct Form 16 document related to the selected client and assessment year.

Step 5: Import the Form 16 Data

After selecting the file, verify the file path and details.

Click on the Import button to start the import process.

The software will validate and process the selected Form 16 file.

Step 6: Verify Imported Data

Upon successful import, the salary and TDS details from Form 16 will be automatically populated in the respective sections of the return.

Review the imported data carefully before proceeding further with return preparation and filing.

Important Points

Ensure that the correct client and assessment year are selected before importing the file.

The Form 16 file should be valid, accessible, and free from corruption.

Verify all imported values before final submission of the return.

In case of import failure or validation issues, recheck the selected file and try importing again.

title: ExpressITR || 80CCD(2) Employer's Contribution to NPS Deduction Calculation
summary: 80CCD(2) Employer's Contribution to NPS Deduction Under Section 80CCD(2), a deduction is available for the employer's contribution to the National Pension System (NPS). Employers can contribute to NPS in addition to contributions made to the Public ...
content:
80CCD(2) Employer's Contribution to NPS Deduction

Under Section 80CCD(2), a deduction is available for the employer's contribution to the National Pension System (NPS). Employers can contribute to NPS in addition to contributions made to the Public Provident Fund (PPF) and Employees' Provident Fund (EPF). The employer's contribution can be equal to or higher than the employee's contribution.

This section specifically addresses the employer's contribution to an employee's NPS account, which employees can claim as a deduction under Section 80CCD(2).

Sections of IT Act, 1961 :  Section 80CCD (2).

Tax Deduction Details : Contributions made by the employer to the NPS/ Atal Pension Yojana accounts of their employee.

Tax Benefits : Up to 10% of salary (basic+ DA) for private-sector employees OR Up to 14% of salary (basic+ DA) for government employees.

Can I claim 80CCD 2 in the new tax regime ? : While the new tax regime offers the NPS benefit only under Section 80CCD (2), the old tax regime allows three deductions under Sections 80CCD (1) (₹1.5 lakh), 80CCD (1B) (₹50,000) and 80CCD (2). However, the higher limit of 14% is only for those who opt for the new tax regime.

Feeding Process in Express ITR

Step 1: Open the Client Profile → Navigate to ITR Summary → Select Return Flow → Open the Salary Head.

      

Step 2: Select Employer Type: Central Government, State Government, PSU, Pensioner, Others

      
Enter Basic Salary and Dearness Allowance (DA) in their respective columns.
Step 3: Go to the Deductions section and enter the deduction amount received from the employer.

      

Calculation Formula for NPS Deduction under Section 80CCD(2):

For Government Employees:

14% of Salary (Basic + Dearness Allowance)

Actual Amount Received from the Employer

Deduction Allowed: The lesser of the above two amounts.

For Non-Government Employees (Private Sector):

10% of Salary (Basic + Dearness Allowance)

Actual Amount Received from the Employer

Deduction Allowed: The lesser of the above two amounts.

We hope this guide assists you in accurately calculating the eligible deductions under Section 80CCD(2) for your NPS contributions.

summary: Process for Change the Tax Rate for a Company Follow these steps to adjust the corporate tax rate for a company in Express ITR based on the applicable tax sections: Tax Rate Overview Normal Tax Rate: The standard corporate tax rate is 30%. Section ...
content:
Process for Change the Tax Rate for a Company

Follow these steps to adjust the corporate tax rate for a company in Express ITR based on the applicable tax sections:

Tax Rate Overview

Normal Tax Rate: The standard corporate tax rate is 30%.

Section 115BA: Under Section 115BA, the basic rate is 25%. However, this rate is different for companies filing taxes under Section 115BAA of the Income Tax Act.
Section 115BAA: Section 115BAA states that domestic companies have the option to pay tax at a rate of 22% plus a surcharge of 10% and cess of 4%.
Section 115BAB: Section 115BAB allows newly registered manufacturing entities, established on or after October 1, 2019, to avail themselves of a reduced corporate tax rate of 15% (excluding surcharge and cess).
Steps to Change the Tax Rate:

Step 1: Access the ITR Summary

Open the client profile in Express ITR.

In the ITR Summary section, click on Return Flow to proceed.

      

Step 2: Go to the E-Filing Option

Navigate to the E-Filing option within the Return Flow.

      

Step 3: Select Applicable Tax Rate

Under Basic Information, select the appropriate tax rate section (115BA, 115BAA, or 115BAB) based on the company’s eligibility.

      

By following these steps, you can easily adjust the applicable tax rate for the company in Express ITR.

Steps for Importing Schedule III Data Using Excel in Express ITR

If you have already entered data into that specific schedule in the software, importing the Excel file will overwrite the existing data.

To prevent the import of a specific schedule through Excel, simply uncheck it before clicking the 'Import' button. This will ensure only selected data is imported into the software.

       
      

Step 5: After completing these steps, all the data you have prepared in the Excel template will be successfully imported into the software.

title: ExpressITR || 80GG Deductions in Respect of Rent Paid
tags: 80gg deductions in respect of rent paid
summary: What is Section 80GG? Section 80GG of the Income Tax Act in India provides deductions for house rent paid by individuals who do not receive House Rent Allowance (HRA) as part of their salary. Eligibility for Section 80GG Deduction Applicability: ...
content:
What is Section 80GG?

Section 80GG of the Income Tax Act in India provides deductions for house rent paid by individuals who do not receive House Rent Allowance (HRA) as part of their salary.

Eligibility for Section 80GG Deduction

Applicability: Section 80GG is applicable to both salaried and self-employed individuals.

Conditions: Business owners and professionals are eligible to claim deductions under this section.

Exceptions: Individuals residing in property owned by themselves or their parents cannot claim this benefit.

Section 80GG Deduction Calculation

The deduction allowed under Section 80GG is the least of the following:

₹5,000 per month (₹60,000 annually).

25% of the total income (before deductions under this section).

Actual rent paid minus 10% of the total income (before deductions under this section).

Example

Total Income Before Deduction (Gross Total Income): ₹8,96,200

Rent Paid: ₹1,20,000

Calculation:

₹5,000 per month = ₹60,000 annually.

25% of total income: ₹8,96,200 × 25% = ₹2,24,050.

Actual rent less 10% of income: ₹1,20,000 - (10% of ₹8,96,200) = ₹1,20,000 - ₹89,620 = ₹30,380.

Deduction: The lowest amount among the three is ₹30,380.

Steps to Claim Section 80GG in ITR
Go to the Deductions section in the ITR form.

Click the down arrow next to 80GG and enter your rent details.

      
Generate the computation and verify your eligibility for deductions.

      

Clarifications

If I am claiming HRA, can I also claim Section 80GG?

No, if you claim HRA, you cannot claim deductions under Section 80GG. Only one benefit can be availed: HRA or Section 80GG.

Can salaried individuals claim this deduction?

Yes, but only if they do not receive HRA as part of their salary.

What is "total income" for Section 80GG?

It is the total income before allowing any deductions under this section.

Can I claim Section 80GG deduction under the new tax regime?

No, deductions under Section 80GG are only available under the old tax regime.

For more details, refer to the Income Tax Act: Income Tax Act, 1961.

summary: Here's a structured guide for preparing Regular Return or Belated Return using Express ITR: Prepare Regular Return Step 1: Access the Return Flow Open the client profile in Express ITR. Sync Data: Click the Sync button to import data from 26AS or AIS ...
content:
Here's a structured guide for preparing Regular Return or Belated Return using Express ITR:

Prepare Regular Return

Step 1: Access the Return Flow

Open the client profile in Express ITR.

Sync Data:

Click the Sync button to import data from 26AS or AIS (TDS, TCS, Tax Paid, or Income details).

Data will be automatically populated into the software.

      
Use the Auto-Fill button under each Head of Income to update the relevant fields.

Step 2: Enter Other Income Information

Manually input any additional income details that are not reported in the AIS.

      

Step 3: Verify Tax Computation

After entering all income data, review the Tax Computation to verify the accuracy of the fed data and the calculated taxes.

      

Step 4: Compare Tax Regimes

Review the Old vs. New Tax Regime comparison.

      
Select the tax regime that benefits the client the most.

      

      

Step 5: E-Filing Preparation

Verify all income and tax computation details.

If accurate, proceed by clicking the E-Filing button.

      

Step 6: Error Rectification

Click on the Review and E-Filing button.

      
Address errors flagged by the Schema Validation system:

Click on the error link to be redirected to the incomplete or incorrect data fields.

Update the information as required.

Step 7: Final Steps

Once all errors are cleared:

Download the JSON file using the Download JSON button.

Alternatively, click Proceed for E-Filing to file the return on the portal.

      

Prepare Belated Return

Follow the same process as above for filing a belated return.

Note: The software automatically identifies the return as Belated if filed after the due date.

Note: Always double-check all entries and computations to ensure the return is accurate and compliant with current tax laws.

Follow these steps to carry forward the unclaimed TDS amount for the next financial year in Express ITR:

After importing the TDS data, locate the Claimed in Own Hands section within the TDS details.

Remove the unclaimed TDS amount from this section by editing the entry.

Once the amount is removed:

The software will automatically move the unclaimed amount to the TDS Credit Being Carried Forward column.

      

Step 5: Verify the Carried Forward Amount

Check the TDS Credit Being Carried Forward column to ensure the amount is correctly reflected.

Save the changes to ensure the adjustments are recorded.
Key Notes

Automated Feature: The system automatically calculates the carried forward amount once it's removed from the Claimed in Own Hands section.

Data Accuracy: Cross-check data with 26AS/AIS to ensure correct TDS details are reflected.

Next Year's Return: The carried forward amount will appear in the next year’s return under TDS Brought Forward.

By following this process, you can efficiently carry forward unclaimed TDS amounts to the next financial year using Express ITR.

tags: key changes in itr for assessment year 2025–26

mandatory disclosures under chapter vi-a deductions
key changes in itrs for ay 2025–26
key changes in itrs for assessment year 2025–26
key changes in itr for ay 2025–26
summary: 1. Enhanced Disclosure in HRA Schedule The HRA (House Rent Allowance) schedule now requires comprehensive details to be furnished. Taxpayers claiming HRA exemptions must provide detailed rent-related information. 2. Detailed Reporting for House ...
content:
1. Enhanced Disclosure in HRA Schedule

The HRA (House Rent Allowance) schedule now requires comprehensive details to be furnished. Taxpayers claiming HRA exemptions must provide detailed rent-related information.

      

2. Detailed Reporting for House Property Income (with Loan Information)

Taxpayers claiming interest deductions on housing loans must now disclose the following details:

Type of Lender

Name of Bank/Financial Institution

Loan Account Number

Loan Sanction Date

Original Loan Amount

Outstanding Loan Amount as on 31st March (Financial Year End)

      

3. LTCG Reporting Now Permitted in ITR-1 and ITR-4
Reporting of Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG) under Section 112A, up to ₹1.25 lakh, is now enabled in ITR-1 and ITR-4.

This applies to gains from listed equity shares and equity-oriented mutual funds.

Previously, such reporting was restricted to ITR-2 or ITR-3.

4. Mandatory Disclosures under Chapter VI-A Deductions

Taxpayers availing deductions under Chapter VI-A must now provide additional details, as outlined below:

Sections 80C / 80D: Policy Number or Document Identification Number (DIN)

      
      
Sections 80CCD(1), 80CCC, 80CCD(1B): Permanent Retirement Account Number (PRAN)

      
Section 80EEA: Stamp Duty Value of the Residential Property

      
Section 80DDB: Nature of the Specified Disease

      

5. Specification of TDS Sections Now Mandatory

For incomes (other than salary) where tax has been deducted at source (TDS), taxpayers must now specify the relevant TDS section (e.g., 194J, 194I, etc.) in the return.

      
      
This change ensures accurate mapping of TDS credits.

Earlier, specifying the section was optional and not enforced.

title: ExpressITR || Income Tax Return Updates (ITR-2,3 &5) for AY 2025–26
tags: enhanced threshold for disclosure of assets and liabilities
summary: Income Tax Return (ITR) Updates for AY 2025–26 (Applicable to ITR-2, ITR-3, and ITR-5 Forms) 1. Separate Reporting of Capital Gains Based on Transaction Date Effective Assessment Year 2025–26, capital gains must now be reported separately based on ...
content:
Income Tax Return (ITR) Updates for AY 2025–26 (Applicable to ITR-2, ITR-3, and ITR-5 Forms)
1. Separate Reporting of Capital Gains Based on Transaction Date

Effective Assessment Year 2025–26, capital gains must now be reported separately based on the date of transaction in relation to 23rd July 2024:

Transactions executed before 23rd July 2024: To be reported under the category "Before 23rd July 2024".

      
      
Transactions executed on or after 23rd July 2024: To be reported under the category "On or After 23rd July 2024".

      
       

2. Treatment of Capital Loss on Share Buybacks

As per the amendment effective 1st October 2024, capital loss arising from share buybacks can be claimed only if the associated dividend income is disclosed under "Income from Other Sources", in accordance with Section 2(22)(f) of the Income Tax Act.

Key Points:

Separate reporting has been enabled for such capital losses and the corresponding dividend income under the capital gains section.

       
The sale proceeds from the buyback are automatically captured under Other Sources > Dividend Income (excluding income under Section 2(22)(f)).

      
The purchase cost of the buyback shares will be treated as a capital loss.

      
Both the sale amount and purchase cost are considered independently for accurate tax treatment.

3. Revised Capital Gains Tax Computation

A. Sale of Immovable Property (Land/Building) on or after 23rd July 2024

In the case of Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG) arising from the sale of immovable property on or after 23rd July 2024, tax is computed to determine the final tax liability:

      

Computation Process:

Calculate Tax Without Indexation: Applicable tax rate: 12.5%
Calculate Tax With Indexation: Applicable tax rate: 20%
Determine the Excess Amount: The excess is the difference between the two tax computations (without indexation minus with indexation). This excess amount will be exempted from the 12.5% tax liability.
Illustrative Example:

Without Indexation (12.5%): ₹2,00,000

With Indexation (20%): ₹1,18,429
Excess Amount: ₹2,00,000 – ₹1,18,429 = ₹81,571
Final Tax Payable: ₹2,00,000 – ₹81,571 = ₹1,18,429

Important Note:
If the indexed computation results in a capital loss, such loss will be treated as Nil and cannot be carried forward to subsequent financial years.

A detailed annexure is provided as part of the computation sheet to transparently reflect the revised tax calculation and its impact.

B. Listed Securities / Zero-Coupon Bonds (Where STT is Not Paid) – Transaction Before 23rd July 2024

For transactions involving listed securities or zero-coupon bonds, where Securities Transaction Tax (STT) has not been paid and the sale occurred before 23rd July 2024, the tax treatment has been amended in line with Section 112(1) of the Income Tax Act.

      
These capital gains must now be reported under the category: ‘Listed Security – STT Not Paid’.

In the computation section, hover your mouse cursor to view the excess amount calculation.

      
      

Computation Method:
Compute 20% tax on the indexed capital gain
Compute 10% tax on the non-indexed capital gain
The excess amount, calculated as the difference between the two tax values, is deducted from the indexed tax amount to determine the final tax payable.

Illustrative Example:

Indexed Capital Gain: ₹28,066 → Tax @ 20% = ₹5,613
Non-Indexed Capital Gain: ₹30,000 → Tax @ 10% = ₹3,000
Excess Amount = ₹5,613 – ₹3,000 = ₹2,613
Final Tax Payable = ₹5,613 – ₹2,613 = ₹3,000

This method ensures correct tax reporting by effectively aligning indexed and non-indexed tax treatments, and providing relief through adjustment of the excess differential.

4. Enhanced Threshold for Disclosure of Assets and Liabilities

The threshold for mandatory disclosure of assets and liabilities under Schedule AL has been revised:

Previous Threshold: ₹50 lakh

Revised Threshold: ₹1 crore

      

This revision applies to individuals and entities filing ITR-2, ITR-3, and ITR-5.

These changes introduced in the ITR forms for AY 2025–26 are aimed at improving transparency, aligning tax treatment with legislative amendments, and simplifying complex capital gains calculations.

summary: Purpose This article explains how Previous Year (PY) figures are automatically displayed within the Current Assessment Year (AY) ITR form in the software. This functionality helps users compare year-on-year data and ensure accurate return ...
content:
Purpose 

This article explains how Previous Year (PY) figures are automatically displayed within the Current Assessment Year (AY) ITR form in the software. This functionality helps users compare year-on-year data and ensure accurate return preparation.

Feature Overview

The software automatically fetches and displays Previous Year figures when the user opens the Return Flow for the selected Assessment Year. These figures appear across relevant income heads, deductions, and tax liability sections for reference.

This automatic display enables quick comparison and verification without requiring manual entry.

Steps to View Previous Year Figures

Step 1: Open the Client Profile

Select and open the required client profile from the dashboard.

Step 2: Navigate to the Current Assessment Year ITR Form

Open the ITR form for the relevant current Assessment Year.

Step 3: Access Income or Deduction Heads

Navigate to any income head such as:

Salary

House Property

Business/Profession

Capital Gains

Other Sources

      
The system will automatically display the Previous Year figures related to that head.

Where Previous Year Figures Are Displayed

Previous Year data is automatically shown across the following sections:

All Income Heads

Deductions (e.g., Chapter VI-A)

Tax Liability tab

Financial Statements (if applicable)

This ensures consistency, transparency, and ease of review during return preparation.

Benefits of This Feature

Eliminates manual data entry of previous year values

Provides instant year-on-year data comparison

Helps identify unusual variances and ensure accuracy

Improves workflow efficiency during ITR preparation
