From the Dashboard, open the required Client Profile.

Navigate to the ITR Summary section.

Click Return Flow to proceed to the detailed return components.

Step 2: Open the Income Details Section

In the ITR Summary, select the Business Profession Income head.

This section displays income and financial details related to the client’s business or professional activities.

Step 3: Open the ITR Balance Sheet

Click on ITR Balance Sheet within the Business Profession Income section to open the Balance Sheet view.

Step 4: Initiate Data Import

Inside the Balance Sheet screen, click on Import from Financial Statement.

This option allows you to directly pull data from the Financial Statement module.

Step 5: Confirm the Import

A confirmation dialog will appear. Click Yes to continue.

The system will automatically import all relevant financial data into the ITR Balance Sheet.

Import from Previous Year (Previous Year Data in New Financial Statement)

The Previous Year figures in the New Financial Statement are automatically retrieved from the last filed ITR.
These values are shown only for reference and comparative analysis.
They do not impact the current assessment year’s calculations, unless manually updated by the user.

By following the above steps, you can efficiently import financial data into the ITR Balance Sheet, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and faster ITR preparation within ExpressITR.

title: ExpressITR || 80PA Deduction
summary: Process to Feed 80PA Deduction Details for Producer Companies Applicability of Section 80PA Eligible Business: Section 80PA allows a 100% deduction of profits and gains attributable to the eligible business of a producer company. Turnover Limit: The ...
content:
Process to Feed 80PA Deduction Details for Producer Companies

Applicability of Section 80PA

Eligible Business: Section 80PA allows a 100% deduction of profits and gains attributable to the eligible business of a producer company.

Turnover Limit: The turnover of the producer company must be below ₹100 crore in the previous financial year.

Steps to Feed 80PA Deduction in Express ITR

Step 1: Access the ITR Summary

Log in to Express ITR using your credentials and open the profile of the producer company.
Navigate to the ITR Summary section and click on Return Flow to start the process.

Step 2: Provide Business Information

Open the Business Profession head and navigate to the Business Information section.

      

Step 3: Update Organization Info

Go to the Organization Info section.

Select "Yes" for the question, "Are you a Producer Company as defined in Sec 581A of Companies Act?"

      
Step 4: Open the Deductions Head

Navigate to the Deductions Head in the software.

      

Step 5: Enter 80PA Deduction Details

Locate the section 80PA: Deduction in respect of certain income of producer companies.

Feed the relevant deduction details based on the profits and gains attributable to the eligible business of the producer company.

       

Note: Ensure the deduction details are accurate and supported by the financial statements and turnover records to comply with the eligibility criteria.

This streamlined process allows you to properly claim deductions under Section 80PA, ensuring tax benefits for eligible producer companies.

title: Password Incorrect / Reset Password / Forgot Password
summary: If you see the error message "Password is incorrect, try again" while logging into applications like ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS or any similar application, it typically means that the password entered is incorrect. Solution → Double-check the ...
content:
If you see the error message "Password is incorrect, try again" while logging into applications like ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS or any similar application, it typically means that the password entered is incorrect.

      

Solution →
Double-check the password you entered. If it was entered incorrectly, simply re-enter the correct password and try logging in again.

If you continue to see the error message "Password is incorrect, try again," you will need to reset your password.

To Reset Your Password, Please follow these steps:

Step 1: Click on the "Forgot Password?" option.

      

Step 2: Enter your Registered mobile number. You will receive an OTP on this number for verification.

      

Step 3: Enter the OTP.

      
      
Step 4: Create a new password, confirm it and click on Update Password.

      

You can now log in with your updated password.

title: Sign-Up and Sign-In Process – SPECTRUM CLOUD
tags: sign-up and sign-in process – spectrum cloud
summary: This article provides a step-by-step guide to help you create an account and log in to the SPECTRUM CLOUD platform by KDK Software. SPECTRUM CLOUD enables tax professionals to manage GST, Income Tax (ITR), and TDS compliance for all their clients ...
content:
This article provides a step-by-step guide to help you create an account and log in to the SPECTRUM CLOUD platform by KDK Software.

SPECTRUM CLOUD enables tax professionals to manage GST, Income Tax (ITR), and TDS compliance for all their clients from a single, secure cloud-based platform.

Note: If you are signing up as a business entity (e.g., SME, Corporate, Enterprise), please refer to the Business Users section below for the appropriate platforms.

🎯 Intended Users : The SPECTRUM CLOUD portal is designed for:

Chartered Accountants (CAs)
Accountants
Tax Practitioners
GST Practitioners (GSTPs)

📝 Step 1: Account Registration (Sign-Up)

To access the platform, you must first register an account.

Navigate to the Sign-Up page: https://app.kdksoftware.com/signup

Complete the registration form with the following:
Full Name
Email Address
Mobile Number
Other required details

Click "SIGN UP" to submit the form.

You will receive a One-Time Password (OTP) on your registered mobile number.

Enter the OTP in the designated field to verify your account.

🔐 Step 2: User Login (Sign-In)

Once your account is verified, follow the steps below to log in.

Go to the Login page: https://app.kdksoftware.com/login

Enter your registered mobile number and password.
Click "Login" to access your dashboard.

🏢 For Business Users (SMEs, Corporates, Enterprises)

If you are representing a Small or Medium Enterprise (SME), Corporate, or Enterprise, please use the appropriate platform designed specifically for business entities. These platforms provide tailored solutions to meet business compliance and filing requirements:

🔗 Recommended Platforms for Business Users:

ExpressGST – For GST filing and compliance 👉 https://www.expressgst.com/

ExpressITR – For Income Tax Return (ITR) filing 👉 https://www.expressitr.com/

ExpressTDS – For TDS return filing and related compliance 👉 https://www.expresstds.com/

Note: These platforms are optimized for organizations and business entities rather than individual professionals such as CAs or tax consultants.

⚠️ Important Guidelines

Password Confidentiality: Use a secure password and do not share your login credentials.
Consistent Credentials Across Products: If you use multiple KDK Software products with the same mobile number, please ensure you use the same password across platforms to maintain account consistency.

📞 Need Assistance?

If you experience any issues during registration or login, please contact our customer support team.

title: Experience Unified Power with SPECTRUM CLOUD Enterprise
tags: experience unified power with spectrum cloud enterprise
summary: To enhance operational efficiency, simplify user management, and deliver a more integrated cloud experience, KDK Software has successfully consolidated its individual cloud applications into a single unified platform — Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise. With ...
content:
To enhance operational efficiency, simplify user management, and deliver a more integrated cloud experience, KDK Software has successfully consolidated its individual cloud applications into a single unified platform — Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise.

With this transformation, applications such as ExpressGST, ExpressTDS, and ExpressITR are now centrally accessible through the Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise ecosystem, enabling organizations to manage all compliance activities from one unified platform.

This upgrade is designed to provide improved accessibility, centralized subscription management, enhanced security, and a seamless user experience across all KDK cloud services.

      

Key Platform Enhancements & Internal Updates

1. Unified Subscription Management

Previously, subscriptions for ExpressGST, ExpressTDS, and ExpressITR were managed independently. As part of the new architecture enhancement, all existing standalone subscriptions have now been automatically consolidated into a single Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise subscription.

Benefits:

Centralized subscription management

Simplified license administration

Unified renewal and access control

Improved operational efficiency for corporate users

This consolidation eliminates the need to manage multiple product subscriptions separately and ensures a streamlined experience across all integrated modules.

2. Centralized Dashboard with Feature Continuity

All existing configurations, operational limits, and user-level permissions from the individual applications have been securely migrated and preserved within the new unified dashboard.

The migration process ensures complete continuity of existing services without impacting current workflows.

Preserved Components Include:

Existing usage limits

Return filing quotas

User access rights

Role-based permissions

Client data and configurations

Operational workflows and settings

Users can continue their activities seamlessly within the new platform environment without requiring any manual reconfiguration.

3. Unified Authentication & Access Management

To simplify user access and improve security management, a centralized authentication framework has been implemented across the Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise platform.

A single Sign-In and Sign-Up interface is now available for all integrated cloud applications, enabling users to access the entire ecosystem through one unified authentication process.

Key Advantages:

Single-point login access

Simplified user onboarding

Improved credential management

Enhanced security and authentication control

Consistent user experience across applications

This unified approach minimizes login complexity and improves overall accessibility for corporate users and administrators.

4. Advanced Customer Profiling & Personalization

As part of the enhanced onboarding process, the platform now captures structured customer profiling information during user registration and sign-up activities.

This information enables the system to deliver a more personalized and optimized platform experience based on the customer’s business profile, operational requirements, and service usage patterns.

Objectives of Customer Profiling:

Personalized dashboard experience

Improved service recommendations

Better customer engagement

Optimized support and communication

Enhanced product customization capabilities

The profiling framework helps ensure that users receive more relevant features, workflows, and support experiences aligned with their business needs.

Conclusion

The transition to Spectrum-Cloud Enterprise marks a significant advancement in KDK Software’s cloud ecosystem strategy. By unifying multiple applications into a centralized enterprise platform, organizations can now benefit from:

Simplified subscription and access management

Centralized operational control

Seamless user experience

Improved scalability and security

Enhanced personalization and service delivery

This transformation ensures a more efficient, secure, and future-ready compliance management ecosystem for all corporate users.

title: ExpressITR || How to Delete Client or Active/Inactive Client
summary: 1. Client Deletion Process The Client Deletion feature in Express ITR allows users to permanently remove a client from the system. Once deleted, the client will no longer appear in the dashboard, client list, or any other section within the ...
content:
1. Client Deletion Process

The Client Deletion feature in Express ITR allows users to permanently remove a client from the system. Once deleted, the client will no longer appear in the dashboard, client list, or any other section within the application.

Steps to Delete a Client

Step 1: Access the Client Profile

Log in to Express ITR and go to the Client List.

Click Edit Details button under the Actions column for the desired client.

      

Step 2: Delete the Client

Inside the client’s profile, locate the Delete Client option.

Click Proceed and confirm the prompt.

A confirmation message will appear: “Data Deleted Successfully.”
      

Note: After deletion, the client is permanently removed from the software and cannot be restored.

2. How to Inactive a Client

Inactive/Deactivating a client temporarily removes them from the active client list on the dashboard. Only active clients will be displayed after deactivation.
Steps to Deactivate a Client

Step 1: Access the Client Profile

Log in to Express ITR and go to the Client List.

Click Edit Details button under the Actions column for the desired client.
Step 2: Change Client Status

In the client’s profile, locate the Active / Inactive toggle.

Switch the status from Active to Inactive.

Click Save to apply the change.

      

Note: Inactive clients will no longer appear in your active client list.

3. How to Re-active a Client

Reactivating a client restores their visibility in the active client list. This is useful when you need to work again with a previously inactive client.
Steps to Reactivate a Client

Step 1: Locate the Inactive Client

From the Client List, apply the "All" filter.

Search for the client using their Name, PAN, Mobile Number, or Email ID.

Once found, click Edit Details button under the Actions column.

      

Step 2: Update Client Status

In the client’s profile, switch the status from Inactive to Active.

Click Save to finalize the update.

      

Note: Once reactivated, the client will reappear in your active client list.

title: ExpressITR || Allowable / Disallowable Remuneration Calculation till AY 2024-2025
summary: Allowable / Disallowable Remuneration Calculation The maximum permissible remuneration under Section 40(b) is allowed for any working partner, provided: It is authorized by the partnership deed. It complies with the terms of the deed and pertains to ...
content:
Allowable / Disallowable Remuneration Calculation

The maximum permissible remuneration under Section 40(b) is allowed for any working partner, provided:
It is authorized by the partnership deed.

It complies with the terms of the deed and pertains to a period after the date of the deed.

The remuneration exceeding the specified limits below will not be deductible:

Permissible Limit Based on Book Profit

1. On the first ₹3,00,000 of book profit or in case of loss: ₹1,50,000 or 90% of book profit, whichever is higher.
2. On the balance book profit: 60% of the balance.

 | Book Profit

 |  Permissible  Limit

 | On the first Rs. 300000 of Book Profit or in case of Loss

 | Rs. 1,50,000 or 90% of Book Profits whichever is higher

 | On the Balance Book Profit

 | 60% of Book Profits

Example

Book Profit for Allowable Remuneration: ₹3,99,314 
(After the notional set-off of unabsorbed depreciation of ₹0)

Step-by-Step Remuneration Calculation

1. 90% of the first ₹3,00,000: ₹3,00,000 × 90% = ₹2,70,000
2. 60% of the remaining amount: ₹(3,99,314 - 3,00,000) = ₹99,314
      ₹99,314 × 60% = ₹59,588
Maximum Allowable Remuneration: ₹2,70,000 + ₹59,588 = ₹3,29,588

Disallowed Remuneration Calculation

Total Remuneration Paid: ₹3,99,314
Allowable Remuneration: ₹3,29,588
Disallowed Remuneration (under Section 40(b)): ₹3,99,314 - ₹3,29,588 = ₹69,726

We hope this explanation helps clarify the calculations and limits under Section 40(b)!

title: ExpressITR || Steps to Import Capital Gain Data Using the Broker Template
tags: import capital gain data using the broker excel template
import capital gain data using the broker template
summary: Importing Capital Gain Data Using Broker Template The Broker Template feature in Express ITR enables the structured and accurate import of Capital Gain data. Transactions imported through this functionality are automatically classified under the ...
content:
Importing Capital Gain Data Using Broker Template

The Broker Template feature in Express ITR enables the structured and accurate import of Capital Gain data. Transactions imported through this functionality are automatically classified under the appropriate income heads, including Capital Gains and Business or Profession Income, wherever applicable.

This feature ensures standardized data formatting, minimizes manual intervention, reduces the risk of errors, and enhances overall efficiency in return preparation.

Step 1: Access Client Profile

Log in to the Express ITR application.

Select the required client from the Client List.

Navigate to the ITR Summary section.

Click on Return Flow to proceed.

Step 2: Navigate to Capital Gain Section

Click on the Capital Gain tab.

The Capital Gain data entry screen will be displayed.

Step 3: Initiate Import Process

Click on the Import CG Statement option.

The import interface will open for data upload.

Step 4: Import Capital Gain Data

Users can import data using any of the following methods:

Method A: Import via Broker Credentials

Enter the required details based on the respective broker:

Zerodha / Groww – User ID and Password

Upstox – Registered Mobile Number

Angel One – Registered Mobile Number or Client ID

Kotak Securities – Upload broker file using drag-and-drop

Upon successful authentication, the system will automatically fetch and import the Capital Gain data.

Method B: CAMS / KFINTECH Mailback Statement

CAMS and KFINTECH provide a facility to generate a consolidated account statement (Mailback Statement) for mutual fund investments.

Steps to Import:

Request the consolidated statement using the registered email ID.

Download the password-protected Excel file received via email.

Unprotect the file using the password defined at the time of request.

Important:  The password required is the Excel sheet unprotect password set during the request process. It is not the broker login password.

Upload the unprotected file using the Browse File option in the application.

Method C: Upload Broker Template File (Recommended)

Select and upload the predefined Broker Template Excel file.

Key Advantages:

Ensures uniform data structure

Enables accurate classification of transactions

Facilitates automatic population of Capital Gain records

The system will process and populate the data automatically upon successful upload.

Step 5: Validate and Complete Import

Click on the Import button after selecting the file.

Review the confirmation message and click OK.

Output
Upon successful import, the data will be reflected under:

Capital Gains

Business or Profession Income (where applicable)

This confirms that all broker transactions have been successfully imported and appropriately classified.

Conclusion

The Broker Template feature provides a reliable and efficient method for importing Capital Gain data into Express ITR. It improves data accuracy, reduces manual effort, and streamlines the overall return preparation process.

title: Express ITR || How to Enter Presumptive Income 44AD
summary: Section 44AD: Simplified Process for Declaring Income Step 1: Open the relevant client profile or click the "Prepare" button. Step 2: Navigate to the 'Business and Profession' section, then click 'Add Business'. Step 3: In the 'Presumptive' section, ...
content:
Section 44AD: Simplified Process for Declaring Income

Step 1: Open the relevant client profile or click the "Prepare" button.

      
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Business and Profession' section, then click 'Add Business'.

      
Step 3: In the 'Presumptive' section, create the business entry. Select the appropriate business type (44AD) and provide the necessary details related to the business or its nature. Once completed, click 'Done' or press the 'Esc' key to save.

      

Step 4: Open the Consolidated Schedule BP, and look for the option labeled "Profits and Gains of Business or Profession in Presumptive Income". Click on the calculator icon next to Section 44AD.

      
      

Step 5: Enter the business receipts, declare the profit percentage, and provide the required financial particulars of the business. Once done, click 'Done' or press the 'Esc' key to save your entries.

      

By following these steps, you will successfully enter the presumptive income under Section 44AD.

Process to Update PAN, Name, DOB as Per PAN Database

If you encounter errors while filing returns due to mismatched PAN, Name, or DOB with the PAN database, follow these steps to resolve the issue:

      

      

      

Step 1: Update Details on the Income Tax Portal

Log In: Log in to the Income Tax Portal using your credentials.

Access My Profile: Navigate to the My Profile section.

      
Update Personal Details: 

Under the Personal Details section, locate the option labeled Click Here.

                  

                  
Click on this option to review and refresh the profile details.
Refresh and Confirm:

Click the Refresh button under the Profile.

A notification will appear stating: "Details have been successfully updated."

                  
Click Close and log out of the portal.

Step 2: Sync Details in the Software

Open Client Profile: Access the relevant client profile in the Express ITR software.

      
Sync Data: Click on the Sync Now button to synchronize the updated details with the software.

Step 3: Proceed with E-Filing

Once the synchronization process is complete, proceed to validate and file the return.

      
The return should now be successfully validated without errors.

Note: Ensure the internet connection is stable during the process for seamless updates.

This process should resolve the mismatch error effectively. If further issues persist, consider contacting technical support for assistance.

content:
Process to Feed Details of Payments of Advance Tax and Self-Assessment Tax in Express ITR

Here’s a step-by-step guide for entering or verifying the payment details of Advance Tax and Self-Assessment Tax in Express ITR:

Step 1: Log in and Access ITR Summary

Log in to Express ITR using your credentials.

Open the client profile for whom the tax return is being prepared.

Navigate to the ITR Summary section and click on Return Flow to begin.

      
Step 2: Open Tax Details Section

Under the Income Head, locate and select the Tax Details option.

      
This section provides an overview of tax payments and deductions.

Step 3: Access the Annexure

Scroll to the annexure titled Details of Payments of Advance Tax and Self-Assessment Tax.

      
This section allows you to feed or verify payment details.

Step 4: Enter Challan Details

If the Challan details are not imported automatically via the sync process:

Click on Add New Entry.

Enter the following details manually:

BSR Code: The bank branch code of the challan.

Date of Payment: The date the tax was paid.

Challan Serial Number: Unique identifier for the payment.

Amount Paid: The amount of Advance Tax or Self-Assessment Tax.

Click Save to record the entry.

Step 5: Automated Import (Optional)

If the Sync Process has been enabled, Challan details will be fetched automatically from 26AS or AIS.

Verify the auto-imported details for accuracy and edit if discrepancies are noted.

26AS/AIS Data Import Process → Click Here !

Tips for Feeding Tax Details

Cross-Check with Official Records: Always verify Challan details with Form 26AS or AIS to ensure accuracy.

Avoid Duplication: Check for existing entries to prevent duplicate records.

Save Regularly: Save your progress to avoid losing entered data.

By following this process, you can efficiently feed and manage the details of Advance Tax and Self-Assessment Tax in Express ITR, ensuring accurate and compliant tax returns.

summary: Procedure for Preparing Tax Audit Forms 3CA-3CD and 3CB-3CD Step 1: Open the client and select the Prepare button under the Audit Summary section. Step 2: Navigate to the Form 3CA-3CD/Form 3CB-3CD option. The appropriate form will be displayed based ...
content:
Procedure for Preparing Tax Audit Forms 3CA-3CD and 3CB-3CD

Step 1: Open the client and select the Prepare button under the Audit Summary section.

      

Step 2: Navigate to the Form 3CA-3CD/Form 3CB-3CD option. The appropriate form will be displayed based on the client's status.

      

Step 3: You will be directed to Form 3CA or Form 3CB, depending on the applicable scenario. Enter the auditor’s details, qualifications, comments and other relevant information.

      

Step 4: After completing the required fields, proceed to Form 3CD.

      

In Form 3CD, the clauses are divided into four parts as follows:

Clauses 4, 8–17

Clauses 18–27

Clauses 28–32

Clauses 33–44

You can also fill in the details using an Excel sheet. For the step-by-step process, refer to this guide: Import 3CD Data Using Excel

Step 5: Select the clause number for which details need to be provided. Use the options YES/NO/NA on the right side of the respective clause and input the required data.

      

If you need to download Form 3CD as a PDF with a different font size, follow this guide: Steps to Download Form 3CD as a PDF

Step 6: Upon completion of data entry, click on E-Filing to validate the return.

      

If validation errors are encountered, click the Error Message link. This will redirect you to the specific section requiring correction. Address the incomplete or incorrect information and revalidate the return.

      

Step 7: After successfully resolving all errors, download the JSON file and proceed to file the return.

      

If the return has already been filed using another application and you wish to import the data into our software, follow this guide: 3CD JSON File Import Process

Hope this is helpful!

content:
How to Enter Information Related to Partners in the Firm/LLP (Remuneration u/s 40(b)):

Step 1: Log in to the software and click on the "Prepare" button on the main dashboard.

      

Step 2: In the menu, navigate to and click on the "Business and Profession" head to proceed with entering business details.

      

Step 3: Select "Add Business" and enter the necessary business details such as business name, type, and other relevant information. Once completed, click "Done" or press Esc to save the entered business data.

      

Step 4: Go to Consolidate Schedule BP. Find the section for "Information Related to Partners in the Firm/LLP (Remuneration u/s 40(b))" and click on the calculator button to open the data entry form.

      

Step 5: Enter the following information for each partner : Name of the Partner, PAN, Profit Sharing Ratio, Remuneration, Interest (on capital, if applicable), Interest Rate, Profit (from the firm/LLP), Address, Pin/Zip Code, City, State, Status (whether active or retired partner), Partner disclosure as of 31st March (Yes/No), Profit ratio as of 31st March, Admit/Retirement Status (whether partner joined or retired), Date of Admission/Retirement, Aadhaar No./Enrollment No., Country
Note: You must provide details for a minimum of 2 partners in the firm/LLP.

      

Additional Information:

If there have been any changes during the financial year, such as new partners joining or existing partners retiring, ensure that you update the respective dates and admission/retirement details accordingly.

      

By following these steps, you can successfully enter the necessary details related to partner's remuneration under Section 40(b) of the Income Tax Act.

title: Express ITR || Presumptive Income under Sections 44AD/44ADA/44AE and New Changes as per the Budget 2023
tags: new changes as per the budget 2023
summary: Introduction to Presumptive Income: Presumptive taxation allows taxpayers to calculate and pay taxes based on a presumed income, rather than having to maintain detailed accounts of expenses and revenues. This system simplifies tax filing, as you pay ...
content:
Introduction to Presumptive Income:

Presumptive taxation allows taxpayers to calculate and pay taxes based on a presumed income, rather than having to maintain detailed accounts of expenses and revenues. This system simplifies tax filing, as you pay tax on a percentage of your gross receipts or turnover, without the need to calculate actual income after deducting expenses.

Section 44AD

Section 44AD was introduced to simplify tax compliance for small businesses with a turnover of less than ₹2 crores, which was amended to ₹3 crores in the Budget 2023, provided that at least 95% of the receipts are through digital (non-cash) modes. This section relieves eligible taxpayers from maintaining detailed books of accounts.

Eligible taxpayers can declare their net income as 8% of the turnover for cash transactions. For digital transactions, the net income is presumed to be 6% of the total turnover, making tax filing easier for businesses that mostly operate through non-cash modes.

To qualify for the ₹3 crore turnover limit, cash receipts should not exceed 5% of the total turnover.

Example:

Turnover from Other Modes (Cash): ₹1,50,00,000

Turnover from Banking Channels: ₹1,00,00,000

Total Turnover (Cash + Banking): ₹2,50,00,000

Maximum allowable Cash Turnover (5% of total turnover): ₹2,50,00,000 * 5% = ₹12,50,000

Thus, the total gross turnover should not exceed ₹3 crores for eligibility under Section 44AD.

Section 44ADA

Section 44ADA is a special provision for calculating presumptive income for small professionals. It extends the presumptive taxation scheme, previously available only to small businesses, to specified professionals. This allows certain professionals to pay tax on a deemed percentage of their receipts, without having to maintain detailed accounts.

Eligibility for Section 44ADA:
Professionals, such as doctors, lawyers, architects, engineers, accountants, or other specified professionals, with annual gross receipts less than ₹75 lakh, can opt for this scheme.
Under Section 44ADA, the presumptive income is fixed at 50% of the gross receipts. This eliminates the need to maintain detailed accounting records and simplifies the tax filing process for eligible professionals.

To qualify for the ₹75 lakh turnover limit, cash receipts should not exceed 5% of the total receipts.

Example:

Receipts via Banking Channels: ₹40,00,000

Receipts via Other Modes (Cash): ₹30,00,000

Total Receipts (Banking + Cash): ₹70,00,000

Maximum allowable Cash Receipts (5% of total receipts): ₹70,00,000 * 5% = ₹3,50,000

Thus, the total gross receipts should not exceed ₹75 lakh to be eligible under Section 44ADA.

Section 44AE

Section 44AE is a presumptive taxation scheme for individuals, Hindu Undivided Families (HUFs), and partnership firms (excluding LLPs) engaged in the transportation of goods. This provision simplifies tax calculations by establishing a fixed income per vehicle, based on the type of vehicle used for transport.

Eligibility for Section 44AE:
The scheme applies to transport businesses involved in the leasing, hiring, or owning of goods carriages. Taxpayers under this section declare a fixed profit for each vehicle, which is treated as their taxable income.

Deemed Profit Calculation:

For light vehicles: Presumed profit is ₹7,500 per month per vehicle.

For heavy vehicles: Presumed profit is calculated as ₹1,000 per ton of gross vehicle weight per month.

This simplification helps transport businesses avoid the need for complex bookkeeping, making tax compliance more straightforward.

By utilizing these sections, small taxpayers and professionals can significantly reduce the burden of maintaining extensive books of accounts while simplifying their tax filing processes through presumptive taxation. Each section is designed to cater to specific groups, ensuring that tax compliance is both easy and efficient for eligible individuals and businesses.

content:
How to Enter Information Related to Other Partnership Firms in Which You Are a Partner (Remuneration):

Step 1: Log in to the system, select the client profile, and click on the "Prepare" button to start the process.

      

Step 2: Navigate to the "Business and Profession" head in the menu to access the business details section.

      
Step 3: Fill details here - 

Click on "Add Business" to create a new business profile.

Select the business type as "Ordinary".

For the Nature of Business, choose "00001: Share of income from firm only".

Enter a relevant Name or Description for this business (this can be the name of the partnership firm or a brief identifier).

Once all information is entered, click on "Done" or press Esc to save the data.

      

Step 4: Navigate to Consolidate Schedule BP. Under this section, find the option "Information related to other Partnership Firms in which you are Partner (Remuneration)" and click on the calculator button to open the input form.

      

Step 5: In the form, enter the required details about the partnership firm where you are a partner, including:
Name of the Firm

Is Working Partner? (Yes/No)

Is the Firm liable to Audit? (Yes/No)

Is Section 92E applicable to the Firm? (Yes/No)

Remuneration (as per agreement)

Interest (on capital or loans, if applicable)

PAN (Permanent Account Number of the firm)

Profit Sharing Ratio (as agreed upon in the partnership deed)

Profit/Loss (your share of profit or loss from the firm)

Expenses (expenses associated with the firm, if applicable)

Capital Balance (capital contributed by you in the firm)

      

Once all the details are entered, review the information to ensure accuracy and click on "Done" or press Esc to save the data.

By following this step-by-step guide, you will successfully enter details related to any other partnership firm in which you are a partner, including your remuneration and profit-sharing details.

title: ExpressITR || Corresponding Income offered as shown for TDS claim should not be greater than Income shown under the relevant Income Head
tags: tds claim should not be greater than income
tds claim should not be greater
tds claim should not be greater than income shown
summary: The income reported for TDS claims should not be less than the income declared under the relevant Income Head while filing the return. Reason for Warning: This warning message is generated based on discrepancies between the income reported in the ...
content:
The income reported for TDS claims should not be less than the income declared under the relevant Income Head while filing the return.

      

Reason for Warning:
This warning message is generated based on discrepancies between the income reported in the software and the TDS details. If TDS receipts are recorded in the Tax Details section but the corresponding income is not reported under the relevant Income Head, the warning will appear.

Examples:

If the TDS receipt is Rs. 100,000 but the income declared under the relevant head is Rs. 0, this warning will appear.

Similarly, if the TDS receipt is Rs. 100,000 but the income declared under the relevant head is Rs. 90,000, this warning will still appear.

Possible Solution:

Ensure that the income declared under the relevant Income Head matches or exceeds the TDS receipts recorded in the Tax Details.

However, in specific cases like Business Professions (under sections 44AD, 44ADA, 44AE), or Capital Gains, it may not be feasible to declare the entire receipt amount as income. In such cases:

Verify that the total receipt declared under the relevant head is consistent with your actual receipts.

Ensure the total receipt is equal to or higher than the TDS receipts in the Tax Details.

If these conditions are met, you can safely ignore the warning message and proceed with filing your return.

Hope this clarifies the issue.

title: ExpressITR || Income Credited to P&L A/c Under Business Head: can not be greater than Income shown under PART-A PL
tags: can not be greater than income shown under part-a pl
income credited to profit and loss account under business head
summary: Income Credited to P&L A/c Under Business Head: can not be greater than Income shown under PART-A PL If you face this error during JSON generation, follow these steps to resolve it: Steps to Fix the Issue Step 1: Locate the Error Click on the error ...
content:
Income Credited to P&L A/c Under Business Head: can not be greater than Income shown under PART-A PL

If you face this error during JSON generation, follow these steps to resolve it:

Steps to Fix the Issue

Step 1: Locate the Error

Click on the error message link.

This will redirect you to the Consolidated Sch. BP.

      

Step 2: Open Adjusted P&L Annexure

In the Adjusted P&L Annexure, verify the details of income credited to the P&L under the Business/Profession head.

      

Scenario 1: Incorrect Reduction of Income

Check if you have mistakenly reduced income from Business/Profession in the adjusted P&L.

If yes, remove the incorrect reduction and generate the JSON file again.

Scenario 2: Income Reduction from Business Head

If you intend to reduce income from the Business/Profession head, ensure the following:

Cross-Check Point No. 14 – Other Income: Confirm that the income you reduced is included in Point No. 14 – Other Income of the Profit & Loss Account.

Adjustments: If this income is not reflected in Point No. 14 – Other Income, add it under the appropriate column in the Financial Statement's Profit and Loss Account.

Step 3: Mandatory Adjustments

After adjustments, ensure that:

The income reduced under the Business/Profession head is equal to or less than the income reported under Point No. 14 – Other Income of the P&L.

The income reduced is correctly categorized under the appropriate income head.

Instructions for Feeding P&L (Point No. 14 - Other Income)

Go to Financial Statement: Open the Profit and Loss Account.
Locate Point No. 14: Find Other Income in the P&L section.
Enter Income Details: Add the income amount under the applicable column.
Save Changes: Click Save or Done to confirm.

      
      

Ensure this matches your adjustments in the Adjusted P&L.

Step 4: Generate JSON Again

After completing these adjustments, generate the JSON file again.

The error should now be resolved.

Additional Notes :

The total income shown under a separate income head must be equal to or greater than the income reduced from Business/Profession.

Accurate categorization and reconciliation are mandatory to avoid discrepancies.

Hope this resolves your issue!

title: Spectrum-CLOUD Mobile Application
summary: The Spectrum CLOUD Mobile Application enables users to securely access Spectrum Cloud data directly from their mobile devices. The application provides seamless access to GST, TDS, and ITR-related information, allowing users to monitor critical ...
content:
The Spectrum CLOUD Mobile Application enables users to securely access Spectrum Cloud data directly from their mobile devices. The application provides seamless access to GST, TDS, and ITR-related information, allowing users to monitor critical compliance activities anytime and anywhere through a secure, efficient, and user-friendly interface.

Key Features & Enhancements

1. Mobile – ITR Notices

Users can now access Income Tax Return (ITR) notices directly through the mobile application.

Features

View notice summaries

Access detailed notice information

Financial Year-wise notice mapping for easy reference
2. Mobile – TDS Notices

The application now supports mobile access to TDS-related notices, enabling users to manage and monitor notices more efficiently.

Features

View detailed notice information

Track notice status in real time

Identify the applicable section under which the notice has been issued

Integrated workflow support with existing TDS processes
3. Mobile Utilities

GSTIN Search → A quick and efficient utility for verifying GSTIN details.
Available Information

Legal Name

Registration Status

State Jurisdiction
TDS Rate Finder → Simplifies the process of identifying applicable TDS rates.
Benefits

Section-wise TDS rate identification

Helps minimize manual errors during deduction processing
Find HSN Code → Enables users to search and identify the appropriate HSN code through keyword-based lookup functionality.

IT New Session → Allows users to initiate a new Income Tax session directly from the mobile application with improved performance and seamless session initialization.

The application is designed to provide fast, secure, and convenient access to essential compliance data through a simplified mobile interface.

Accessing the Application

Downloading the Application →  To download the application:

Open the Google Play Store or Apple App Store.

Search for KDK Software.

Download and install the application.
Sign-In Process → Existing Spectrum Cloud Web Application users can log in using their registered mobile number.
Steps to Sign In

Enter the registered mobile number.

An OTP (One-Time Password) will be sent to the registered mobile number.

Enter the OTP to complete authentication and securely access the application.
Client Master → The Client Master section displays all companies configured within the Spectrum Cloud Web Application.
Companies are automatically categorized under the following modules:

GST

TDS

ITR
Accessing Company Information → To access company-related information:

Select the required company from the list.

Choose the appropriate module to view the relevant details.
GST Module → The GST Module provides comprehensive access to GST-related information for the selected company.
Available Sections

Notices

Ledgers

Returns
Notices → The Notices section enables users to view GST notices and orders received for the selected company.
Viewing Notice Details

Click the View (Eye) icon corresponding to the notice.

The application will display detailed information related to the selected notice or order.

Available Notice Information

ARN Number

Application / Case Creation Date

Notice Sub-Type

Status

Order Description

Order Number

Issued Date

Due Date

Demand Amount

Reply Details

This functionality helps users efficiently monitor notices and track compliance activities.

Ledgers → The Ledgers section provides access to various GST ledgers associated with the selected company.
Users can:

View ledger details directly within the application

Download ledger reports in Excel format for detailed analysis and record-keeping

Available Ledgers

Cash Ledger → Displays tax head-wise balances along with the total available balance in the Cash Ledger.
RCM Liability / ITC Statement → Provides details of Reverse Charge Mechanism (RCM) liabilities and Input Tax Credit (ITC) balances.
Negative Liability Statement → Displays details related to negative liability adjustments along with tax head-wise information.
Electronic Credit Reversal → Provides information regarding credit reversals recorded in the electronic credit ledger.
Credit Ledger → Displays Input Tax Credit balances with tax head-wise breakup.
Non-Return Liability Ledger → Shows liabilities arising due to non-filing of returns, including detailed tax head-wise balances.

Ledger Options → Each ledger provides the following options:

View (Eye) Icon – Access detailed ledger transactions

Date Range Filter – Review transactions for a selected period
Returns

The Returns section allows users to monitor the filing status of GST returns for the selected company.

Return Categories

Pending Returns

Overdue Returns

Filed Returns
Financial Year Selection → Users can select the applicable Financial Year (FY) to view return details for the respective period.

Return Information Views

The application provides two convenient viewing modes for return information.

Month View → The Month View enables users to:

Select a specific month

View return details for the selected month

Download or share return information
Return View → The Return View enables users to:

Select a specific return type

View filing status month-wise

Check return filing dates

Available GST Returns → The following GST returns can be viewed through the mobile application:

GSTR-1

GSTR-1A

GSTR-3B

GSTR-9

GSTR-9C

The KDK Software Mobile Application is available for download on both the Google Play Store and the Apple App Store, enabling users to access Spectrum CLOUD services seamlessly across Android and iOS platforms.

title: Express ITR || How to Import 3CD Data Using excel
summary: Excel Import Process for 3CD Data The Excel import process helps you fill out specific clauses that require detailed information and annexures. For other clauses, manual data entry into the software is required. Please follow the steps below to ...
content:
Excel Import Process for 3CD Data

The Excel import process helps you fill out specific clauses that require detailed information and annexures. For other clauses, manual data entry into the software is required.
Please follow the steps below to import 3CD data using Excel.

Step 1: Open the client profile and click on the "Prepare" button under the 'Audit Summary' section.

      

Step 2: Select either '3CA-3CD' or '3CB-3CD', then click on '3CD' and choose any specific clause from Form 3CD.

      

Step 3: Click on the 'Import Excel' option. Then, select 'Click here to download template' to download the 3CD Excel template.

      

      
      
Step 4: Once the template is downloaded, fill in all relevant data in the appropriate sheets provided in the 3CD Excel template. Ensure that you enter the data based on your records.

      

Step 5: After entering the data, browse to the saved Excel file location and click on the 'Import' button.

      

Step 6: If you have already entered some information into the software, note that it will be replaced by the data from the Excel file upon import. If you want to retain any annexures you prepared within the software, you can uncheck them before importing the Excel data.

      

Step 7: Click 'Import', then confirm by clicking 'Yes'.

      

Your data will now be successfully imported into the software.

We hope this process assists you with importing your 3CD data efficiently.

summary: Express Connector – Installation & Troubleshooting Guide This document provides step-by-step instructions to install, verify, and troubleshoot the Express Connector to ensure uninterrupted application functionality. Overview: If you receive an error ...
content:
Express Connector – Installation & Troubleshooting Guide

This document provides step-by-step instructions to install, verify, and troubleshoot the Express Connector to ensure uninterrupted application functionality.

Overview: If you receive an error message related to the Express Connector, it typically indicates one of the following:

The connector is not installed.
The installed version is outdated.
The connector service is not running.
Browser security settings are restricting required permissions.

      
      

Please follow the instructions below to resolve the issue.

Step 1: Download the Express Connector

Log in to the application.
Download the latest available version of the Express Connector.

      
            

Step 2: Install the Express Connector

Open the Downloads folder on your system.
Locate the downloaded installer file.
Double-click the file to initiate the installation process.
Upon successful installation, the following confirmation message will appear: 
“The installation has completed successfully! Express Connector is ready to use.”

Click OK to close the installer.
Step 3: Verify Connector Status via Task Manager

After installation, please confirm that the connector service is running:
Open Task Manager (Ctrl + Shift + Esc).
Go to the Details tab.
Verify that ExpressConnector2.exe is listed and running.

If the process is not visible:
Restart your system.

Reopen Task Manager and verify again.
If the process still does not appear, reinstall the connector.

Step 4: Enable Required Browser Permissions (Google Chrome / Microsoft Edge)

When accessing the application via Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge, certain browser permissions must be enabled.

Recommended Method: Using Site Settings
Open the application in your browser.
Click the “View Site Information” icon located next to the address bar.
Locate and enable the following permissions:

Local Network Access

Apps on Device

Refresh the page or restart the browser to apply the changes.

Important: If these permissions are disabled, the application may not communicate properly with the Express Connector.

Conclusion
To ensure proper functionality of the application:

Install the latest version of the Express Connector.
Confirm that the connector service is actively running.
Enable the required browser permissions.

If the issue persists after completing all steps outlined above, please contact the Support Team and provide relevant screenshots or error messages for further assistance.

summary: If you subscribe to or renew any of our applications, such as ExpressGST, ExpressITR, or ExpressTDS an invoice copy is automatically sent immediately to the registered email ID associated with your account. Additionally, you can conveniently download ...
content:
If you subscribe to or renew any of our applications, such as ExpressGST, ExpressITR, or ExpressTDS an invoice copy is automatically sent immediately to the registered email ID associated with your account.
Additionally, you can conveniently download the latest invoice copy directly from the application, which includes all plan details.

Steps to Download the Invoice:

Step 1: Open the software and log in with your registered credentials.

Step 2: Click on the Profile button and select the Subscription Plan option.

      

Step 3: In the My Subscribed Plan window, you will see details of your current plans, features and the history of purchased plans.

Step 4: Click on the Download button to download the latest invoice.

      
            

With these simple steps, users can easily download their invoice copy directly from the software.

title: Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for Enhanced Security
tags: enable two-factor authentication for enhanced security
enhance your account security with two-factor authentication
summary: To ensure the highest level of security for your account, we strongly recommend enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) across all our applications, including ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS, and ExpressReco. With 2FA enabled, a single One-Time ...
content:
To ensure the highest level of security for your account, we strongly recommend enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) across all our applications, including ExpressGST, ExpressITR, ExpressTDS, and ExpressReco.

With 2FA enabled, a single One-Time Password (OTP) generated through the Google Authenticator app will be used for authentication across all these applications, ensuring seamless yet secure access.
​

Why Enable 2FA?

2FA provides an additional layer of protection by requiring a One-Time Password (OTP) generated via the Google Authenticator app. This extra security measure helps prevent unauthorized access, even if your password is compromised.

​

Steps to Enable 2FA:

Access Security Settings: Navigate to your Profile and select the Security option.

Initiate Setup: Click on "Set Up Now" to begin the activation process.

Install Google Authenticator : Download the Google Authenticator app on your mobile device.

Scan the QR Code: Use the authenticator app to scan the QR code displayed on your screen.

Enter the OTP: Retrieve the generated OTP from the app and enter it to complete the setup.

​

How 2FA Works During Login:

Once enabled, every login attempt will require an OTP from Google Authenticator. Simply open the app, retrieve the latest code, and enter it to access your account. 

      

Without the correct OTP, login will be denied.

      
​

​

The Importance of 2FA:

By activating 2FA, you are significantly enhancing your account’s security, making it nearly impossible for unauthorized users to gain access — even if they have your password. This is a crucial step in protecting your personal data and preventing security breaches.

🔒 Secure your account today by enabling 2FA.
